title: No Fear of the Dark
description:
Against all odds, we made it. 

I can open a way into the storm, but it will be a tenuous connection. We can only get a small team through the portal. 

We have no idea what awaits us on the other side. We must be prepared. 

I can use the Cynosure to bind us to Magisters' Terrace. If we need a quick escape, I can bring us back there. 

Come--help me calibrate this spell.

title: A Matter of Strife and Death
description:
Ha! A distant realm, crawling with vicious evil monsters? Just like old times.

Alleria, Turalyon, and I faced a thousand years of war with demons in the far-flung realms. It is as simple a solution now as it was then. 

Kill or be killed.

title: Save a Piece of Mind
description:
The Void here. It is like nothing else. Surely you can feel it, too? The overwhelming power...

We must do something to stabilize the area. I'm barely able to keep our escape route from imploding, and the rest of our scouts... I can only hope they can hold it together.

The ren'dorei know what to do. They will place Shadestar Loci to protect this area, but will need your help activating them.

title: The Far, Far Frontier
description:
We have secured this location. Some void elves can make this into a proper camp.

There must be some weakness we can exploit to stop the storm. We must bring my army here to mete out justice to Xal'atath's armies!

Join me and young Arator. Let us see what we can gather from this place across the horizon.

title: A Strange, Different World
description:
I don't know the purpose of these towers, only that they are called Nexus-Points and they thrum with Void power. We must get more information. 

The domanaar use some kind of Void-infused metal on the beasts here to add them to armies of the Devouring Host. They call it shadowgrafting.

It empowers them, but also influences their minds.

<Alleria smiles wryly.> 

What power doesn't? 

I have a plan for it. With you here, I can finally act on it.

title: Dancing with Death
description:
We may be a small strike team, but we are strong. We have you, after all. 

There is no need to move with complete stealth. We must cut a path to the domanaar here. 

Take down the strongest enemies you can find. We will join you in the fray.

title: No Prayer for the Wicked
description:
Alleria has a plan to use this shadowgrafting material to capture that domanaar for information. 

But what good is information given by evil? I've fought countless demons and every one of them was a liar.

<Lothraxion scowls.> 

Let us follow her plan, for now. Do what she asks with this metal, then slay the domanaar's pet beast. 

Once we speak with this vile creature, I will decide how long it lives.

title: Reliable Enemies
description:
The ren'dorei scouts report a familiar enemy upon this world: ethereals. Wretched scavengers and bandits that plague every corner of the Great Dark Beyond. The Shadowguard are the worst of them. We'll handle them.

This... thing we've taken prisoner must know what they're doing here, and much else. Press the Void's secrets from it. See that it is convinced to share what it knows, or I will employ my own methods.

title: Overwhelmed
description:
We were too late. All of the strength between us wasn't enough.

<Arator looks over his shoulder, wary. Then, he crouches and places his hand on Alleria's shoulder. She responds only with a ragged exhale.>

Let's fall back. The ren'dorei have been exploring the wilds while we've been following Lothraxion. We might be able to find their encampment nearby.

title: Work Disruption
description:
The Shadowguard may once have defied Xal'atath, but they're part of her army now. Whatever they're doing with the energies gathered here must be stopped.

Put down their soldiers, harvesters, and machines. I'll do the same, and meet you deeper within.

title: First, The Shells
description:
Ages ago, on a dismal world of swamp, I led the Army of the Light to a demon-infested manaforge obscured by vines and fog. My soldiers left that world in a haze of flame and smoke, the scent of burning mana and victory in the air. We will do now as we did then.

We'll begin by collecting the empty canisters they use to hold and transport fuel. The ethereals, and especially their automatons, will have them.

title: A Cracked Holokey
description:
<A unique-looking ethereal device rests upon the cold ground. It bears a crack down the middle from damage in a scuffle.

Perhaps Alleria will know what it is.>

title: Second, The Fuel
description:
Ethereals seek corners of the cosmos to drain of potent nether. Arcane, Void, fel--to them, it matters only the yield. And we have a rich vein to work with.

Now, slay those who possess that energy: wild arcane elementals from their pipes, or their ethereal harvesters flush with energy. We have need of it.

title: Network Insecurity
description:
Judging by the design and the strength of this holokey's energy, it must be used to encrypt important communications.

Let us try this lead. See if it's compatible with any of the consoles you find around the refinery. Given the damage it's taken, we'll have to piece together whatever scraps of information we can.

title: Agents of Darkness
description:
When that shield goes down, Xal'atath's forces will be upon us in droves. If I'm going to detonate those manacells from afar, I'll need an opening. Thin the Devouring Host of their strongest, and the rest of Xal'atath's lackeys will be easier to fight off.

We will make sure the shield goes down in the meantime. Good luck, {name}.

title: Third, Blow It Up
description:
The manacells have been prepared. Set them near the pylon and its supporting machinery. When it's done, I will command Alleria to ignite them from afar.

But we cannot proceed until the naaru has been brought to safety. Go now and I will make certain of her freedom.

title: Just In Case...
description:
Perhaps Lothraxion will be able to bring that shield down on his own. Or perhaps he is a pompous blowhard.

The Devouring Host offers its trusted soldiers fragments of a relic that can override any ethereal device, including that shield. You'll need to carve quite a swath through their forces to recover enough shards.

But what do I know? I am merely unworthy voidspawn.

title: Flicker in the Dark
description:
We're dawdling! There's no time to waste!

Get that shield down, now! T'era's anguish tears at my soul!

title: Post-Mortem
description:
We all bear a heavy burden in this place. I feel myself slipping towards hopelessness. But we haven't failed until we refuse to stand back up. I know my mother will collect herself. But the others need to know this fight isn't over. 

Speak with Hieron. He and the other scouts should have information. We can start there.

title: The Light's Brand
description:
No! {name}, we have to stop them! Lothraxion's boiling temper risks everything! That he chooses now of all times, when my mother is out of commission...

There's no time. We have to go!

title: A Naaru!
description:
I refuse to let another naaru fall to darkness.

Before our departure, the scouts reported they had found a Nexus-Point connected to this forge. If the Void's forces possess a naaru, it might be where they're keeping it.

I will prepare the manacells as we conduct the search. Come.

title: Friend or Fiend
description:
Despite that spirited display, I'm inclined to help you. After all, what's a little violence between friends? 

And since we're friends, I'll let you in on a secret: shutting down the storm is only possible from inside the Nexus-Points. Of course you can't just walk in--you need the Mantle of Predation.

<Decimus seems to savor the words.>

Do I have your attention? Let's shake the dust off and discuss this somewhere... civilized. Meet me at my estate.

title: Domus Penumbra
description:
It seems we all wish to stop the Voidstorm. Common ground--a rare thing here. I'll even tell you how to do it, and all I ask in return is for you to let me enjoy the show.

But you must be tired. Show yourself around, and help yourself to the amenities. Then we will continue our discussion.

Don't take too long. There is much to see, and the storm rages on.

title: The Lay of the Beast
description:
Your friend's scheme to blow up the Nexus-Point was adorable. You all looked so determined, I didn't have the heart to tell you how simple the solution really is.

The Mantle of Predation will allow you to enter the Nexus-Points and weaken the storm. Alas, it's claiming the Mantle that will truly test you.

Borrow my pet--see through his eyes. I'll show you who you'll steal the Mantle from.

title: Edge of the Abyss
description:
I don't trust Decimus any more than the rest of the beasts here. I do trust that they only serve themself. Seems having Xal'atath as a master isn't to their taste. We can turn one foe against another. Even Lothraxion would agree if he weren't so stubborn.

If this Mantle of Predation will let us stop the storm, then it's our best shot. A troop of ren'dorei have been scouting the Obscurion Citadel. We'll meet with them and see what they've learned.

title: The Harvest
description:
Two sigils--you'll need both to open Terminas's vault and claim the Mantle.

The first belongs to Torentia, petty ruler of this sad trench. Too ambitious for Terminas to bring her into his inner circle, too cowardly to strike out on her own. So here she cowers behind an unbreakable shield of Void.

She's terrified of losing her already meager holdings. What a pity. Slaughter your way through the trench. The potential emergence of a new apex predator is something she won't ignore.

title: Face the Tide
description:
I expected an army, but this is chaos--beasts pouring from the Void, gorging themselves, taking captives for sport.

Our mission is to claim the Mantle, but we can't ignore this. Sooner or later, these forces will be loosed against Silvermoon. Any blows we strike now will save lives down there, {name}.

Starve their forces, stem the flow of beasts from the Void, and free every captive they've abducted.

title: Lady of the Pit
description:
For all their power, these domanaar still behave like beasts. That's good for us. Wolves try to avoid each other, but if one threatens another's territory, they'll fight to the death. 

By now, I think you've more than proven that you're a threat. Torentia can't back down if you challenge her--it would go against her nature.

So take the fight to her, slay her, and claim her sigil.

title: Hollow Hunger
description:
Alleria's judgement is clouded. She's spent too much time steeped in Void. I've prosecuted campaigns across the cosmos. Casualties are inevitable. I could not command if the loss of every soldier broke my will.

We have the information we need: the location of the sigil bearer. The fiends whisper of a domanaar named Severum. Slay him and take his sigil, then we can be rid of this place.

title: All Become Prey
description:
Lothraxion's acting like we have an army, but a single ultradon will tear through us if we're caught off guard. We need to be smarter.

I've watched the beasts of this world: studied them, tracked them, killed them. They prey on weakness, and cull their own kind at the first sign of frailty. 

Harvest carrion essence from the carcasses around the citadel. Use it to mark ultradons. Other beasts will pounce, thinking they're wounded or dying. Let them tear each other apart, then clean up what's left.

title: Vanished in the Void
description:
"Acceptable losses." I hope I never find it that easy to dismiss the deaths of friends and comrades. What use is the Light if it leaves us as callous as our enemies?

Sideras was nearly killed. Luckily, he escaped through a rift. Riftblade Maella led the others in a fighting retreat. Find her. Hopefully they stuck together.

I can't leave Sideras--not until he's stable.

title: The Mantle of Predation
description:
Let's move quickly. We need to steal the Mantle before Terminas returns and before Lothraxion does something rash. 

I understand this place grates on him, but he should know to trust my instincts. I thought we'd fought together long enough to earn that.

Let's end this. Ready when you are.

title: Abyssus, Abyssum
description:
I don't see Decimus. For all their glee at handing us over, I'd expect they'd want to watch the fight. What could be more important to a domanaar than enjoying a battle?

<Alleria nocks another arrow.>

Answers can wait. Right now we need to survive.

title: Hunt the Light
description:
There's no time. Lothraxion already has the lead. 

Get to Nexus-Point Xenas, {name}. We'll meet you there.

title: Nexus-Point Xenas: Eclipse
description:
If he destroys the Nexus-Point, everyone we're fighting for could perish. I'll try to talk him down, but if I can't, if I have to...

<Alleria's face clouds.>

No hesitation. We end this, one way or another.

title: Daylight is Breaking
description:
Turalyon is busying himself with the troops. We'll need to let him know what they'll face here, tell him about Lothraxion...

<Alleria shakes her head.>

Thank you, {name}. Even when my thoughts feel clouded, you never seem to waver. Go, give Lor'themar my report. Arator and I will brief Turalyon.

title: The Wayward Magister
description:
Magister Umbric--he's second only to mother in mastering the Void. If anyone can help us with that storm in the sky, he's the one who can make it happen... if he can forgive the others for not listening to him in the first place. 

At least Lor'themar saw reason. There's no way we can take the fight to Xal'atath without Umbric.

The forces keeping the Voidstorm at bay will only hold out so long. Let's make haste.

title: Appeal to the Void
description:
Kasiea... whatever you've found, you've perished for it.

I won't let her work be in vain. 

There's something to be found here, {name}. There are a few locations listed. Let's travel to them and try some of these theorems. The formulae may be incomplete, but perhaps we can discover what she was looking for. 

It had to have been important.

title: Rational Explanation
description:
Something just doesn't feel right about all of this. Umbric can look into the notebook, but I suspect there's something more going on here. 

It couldn't hurt to ask around Tranquillien and see if anyone's heard anything odd. I'll take the north side of town, and you take the south.

And... use your discretion, my friend. We should avoid alarming anyone for now.

title: The First to Know
description:
Notes speaking of fear, of uncertainty--of some mysterious truth? This is not good, {name}. By all accounts, it sounds as if a cult has managed to infiltrate Tranquillien. 

If that journal entry was true, Kasiea was attempting to leave it. 

We must tell Antenorian. He'll want to secure the town and make sure his people are safe.

title: Chance Meeting
description:
I've heard some odd rumors myself. Strange happenings to the south--but also to the east and west, and the north. Tranquillien is surrounded by enemies, it seems.

Since you are so dead-set on your investigation, would you look into something for me?

There is an important report I need from one of my scouts. Check in with them, while I check over the rest of town.

title: The Ransacked Lab
description:
My notes, my research... I never should have left it alone.

I was just beginning to understand the Voidstorm! Everything I'd learned, torn from my hands by cultists... Were they watching the entire time? Waiting for me to turn my back?

I cannot let this stop me. They can't have destroyed everything. Perhaps there's enough here to salvage.

title: The Battle for Tranquillien
description:
The Twilight's Blade? Attacking in broad daylight now that their cover's blown... we'll prove what a mistake that was.

Slay the traitors and the creatures they've summoned. Destroy their effigies and their supplies. 

Make this retreat costly, {name}.

title: The Heart of Tranquillien
description:
We can't let the Twilight's Blade get away with this. There are innocent citizens all over town who had nothing to do with this cult. You heard the governor: anyone who doesn't join will be executed.

I won't stand for that.

Go--aid any civilians you can find. Get them out of harm's way. If they're being attacked, take their attacker down. Tranquillien will not fall under my watch.

title: The Traitors of Tranquillien
description:
Some of the town's most respected citizens, traitors?

How long has the Twilight's Blade been here? How many scouts have gone missing? Crimes gone unreported? Innocents recruited by those they thought they could trust?

They must be stopped. Cut down the traitors. Show them that Tranquillien is no home for cultists.

title: The Missing Magister
description:
Umbric went to chase down Antenorian when the fighting broke out, but nobody has seen him since. 

I trust him to hold his own, but between Kasiea's death and Antenorian's betrayal, I worry he may be so set on revenge that he won't see an attack if one happens. Can you find him for me?

I'll stay here and make sure these civilians remain safe.

title: Face the Past
description:
<Umbric scans the Eversong map with grave intent.>

Tranquillien was marked, as are a number of other locations. 

<Umbric stops, voice tightening.>

Windrunner Village. It's marked.

The Twilight's Blade retreated to their base in Deatholme, but my home may be in peril. We must make sure my people in Windrunner Village are safe before pursuing them.

title: The Past Keeps Watch
description:
I don't trust the quiet here. Let's shore up the defenses. Did you see those slumped-over arcane golems around the village?

They may not look like much now, but a quick tune up will have them on their feet soon enough. They're good for standing guard, and also for ferreting out any hidden snakes in the weeds.

Let's get to work. The sooner the golems are running, the better I'll feel about this town's safety.

title: Comprehend the Void
description:
Kasiea's notes may be lost forever, but we must try and improvise. I have one final ritual to try.

If this doesn't work, then the answers lay in Deatholme, the Twilight's Blade's heart. But first...

title: To Deatholme
description:
That coward Antenorian has doomed us all. I had hoped it wouldn't come to this, but...

We need the rest of Kasiea's research--the missing pages that were stolen from her corpse. But the cult has retreated south, to the ruins of Deatholme.

Fitting that such traitors would sequester themselves in such a vile place. But we must root them out.

title: Void Walk With Me
description:
I did not expect this place to be so heavily guarded. This is far more complicated than one charismatic leader and a group of wayward pilgrims. Still... if we take out Antenorian, it should weaken them considerably. A cult is nothing without its leader.

Valeera tracked the governor to that cavern. He's gone in. We'll make sure he never comes out.

title: Anything but Reprieve
description:
This is dire. The Twilight's Blade is much larger--much more powerful--than any of us could have guessed.

I'm relieved Umbric is on the case, but we need to let Silvermoon know what's happened here.

title: Choking Tendrils
description:
I haven't the expertise of Orweyna in these matters. But if the Lightbloom moved this quickly, I assume the rutaani are accelerating the spread here just as they were in Fairbreeze.

The town is beyond saving, but we can at least try to contain the Lightbloom within its walls.

title: What's Left
description:
You see what we have to contend with here. Runestone Shan'dor is helping stop the spread further north, but if this Lightbloom surges again, I don't know if we can halt it.

I've heard cries for help from survivors still trapped within. We have to find them, and direct them to safety!

title: Premonition
description:
I came as quickly as I could. This is... devastating.

I have called in our Magisters to reinforce this runestone. They will keep an eye on the Lightbloom spread.

I suspect Halduron Brightwing and the Farstriders have uncovered more than the villagers know. I can rely on him for anything. We shall rendezvous with him at the encampment to the north, and soon have some answers.

title: Old Scars
description:
Of course the Amani would use this moment to encroach, the cowards. We are beset on all sides!

This land belongs to the sin'dorei, and I will not see it taken by anyone. Not by raging plants, not by a cult, not by the Void, and never by the Amani!

Whatever they are doing over there, it must be stopped. Do whatever you can to disrupt them and send them scurrying back to Zul'Aman where they belong.

title: A Foe Unseen
description:
I know Lor'themar is angry, but... this is strange, {name}. They didn't attack Suncrown--the Lightbloom did. What are the Amani doing over there?

There has to be some kind of explanation for this. It doesn't look like an invasion. Let's see what we can find out.

title: Following the Root
description:
Lor'themar has a right to defend this land, but I don't think he's seeing clearly. The presence of Amani is too inciting.

This doesn't add up. The trolls are going out of their way to craft Lightbloom weapons--to fight what? It isn't us. They didn't attack Suncrown. They could have, and they didn't.

We need to find the truth. Let's head south. That hideout I discovered must have some answers.

title: Gods Before Us
description:
I suppose I shouldn't have expected a greeting with open arms, but I did not expect to be trapped in here.

The Amani are clever. They must have made a way to escape. We have only to find it. Let's look around.

title: An Impasse
description:
How deep does this crypt go?

We need to find Zul'jan and explain to him that we fight a common enemy. Surely he'll see reason!

Come on. They can't have gotten much farther in this maze.

title: Beat of Blood
description:
None of this is necessary. We need to make them see reason!

My father will know what to do. I know he'll see reason, once I explain what has happened here. There is no need for bloodshed! It's simple: if the Amani are set against the Twilight's Blade, we fight on the same side.

title: Light Guide Us
description:
Our findings at Tor'Watha were correct. By the time Turalyon and I arrived here with troops, the Amani had already begun to take the pass. They seek to establish another path to acquire the lightwood from our land.

The Amani have chosen war, as they always do. This, at least, is a threat I know how to handle.

Arator is but a boy. He cannot grasp the magnitude of history between the sin'dorei and Amani--and I don't expect him to.

Will you stand by the blood elves, and all that is right?

title: Past Redemption
description:
These monsters deserve nothing more than swift retribution. For Silvermoon. For the sin'dorei.

<Turalyon's eyes blaze with Light.>

For Alleria. This will be finished!

title: Fractured
description:
Lady Liadrin will remain here. The rest of us shall return to Silvermoon and debrief.

<Lor'themar looks at the devastated Turalyon.>

We still have many battles ahead, it seems.

title: The Path of the Amani
description:
Kinduru is my uncle, and I trust him... But dis "path of de hash'ey"...

Things are not what dey were decades ago. The loa are gone--they not gonna hear us.

<Zul'jarra lets out a low growl only you and Liadrin hear.>

No. We're running out of options. If dis is de time for old myths and dreams, so be it.

We meet at the foot of the mountains, Akil'zon's Reach, where de old hash'ey shrines are.

title: Gnarldin Bashing
description:
Zul'jarra regards the gnarldin as a fierce, nomadic people that normally keep to themselves. It is clear by their clothing and weapons that they have a primitive but unique culture.

If the circumstances were different, I would advocate for diplomacy.

But they are between us and our mission.

They cannot block our path or continue to damage the shrines or the nests of the eagles that roost here. We will eliminate only those that are in our way. The Amani can choose what to do with them later.

title: De Ancient Path
description:
My uncle, de loa speaker, spoke of de hash'ey when I was a child. But when de loa remained silent so many years...

I do not think he would admit it, but he lost a little faith too.

I remember some of his lessons.

A chieftain must light de shrines on de way to de temple. De hash'ura must as well. Why? I do not remember.

But if it is de only way to get de loa's favor? Come. Let us get dis out of de way.

title: Ahead of the Issue
description:
I do not know de loa Akil'zon, but she cannot be happy with de gnarldin defilin' de hash'ey shrines and makin' meals of her eagle kin.

We gotta let dem know dis is a sacred place and dat we will not take kindly to their presence here.

I may not know Akil'zon, but I know de giants. All dey understand is strength--de language of Zul'Aman.

Our strongest message we can send is to take de heads of their fiercest warriors.

title: Brutal Feast
description:
<Zul'jarra's brow furrows and her lip curls as she looks toward the temple.>

When we lit de last shrine, some of de eagles returned to de temple. It could be a sign.

<Zul'jarra scoffs.>

Or de eagles are movin' in after de gnarldin started movin' out.

When we get to the temple, all we gonna find is silence. Just like always.

But we still gonna try. We should go now and present an offerin'.

title: Test of Conviction
description:
We got her attention. But now what?

I know there is a trial. But Akil'zon is not givin' any hints on what it is.

Think. Think. What did Kinduru teach me as a child? She is de loa of victory. But also of oaths and conviction.

If only we had made it to de top when I was a child, and I hadn't fallen...

title: Shadebasin Watch
description:
<Zul'jarra smiles slightly as Akil'zon's blessing buffets her.>

Kinduru's stories were real. We have de blessin' of Akil'zon, and de path of the hash'ey stretches out before us.

We must follow Akil'zon's advice and seek out Halazzi next. As Akil'zon is de loa of de rulin' tribe--de Amani--and my family, Halazzi is de loa of the Witherbark and their leaders.

If their loa speakers are gone, then maybe Kul'amara will know how to approach him.

title: The Crypt in the Mist
description:
<Zul'jarra growls through her gritted tusks.>

Now it's not as surprisin' dat Malacrass's tribe, de Vilebranch, fell to the lure of hex magic.

But dat is their choice and a path dey chose long ago--a path away from us.

I won't let dem live long enough to regret it.

What de Vilebranch have done to de Witherbark has gone too long without an Amani response. De mist is not in our way.

We can use it to our advantage.

We will take a life for all de lives dey took. And make sure dey stay dead.

title: Rescue from the Shadows
description:
Kul'amara has told me dat a shadowy mist has fallen over Numazon. She believes de Vilebranch are behind it, usin' their magic to hide de crimes dey commit against de Witherbark.

She believes dey are hidin' in de Numazon crypts. De hash'ey shrines are near there as well.

We must find our way to Numazon and rescue de Witherbark captives. If we find de hash'ey shrines along de way, so be it.

If Halazzi will not stand with us, den maybe de Witherbark will.

title: Breaching the Mist
description:
The Vilebranch seemed to be just as confused by the mist as the Witherbark.

Whatever has caused de mist, dealin' with de Vilebranch changed nothing.

And now we must find de hash'ey shrines in de dark.

<Zul'jarra moves her hand in front of her face, testing her range of visibility, and frowns.>

We must light de shrines. Try not to get separated.

Dis will try my patience. But I'm sure if we use our heads, we will find de path.

title: Halazzi's Guile
description:
Who are these people? Where did they come from?

Did dey find their way here from Numazon? How? De mist keeps you from knowin' if you are comin' or goin'.

And where is Halazzi?

I need answers... now!

title: Coals of a Dead Loa
description:
By helpin' de Witherbark, we helped ourselves--and passed Halazzi's trial.

There is a lesson in that.

Now dat Halazzi has returned, we should go back and let Vun'zarah know. I hope dis will improve de relationship between our tribes.

And maybe he knows where we can find out how to resurrect Jan'alai.

title: The Riddled Speaker
description:
Chief Vun'zarah knew little of Jan'alai, but he did know about their loa speaker.

If dey are still at de temple, then there is hope we can resurrect de loa.

<Zul'jarra's hand tightens on her axe.>

Makin' Akil'zon flee, woundin' Halazzi, sendin' Nalorakk into a rage, and killin' Jan'alai...

My grandfather and Malacrass have so much to pay for.

title: Embers to a Flame
description:
The loa speaker talks of fire in de heart--in all hearts.

Jan'alai is de loa of birth, death, and rebirth. Maybe de loa speaker is askin' for sacrifice.

<Zul'jarra smiles deviously.>

Hey, Liadrin. How much fire you think is in an elf's heart, eh?

<Liadrin gives her a sour look.>

Heh.

Whatever de loa speaker wants, it is either part of resurrectin' de loa or part of de trial. Lets see what he has do say.

title: Seer or Sear
description:
Dis old, crazy loa speaker is too busy dancin' to tell us what to do next.

But I think I know.

We could not light de shrines on de way to de temple. But now dat Jan'alai's presence is here...

Maybe now we can.

I will take de ember to de hash'ey shrines. Den maybe de trial will start.

I jumped from de top of de temple of Akil'zon. I found my way through de mists of Halazzi.

I am ready for Jan'alai's fire.

We can only hope dey will receive us after what my grandfather has done.

title: Face in the Fire
description:
First my grandfather, then my brother.

Jan'alai knew my shame at my grandfather's rage. And de rage I feel when Zul'jan disrespects me. I know I put fuel on his fire by respondin' de way I do. 

Maybe dat needs to change. I have left my doubt of de loa behind me. I can leave those thoughts behind too, and continue de path of de hash'ey.

Stoke de coal, {name}. I am ready for de final trial, whatever it may be.

title: The Flames Rise Higher
description:
De coal... it's white-hot now. And I feel like there is somethin' inside it?

Brek said dis was de last trial. But Jan'alai is not here.

What do we do now?

<Zul'jarra looks at Brek and scowls.>

Talk to Brek and see if he's got anything to say we can understand.

title: In the Shadow of Rebirth
description:
Jan'alai's trial has made me realize...

I need to make things right between me and my brother.

He acts out at me, but it's because I put him down. I do not think about how hard it must be to be my brother.

We should also check on how our preparations are goin' and if my troops are gettin' de food and rest dey need.

Nalorakk's not gonna go anywhere. He can wait a few hours.

title: Hash'ey Away
description:
We've done it, {name}. De loa are at our side once again.

Am I hash'ey? I do not know. But I do know I would not have gotten dis far without my hash'ura.

Come. We will return to Amani'Zar and tell Kinduru and Zul'jan de good news.

title: The Blade's Edge
description:
With de other tribes and de loa's help, we can take de fight to de Twilight's Blade and take dem down.

Blood for blood!

title: Chip and Shatter
description:
<Zul'jarra shakes her head back and forth and jumps in place, shaking out her muscles.

She stops and turns to you, grinning broadly.>

Heh. Do you feel it, my hash'ura?

De electricity in de air? De smell of blood and fire?

My soul is leapin' to join de fight.

You ready to chop de enemy down, bathe in dey blood, and make dem pay for what dey done?

Me too.

title: Light Indiscriminate
description:
Though the dead litter this field, I still see plenty of Amani clinging to life.

Help me heal the injured. We'll ensure their reputation for survival continues on after this day.

title: Conduit Crisis
description:
With de loa at our side, we can finally break de Twilight's Blade and return to Amani'Zar with Mor'duun's head.

De loa are already attackin' de main source of Mor'duun's power: summoned spheres of pure void energy.

Go to de loa and offer your assistance. When de orbs are destroyed, we can move forward and strike at Mor'duun!

title: Clear de Way
description:
With dey magic and dey line broken, de Twilight's Blade is losin' ground.

We must take advantage of dey turned backs.

I will lead de charge through de ravine to de Conquerer's Sanctum beyond. Mor'duun is there, keepin' his coward-body safe from our spears and axes.

He won't be safe much longer.

title: Blade Shattered
description:
It ends here. De path of the hash'ey, my path, has led me here.

Let dis fight bring a new age for my people.

Come. It is time.

title: De Legend of de Hash'ey
description:
Mor'duun is slain.

De Twilight's Blade will continue to plague our lands, but of dem only Mor'duun's "fodder" is left. Dey will be easy prey for us.

And now I will say somethin' my uncle would be proud of. Let us follow de teachin' of Nalorakk, de Loa of War.

De battle is over. It is time to rest--I can tell by your eyes both my hash'ura need it too.

Let us leave dis place. We can speak again back in Amani'Zar.

title: Broken Bridges
description:
I make one last request of you before you return with Liadrin to Silvermoon, hash'ura.

Find Zul'jan. None have seen him since returnin' to Amani'Zar.

On de way back here, I confronted him about takin' our soldiers to Broken Throne against my orders.

People died dat shouldn't have. I asked him what he don't understand about dat.

He didn't argue. He was blank. His body was there, but his mind?

Sometimes he seeks peace in de sound of ocean waves.

You might find him on de Skybridge.

title: The Gates of Zul'Aman
description:
After the Amani surrender, we demanded Zul'jan relinquish our lightwood and return to their lands. Even so, Zul'jan still lingers at the gates of Zul'Aman with his troops.

Lady Liadrin and her Blood Knights hold the pass, and Liadrin is trying to remedy the situation with diplomacy, but she reports that aggression may begin anew.

She requests your assistance in this precarious matter.

title: The Line Must be Drawn Here
description:
I had hoped that Zul'jan would leave the lightwood and return swiftly to his lands after our confrontation with him.

But dealing with the Amani has never been a simple matter.

Diplomacy is the only way to avoid more bloodshed, but Zul'jan is finished listening to me.

Perhaps he will listen to you?

title: Our Mutual Enemy
description:
<Liadrin lowers her voice, speaking only to you.>

We have two choices. We can either continue to talk to the Amani or fight our way inside.

I would still prefer to negotiate passage with Zul'jarra, but if our diplomacy goes awry, be ready.

For now, follow my lead.

title: Amani Clarion Call
description:
If you want to survive here in Atal'Aman, find and beat de city's battle drums. Dat will rally our remainin' soldiers forward to join de fight.

It will also bring all de tribes of Zul'Aman--de Witherbark, de Revantusk, and de Shadowpine--to our aid and tell our civilians to retreat to de Altar of Malacrass if we need to evacuate.

Use the drums to spread de word. Find me at de Altar of Malacrass when you are done.

title: Goodwill Tour
description:
The situation here is far worse than I imagined.

The Twilight's Blade and their leader, Mor'duun, have completely overrun this city.

We will use this opportunity to show the Amani how efficient we are at eliminating forces of the Void. Perhaps that will help convince them to stall hostilities between our people... at least until the current threat has passed.

title: Important Amani
description:
De drums have sounded. But de elders have not come.

We cannot lose them. They are our link to our culture--our sages and our best minds.

Torundo helped devise de lightwood weapons. And Doru is our provisioner. We will need both if de battle goes on.

Go find Doru in de market and Torundo in de tribute hall.

De last one is de old loa speaker--our uncle, Kinduru. He may need... special persuasion to leave. I will go to Talonstrike Terrace to speak to him. Meet me there after you find de others.

title: Show Us Your Worth
description:
De Twilight's Blade's General, Mor'duun, has got us by de throat. We're not winnin', and we are too few.

We've got to evacuate before there's no one left--and hope de other tribes meet us at Amani'Zar.

In de meantime, I'm glad to use you while we rally de people of Zul'Aman. 

De civilians' lives are my priority. Go to Swiftclaw Terrace and evacuate de people there.

Tear through de Twilight's Blade as you go. Find your way to Zul'jan when you're done.

title: Armed by Light
description:
You might be good at killing, {class}, but so are we Amani.

And even more de better our weapons!

I took an army into Eversong to reclaim de lightwood forests for de Amani. It was against my sister's orders, yes, but lightwood gives us an advantage.

Take back de weapons being stolen by the de Twilight's Blade, and I'll show you why it was worth de effort.

title: Everything We Worked For
description:
My blood is boiling! While waiting for Kinduru, de Twilight's Blade was destroying what little stores of lightwood we had left at Ragemaw Terrace!

But if we act quickly, we can save enough to arm de lightwood ballista--and take a shot at Mor'duun.

And without de ballista, we cannot claim victory!

title: Twilight Bled
description:
Mor'duun may be de leader of de Twilight's Blade in Zul'Aman, but dat ogre can't be everywhere. De elf found his military commander--a hulkin' pile of armor and flesh named Benvis Bladesplatter.

He's paradin' 'round like he's already won just because dey seized Firewing Terrace.

Make him pay for his arrogance.

title: Rituals Cut Short
description:
In our reconnaissance flight around the city, Zul'jarra and I got a look at the Twilight's offensive. They're summoning more Twilight's Blade directly into Atal'Aman.

We can counter them by disrupting their dark summoning rituals. That will stop the flow of their cultists into the city and give the Amani time to strategize.

Kill the ritualists, and any trollbreakers already portaled into the area.

title: The Amani Stand Strong
description:
Zul'jarra doesn't think de lightwood is worth de risks I am taking. We will prove her wrong.

I have seen too many warriors discouraged because their weapons don't work against de Twilight's Blade.

Don't just tell them to retreat. Give them de lightwood weapons.

Nothing stirs Amani blood like an effective axe in their hands. Let dem fight their way back to de Altar of Malacrass.

title: Break the Blade
description:
Zul'jan's ballista is ready. And it had better work after what he has done.

We lost de army we needed to defend de city, but we can fend off Mor'duun long enough to let our people escape.

Help my brother man de ballista. Focus on de shadow warders and de pylon. Liadrin and I will distract Mor'duun.

I will kill him if I can, but even then we can't stay here. There's too much void energy. And too much destruction. Atal'Aman is truly lost.

title: Heart of the Amani
description:
You and your elf friend amuse me. And you've more than proved yourself in battle.

Our people are headin' to Amani'Zar. We hope dat de other tribes rallied there instead of Atal'Aman. Their representatives will give us council, and then we will mount an attack on de Twilight's Blade.

If you still wish to fight with de Amani, meet me on de northwest side of de village. Zul'jan will have gone ahead to speak of his deeds--and yours--but do not enter de village without me. Amani kill strangers on sight.

title: Isolation
description:
We will speak to Torundo, my war advisor. And if de representatives of de tribes are here, we will have a council meetin' in de Zari Lodge.

Together, we must agree on a way to defeat de Twilight's Blade.

Explore de village if you want. But keep your weapons where dey can be seen. You are here by my invitation and my word is law, but I can only run so fast if one of my people thinks you looked at dem in de wrong way.

<Zul'jarra smiles a tusky grin as if proud of her people's short fuse.>

title: Left in the Shadows
description:
De loa have not spoken to us in many years... not since I was a young child.

And dey have shown no sign of wanting to help us now.

No. It is up to us alone. We must find a way to rally de other tribes and fight de Twilight's Blade as one.

I will speak to de tribal representatives and help de refugees where I can--dey are our priority.

Come, you might learn somethin' about de Amani.

title: Fair Breeze, Light Bloom
description:
In recent years, small pockets of wild growth we call "Lightbloom" have blossomed throughout Eversong. A curiosity for our local botanists, for the most part.

However, the Magistrix in Fairbreeze is asking for help with the local Lightbloom. Every capable hand I have is focused on holding back the Voidstorm--but if the Magistrix cannot handle the matter herself, it requires swift attention.

I'd like you to see to the issue. Magister Rommath can provide you with an arcane projector to keep in touch.

title: Sharpmaw
description:
These beasts roaming the village are nuisances, but we have a bigger problem.

There's a feral lynx prowling nearby that we call Sharpmaw. She used to keep her distance from the village, but the current situation has made her more bold. We've plenty of little ones around here, and more than once the guards have caught that beast eyeing them like they were a snack waiting to be snatched. 

Stop her before she starts attacking my people in earnest!

title: Fairbreeze Favors
description:
This is the oddest situation I've ever been in--our village is being overrun!

I like to keep things neat and tidy around here, but these animals... they've come in from the wilds like they own the town. They're attacking our sweet pets, overturning our belongings, and causing havoc. No sooner have I solved one problem than another is rearing its head. I'm only one elf--I can't stop all of this on my own!

Lend a hand, won't you? This... elf... troll... person hasn't lifted a finger.

title: Displaced Denizens
description:
The wildlife here seems to have been upset by something. Perhaps this is why the world's song bade me here: to discover why and correct it.

Study the displaced wildlife. Bring me your findings. If there is anything unusual, I wish to know of it. I will speak with the Magistrix. Perhaps together we can uncover this mystery.

title: Lightbloom Looming
description:
The wildlife seems to have been displaced from the south. It is there we will surely find answers.

Come--I will use my magic to illuminate the path and we will follow the trail.

title: Curious Cultivation
description:
This place is unlike anything I have seen before. The plant growth is unique.

{name}, let us examine the Lightbloom more closely. Perhaps we can uncover the secrets of this place together.

title: Trimming the Lightbloom
description:
The creatures of this Lightbloom see us as the intruders. They threaten the surrounding wildlife and inhabitants who are naturally here.

We should reduce their numbers and end this threat.

title: Seeking Truth
description:
I will channel on this strange seed we have collected, and see if I can discover what has happened here.

This may upset the rutaani. Make sure I am not interrupted. This is why I was called here--I know now. But I must discover what needs to be done.

title: Silvermoon Must Know
description:
This growth--Lor'themar needs to know about this. I worry what will happen should it get out of control. 

And the Light's influence... my father should know, too.

title: Eversong
description:
Magistrix Landra Dawnstrider has sent a request for aid in dealing with the local Lightbloom. While typically harmless, it has recently begun to grow at an incredible rate.

Meet up with Arator in Fairbreeze Village to investigate the threat and provide what assistance you can.

title: Watch the Den
description:
Finally, we have a moment to breathe. This is where we feel closest to the goddess--we are below the Cradle where she used to rest.

The elders will not allow us to use any of the Alndust they have gathered over the centuries. They see only what the goddess asked of us before, not what her world needs now.

Alndust is rare, but there are ways to get it. Ask around. I will check... elsewhere.

title: The Hunter Awaits
description:
I have warned Orweyna of the dangers and madness that lay within the Rift of Aln, but she believes it is her only path forward. I envy her faith.

Someone can help show her the way: the Shul'ka. The Hunters of Shadows. They have sacrificed everything to protect the haranir from the effects of the rift.

I will let one know that you need help. You will find him in the Vale of Mists.

title: The Foundation of Aln
description:
The presence of the goddess reverberates through everything in the Rift of Aln. The voice is too powerful for haranir to resist. In mere minutes, one of our flock would be lost forever.

There are, however, ways to protect oneself for a short time. Kill the rejects that crawl up from the rift and gather the Alndust from which they are created. I can show Orweyna how to craft a short-term solution for entering the rift.

title: Consequences of Our Duty
description:
You must see with your own eyes the effects that the Rift of Aln can have on any haranir. Even those who stand guard over the border are oft affected by its call.

Take this. I use it as an emergency salve to help those who are stricken of the dangers of Aln. Patrol these lands and give aid to those who need it. Perhaps then you will understand the gravity of what you seek to do.

title: Dampening the Call
description:
Amarakk told me how to prepare Alndust to create the salve. In small doses, Alndust can strengthen the mind against madness and terror. It offers clarity and peace, but only if expertly employed.

I could use your help to set an anchor to this place, {name}. The ritual is delicate, and failure could be disastrous.

title: Descent into the Rift
description:
With this, I will be able to venture down into the Rift of Aln. I will not have long, so we should not dawdle.

Let us find Amarakk and gain his help.

title: The Madness Roots Deep
description:
I found you while I was in the middle of one of my duties. Someone has spent too long within the rift and has fallen prey to the call of the goddess. I must rescue him and send him back to where he belongs.

Help me with this task, and I will help you.

title: Grinding Out a Solution
description:
I have been pondering what Hagar could have meant from the moment Orweyna mentioned an answer to your problems. There are no mystical fountains--no weapons. Everything in the rift is created from the same source: Alndust, the distillation of the influence of the goddess. If we hunt a large manifestation, you will have the power of creation in its purest form.

We cannot defeat it without help. Search the rift and look for fungi large enough to cultivate. I can use them to give us an edge.

title: Before They Grow
description:
Within every creature that roams the Rift of Aln thrums a gathering of the foundation of the goddess's influence. This Alndust yearns to merge with others of its ilk. Part of my duties as the Shul'ka is to make sure to keep the number of large manifestations low so they do not overwhelm the Den.

It is easiest to do that while they are small.

Take this spear. You may use it to shatter the smaller creatures down to... trivial sizes.

title: Herding Manifestations
description:
We are preparing to fight a giant manifestation. Let me emphasize this again. We must fight just one.

If we fight two at a time, we would have no chance of survival. Place these wards around the rift to draw the others away. They will not last long... but we will need every advantage we can gain.

title: The Greater They Aln
description:
We have one chance at this only.

I pray we have prepared enough.

title: In Search of the Problem
description:
We did what Hagar asked us to do. The next step will be to test this on something--or someone--afflicted with the Lightbloom.

It may be difficult to find the Lightbloom in enough quantities in Harandar, but I believe we will find what we need at Har'alnor. I will meet you there!

title: The Missing Rootwarden
description:
Something keeps troubling me about this, {name}. Where is the Rootwarden?

Ruia has a solemn duty to watch over the World Tree and the land around it. He should be here, with his people in need. So where could he be when they are in crisis? What is he thinking?

I am sure that we can find clues to where he has gone if we search his town. Perhaps we can learn more about his state of mind along the way.

title: Can We Heal This?
description:
The rutaani have escaped their nearby villages to Har'alnor. The guards have told me that some of them have been afflicted with the Lightbloom, yet have not succumbed to its effects.

This may be our best opportunity to test the Alndust's powers. Time is of the essence!

title: Alndust in Right Hands
description:
We can now go to the roots of Shaladrassil and stop the Lightbloom spreading even further into Harandar.

We have enough Alndust to inoculate any haranir who are willing to risk their lives to follow us into battle. Find them, {name}, and give them the Alndust.

title: Righteous Pruning
description:
This is a threat that could destroy all of Harandar if left unchecked. That's why we will pull this out at the root.

The ruutani who did not have the time to escape this place must be fully corrupted by now. Unfortunate, but it makes our plan of attack simple. Their leaders must be spreading the Lightbloom. We must strike them down.

title: Our Beloved, Returned
description:
The haranir had a presence by the roots of Shaladrassil, as a sign of goodwill with the cultures that live within our home. When the guards retreated, they left some of our treasured mementos and artifacts.

Since the spread of the Lightbloom has been greater than I ever expected, I do not know when we'll be able to come back. Let's find those artifacts in the Blinding Bloom and bring them home.

title: At the Root
description: The growths that help them spread the Lightbloom so quickly should be vulnerable after your attack from the air, but we still need to destroy them. Return to the Blinding Bloom and uproot the spreaders before they can recover from our attack.

title: Into the Lightbloom
description:
We've gathered the haranir we have available and distributed the Alndust. We have everything we need, but they need to be able to safely enter the area. Scouts have told us that the creatures spreading the Lightbloom are protected by a shield. We must stop them. We will also need to thin their numbers.

Get on the petalwing, {name}, and let's clear the way.

title: Tell the People What You Have Seen
description:
We may have stemmed the malignant growth, but we have so many more questions than answers. How did this happen? How did this grow so much? How did Ruia not understand the extent of the calamity?

{name}, we must return to the Cradle immediately. The elders must know what happened today.

title: Loa Disturbance
description:
Now dat we are tryin' to contact all de loa, my mind turns to Kulzi, de loa of medicine--and the madness that surrounds his shrine.

His loa speaker, Sij'ta, was a good friend. He healed many war parties of their injuries. But somethin' happened at Kulzi's shrine, and it is full of angry spirits.

Dey attack anybody dat tries to get through to dem, and de loa is silent.

Perhaps you can finally reach out to de loa and find his speaker!

title: Curse Cleanse
description:
Warlord Akutu could die at any moment, and you brought no pango liver? Bah!

Each day I scream at Kulzi to tell me how to cure him. I say, "you force me to use de forbidden liver ritual!" But de loa says nothin'.

<Loa Speaker Sij'ta's eyes seem angry but also afraid.>

If he won't tell us what is wrong, then we will have to use de pango liver ritual. It is rumored to be a potent but forbidden ritual.

Get de largest pango liver you can find. There is no time to waste.

title: Alternative Medicine
description:
I can't leave de warlord's side. I swore an oath to Zul'jin himself to save de Amani warlords at any cost. If I leave, he is sure to die. He is already cold as death! If he weren't movin' and talkin', I'd swear he was already dead.

There are things we can do without de loa's diagnosis. Bring me de bloodweed branches dat grow below de shrine. They're very important to de healin' rituals we can still do.

Gather me a heap of dem.

title: Demands Unmet
description:
Good, we have de branches. We will use them! Above us are de altars where we bring de injured to Kulzi for healin'...

<Sij'ta looks very disoriented.>

Wait. I already did this. I brought Akutu up there, an' laid him upon one of de altars. Dat is where he held my hand, made me swear to...

What? Why can't I remember?

You--you're here for a reason. Go get answers. If de loa appears, tell him he's gotta fix dis--or else!

title: Required Repentance
description:
I remember dis staff. And I remember dat day.

We are... dead, then.

<Sij'ta seems to take in the realization with a sort of relief.>

Akutu was near death from a battle in de city. He had some serious wounds. Kulzi said he could not heal dem. Akutu would not accept dis. He wanted me to use de staff--a hexlord staff--on Kulzi to force him to heal.

And then... I think I broke it.

Show de other spirits here dis staff. Maybe we can piece together what happened from their memories.

title: Denial Denied
description:
So, I broke de staff. And everybody died.

Maybe dis is my mistake. Sometimes it's too hard to say we made a mistake. And den we made even more mistakes. We in this deep, now.

While you were talkin' to the others, I tried to get through to Akutu that we're dead. But he's not listenin'. All he does is blame others. Maybe I deserve it.

Show Warlord Akutu de staff. Den take him to Kulzi. We need to try to talk to de loa.

De pieces of dis puzzle are fallin' into place.

title: The Medicine Loa's Shrine
description:
Everyone has their own path toward understanding, even Akutu--though he has yet to set foot on it, unfortunately.

My shrine was once a place of discovery and understanding for the Amani. Please take word to them that I welcome their return.

The ghosts will calm in time.

title: Down a Peg
description:
There seems to be a problem student at the academy.

I have heard reports that a student, Tytalor Goldenblade, is stirring up trouble in Sunstrider Isle. I'd go myself but I have my hands full running practice here already.

Could you go check on him and... educate him of his misgivings?

title: Spellbook Scuffle
description:
You there!

I've heard whispers of your so-called "heroics," but I'm convinced they must be false. For if they were true, it would mean you would have achieved more than I, Tytalor Goldenblade--the finest, smartest, and most handsome mage at this academy.

Therefore, I challenge you to prove your worth, if you are not a coward. The arcane tomes will make a fine warm-up for you... if you survive. Tear out their pages and prove you're not a complete fraud.

title: Training Arc
description:
Hah! You actually managed to defeat the spell books? I suppose even a blind squirrel finds a nut once in a while.

Don't get too proud of yourself though! My followers ahead won't be so easily dismantled by your flailing. When you inevitably fail, I'll be sure to remember this as the moment your arrogance got the better of you.

title: Academic Aspirations
description: How charming. You've actually made it this far thinking you've proven something. Let me make it clear--nothing you've done compares to my prowess. You've struggled through the simplest of challenges, but now you'll face me. Meet me at the top of this tower and I'll show you exactly how far beneath me you really are.

title: The Devouring Citadel
description:
I must return to Silvermoon, but Turalyon has commanded all able-bodied champions of the Light to meet him at the muster point outside the Voidspire.

The forces of the Devouring Host will fall, {class}.

One way, or the other.

title: Clarity of Purpose
description:
The ethereals on the nearby plateau are forcing us to ground our heavier weapons. We'll need to neutralize their turrets before we can begin our assault on the Voidspire.

Speak to Umbric and he'll help you gain a tactical understanding of the battlefield.

title: Master of Mayhem
description:
{name}, ethereals have been striking out from the Ethereum Overcroft, harassing our Vanguard forces where they find them. If you can bring the fight to them, it'll take the pressure off our muster.

Can you do that for us?

title: Powerless
description:
Their very life force is being siphoned to power the ethereals' guns, {name}! This cannot be allowed to continue.

Head into the Ethereum Overcroft and free my fellow void elves from the ethereals' clutches. With their freedom, the turrets will have no power, and Turalyon will be able to launch his warframes.

title: Seek out Arator
description: I see that my son is spending his time with the wounded. Do a father a simple favor and see if he could use your assistance.

title: Two Tons of Metal and Holy Fire
description:
Finally, the might of our warframes can be brought to bear.

With the Overcroft's air batteries silenced, our powerful warframes will be able to perform strafing runs along the spire ramparts, softening the opposition to our siege.

If you'd like, you may have the honor of piloting one of these warframes for the glory of the Light!

title: The Memory Remains
description:
Our Vanguard forces have been harrying the Devouring Host, probing for weaknesses and gathering intelligence.

Unfortunately, nearly all of them have succumbed to the Host. We are too late to help those who have fallen, but perhaps we can gather something to remember them by.

Will you do this, {name}? Recover mementos of the fallen so they can be returned to those they fought to protect.

title: Aegis of the Redeemer
description:
We're more than just an army, {name}, even if my father doesn't see that.

We're a fellowship, joined by a desire to protect those that matter to us, and those that don't have a protector of their own.

And now, that protection extends to those wounded on the battlefield.

title: The People's Champion
description: I think we've done all we can for the moment, {name}. Let's return to the muster point.

title: The Night Before
description:
Vicious voidbeasts have been snatching our officers, {name}. I've been told that you saw one from afar, through Umbric's void scryer.

Those beasts are steeped in void energy, using their instinctual mastery of teleportation to strike at our most vulnerable before melting back into the shadows.

Alleria has gone after them, and sent a request for another pair of eyes. You can find her at a campsite at the mouth of the gorge to the east. I'll mark it on your map.

title: The Patient Hunter
description:
These void beasts are elusive, able to open temporary void portals that they can use to move short distances instantaneously.

They may be beneath Turalyon's notice, but they're having devastating effects on the Vanguard's morale.

Between the two of us, I'm confident we can outmaneuver them.

title: Killing Blow
description:
I heard about these beasts, but I thought it was a rumor. Void creatures appearing out of void portals, kidnapping and eating our officers... sounds like a load of bunk. I never thought it'd happen to me.

There was a big one that got me, but he ran off. Please, let's get out of here before it comes back!

title: Nothing Stands Forever
description:
With the Patriarch defeated, these cunning creatures should no longer pose a serious threat.

Return to Turalyon and report on our success. I will join you all shortly.

title: Second Time's a Choice
description:
A little lesson. Trying to fool me twice is a grave error.

My dim-witted pupil has yet to return from their rendezvous with their acquaintance. Normally I would not bat an eye, but he has a journal of mine. I want that journal back.

Would you be a dear and remind him?

title: Reenact the Crime
description:
Ah, you there! You appear to have the wisdom to assist me, unlike a certain bow-wielding simpleton who thinks sniffing dirt will solve our problem.

We've accidentally misplaced a very important journal--the sort my mentor would transmogrify me to have lost!

Please use my arcane timepiece to summon echoes of the past and investigate these moments. That should allow us to find my journal properly, without indulging Vardis's primitive methods.

title: Tracking the Trail
description:
While a certain wyrm-brained mage wastes time with his inept magic, we'll actually solve this case.

The local wildlife has likely eaten the journal. Time to gut them and see if we can recover it.

If the journal's inside, we'll find it... unlike someone who couldn't track his own insipid ego if it were carved into the ground.

title: Caught Red-Handed
description:
A dog, of all creatures? This is the thief we've been chasing?

And now we must save it from a lynx? Truly, this day has taken a most... irritating turn.

No matter, we shall retrieve the journal... though I do wonder if this canine truly understands the gravity of its actions.

title: Thief at Bark
description:
<Luma stands in front of you, ears perked, thankful for your help.

She lets out a short bark, tail wagging once before trotting a few steps forward, then pausing.

Glancing back, Luma waits expectantly. Her body language pleads, "Come on, follow me!">

title: Smuggler Showdown
description:
The naga acted out of desperation. Tideborne's crew, however, is driven by arrogance. They sit smugly aboard their ship, convinced of their invulnerability and counting their ill-gotten wealth. It is time we put an end to that.

Captain Bloodwake shall be made to answer for every crate, every transaction, and every transgression. Are you prepared to extract the truth?

title: Cargo Conspiracy
description:
The Sunwell's radiance may shine, but its Light has not purged the filth that festers within this city. Smugglers scuttle through the streets like vermin, preying upon the desperate and enriching the spineless.

Yet, there are whispers of something fouler still--trade in wares so vile that even the Venture Company would recoil. I require someone to spy on each shipping company and find out which cloaks a smuggling ring.

Will you stand with me, or will you turn away as the rot spreads?

title: Warranted Search
description:
Tideborne Freight, is it? A name far too polished, like an ornate blade designed to distract from the blood it has shed. Smuggling through the port... but I suspect this venture involves something far more perilous than we think.

We must act with haste before they set sail and vanish into the mists. Keep your weapons honed and your senses sharper. Make them understand that crossing me was a grave miscalculation.

title: Supplier Surveillance
description:
This note all but drips with guilt, but mere words alone will not bring down Tideborne Freight. I require undeniable proof--evidence so damning that not even their well-bribed magistrates can turn a blind eye. That is where you come in.

Take this arcane recorder and make your way to the docks. Document their shipments, their crew--anything that reeks of smuggling.

A single image can speak volumes, and I intend to make every word count.

title: Below the Brine
description:
Discarding crates into the ocean? How utterly desperate.

Whatever they seek to conceal must be damning--sufficient enough to unravel their entire operation, should we recover it.

Take a deep breath and retrieve it before the current sweeps it away. Every thread we pull unravels their scheme further. Soon there will be nothing left but the ruin of their own making.

title: Dead to Rights
description:
Tideborne's decision to discard crates into the ocean was no mere error. It was a calculated delivery.

Pursue them, and ensure the naga are reminded of the grave consequences they face when they dare to cross us.

title: Cargo Collateral
description:
If the naga manage to siphon the remaining arcane energy from those unstable arcana, they will gain the very power they seek.

Destroy, incinerate, shatter them--do whatever is necessary. Ensure not a single shard remains for the naga or Tideborne to exploit. I do not wish to see even the faintest trace of arcane energy linger.

title: Harandar
description:
With the Devouring Host occupying much of our attention and time, we leave our backs exposed to other threats that wait in the wings. This Lightbloom that has afflicted Eversong grows worse by the day. If it gets out of control, then we might find ourselves fighting a two-front conflict--or worse, having our home consumed completely while we busy ourselves with the Void.

The strange woman who you found poking about the Lightbloom... You seem to be familiar with her. Seek her out. She may have answers.

title: The Root Cause
description:
The rutaani who attacked Silvermoon come from my homeland, Harandar. This Lightbloom infection is new, however.

The time has come for me to return to my people. I will face their judgement, and explain my actions.

If the Lightbloom has spread, I must warn my people of the danger. If you're willing to join me, I will take you and your insistent friend through the rootways to Harandar.

Let me know when you are ready to go.

title: To Har'athir
description:
All of the world's roots grow toward our homeland. The ones above you are mighty, but new--born of a fledgling World Tree you call Amirdrassil.

As far as World Trees go, it is a strange one. But its nature matters less than its reach. Any roots that seek to protect our world from harm are worth protecting.

Let us follow the road down to Har'athir and check in with the Rootwarden. We will ask if he's noticed signs of Lightbloom yet.

title: The Rift and the Den
description:
As I suspected, the elders are quite upset with my actions. Now that I have seen the children and Ku'paal to safety, I am ready to face the Council.

I will do what I can to make them see that the Lightbloom is a threat, and to enable your ability to move freely through Harandar.

Petalvane will carry you safely to the Den. Let us make haste.

title: The Den of Echoes
description:
Greetings, stranger. I am Elder Hagar. You have been granted permission to stay, as long as you pass through the trials of the Den of Echoes. 

Aln'hara will determine your fate, and you will learn about our people and customs.

Let us meet at the caves.

title: Koozat's Trample
description: Koozat is usually very docile. This isn't like him at all. Please, find out what happened.

title: Echoes and Memories
description:
The history of the haranir has been recorded for tens of thousands of years in the caves within. The memories of our people are painted on the stone, that we might never forget where we came from and what our purpose is.

You are the first outsiders to ever witness the Den of Echoes. When you touch the paintings, our ancestors will share our stories and struggles.

Zur'ashar Kassameh awaits you within. He will guide you and Halduron on your journeys. I wish you luck.

title: Echo of the Hunt
description:
Haranir are fierce and ferocious--cunning and canny. In time, we became the most feared beasts upon the mountain.

A great many wise and powerful beasts roamed the hills of our old mountain. But in time, we came to master them all.

Today, you shall hunt as we once did.

title: Echo of the Call
description:
When the haranir began to grow restless, they looked to new sources of purpose. Some cast their gazes upward, toward the stars and the moon. Others, such as the Hara'ti, heard a call from deep within the world.

The moment the first of the Hara'ti heard the Song echoing from the roots, we knew that we had found a new purpose. We would seek out the source of the Song, and discover the truth of it for ourselves.

But it would be a difficult road. Strange stone monsters sought to block our path.

title: Down the Rootways
description:
You have gained a glimpse into our history. There is one trial yet remaining.

If you survive, the goddess's blessing will be upon you, and the Council will not be able to deny your presence here.

Descend now into the earth and follow in our ancestors' footsteps.

title: A Hut in Har'mara
description: The others have gathered downstairs. Come. Orweyna is eager to see that you are in one piece.

title: Tending to Har'mara
description:
Har'mara has long been a refuge for those who have removed themselves from the conflicts that often arise between the rutaani and the fungarians. 

Here, they learn how to live with one another. They work, play, and live together--balanced.

You have learned much about the past of the haranir, including our deepest shame. Now you will see our current work: harmony.

title: The Council Assembles
description: Guards collected Orweyna and took her to get ready to meet the Council. When you are ready, we should attend the gathering.

title: To Sow the Seed
description:
Eonka's seed from Rift of Aln is the clue we were looking for. The inhabitants and plants there seem immune to the Lightbloom!

We must investigate the rift for ourselves. Let us return to the Den and plan our next moves.

title: Burning Bitterblooms
description:
Lightbloom-infested rutaani are working to spread the contamination through the village. This plant showed significant signs of the disease.

Destroy any of the plants they've come into contact with. We must contain this!

title: Halting Harm in Har'mara
description:
Lightbloom-infected rutaani are attacking the village! Rizam and Teetem both succumbed to the infection. It looks like Eonka hasn't been affected, but we must handle the others.

I will stay here and keep an eye on her. I need you to do what you can to protect Har'mara. Take out Rizam and Teetem as well.

title: Culling the Spread
description:
Powdercap said that the visiting travelers were the first to show signs of the Lightbloom infection--right after you questioned them!

Sadly, it seems Koozat has also fallen to the sickness. Take out the merchants and see to Koozat as well. He's far too dangerous a creature to be trampling around here.

title: Seeds of the Rift
description:
The situation is stabilizing, but let's make sure we're in the clear. I will see how Hagar is faring.

Check on Eonka again and be sure that she's not showing any signs of Lightbloom. She is on an upper level in Hagar's Hut.

title: The Traveling Flowers
description:
Orweyna mentioned that there are rutaani who are visiting from other villages. This is an opportunity for us to get to know what they're like when they're not Light-crazed.

I will stay here to speak with Meeka. You should speak with the visiting rutaani to determine whether the Lightbloom has reached their homes.

title: The Frenzied March
description:
Halduron's news is dire. If the Lightbloom is frenzying on the surface, then the danger has grown far more quickly than we were prepared to reckon with.

We must make for Eversong at once. I will stand against any threat to Aln'hara's creation, be it here or in the world above... and I will do it alone, if I must.

title: Cull and Burn
description:
Simply culling these creatures is not sufficient. If their bodies are left unattended, the Light will seep from their remains and spread through the soil to the surrounding foliage.

We must take more drastic measures. Gather the bones of the creatures you slay. If we burn them, perhaps we can prevent their further spread through the roots of this forest.

title: Stem the Tides
description:
When the Farstriders first discovered the Lightbloom, we thought it to be merely an aesthetic change--a small quirk of the Sunwell's influence. Would that we knew then what we know now.

These Light-frenzied rootways must lead all the way back to Harandar. If we don't close them, it won't matter how many of these damned things I bring down. Close the rootways and we might have a fighting chance.

title: Expeditious Retreat
description:
I have a way to stop the Lightbloom from spreading further through the forest, but it isn't exactly a delicate touch. Normally, I'd never dream of resorting to a controlled burn in such a healthy portion of Eversong... but the frenzy is spreading too quickly, and we are running out of options.

I've sounded the retreat to my Farstriders, but some of them haven't responded. I need you to find any rangers in particularly sticky situations, and lend your aid in securing their escape.

title: A Last Resort
description:
With our ground forces pulled back, our position will be overwhelmed in a matter of minutes. It's time to execute our gambit.

Rommath and the Magisters have provided us a mana gem that can be used to overload Lash'ra's runestones. Take it, and infuse the stones with the mana contained therein. That will set the stage for the Magisters' ritual: to detonate the runestones and burn the entirety of Lash'ra to the ground.

We just have to hope the Lightbloom cannot survive in an inferno.

title: Survive
description:
Our retreat to safer ground has not gone unnoticed. The rutaani mark us at every turn, and now they mean to cross the river.

We have to buy Rommath and the Magisters as much time as we can. If we fail here, the whole of Eversong could be lost.

Stand tall. Do not falter. And do your best not to die.

title: The Gift of Aln'hara
description:
Ruia has always been a zealous advocate, but this behavior is beyond the pale. The Lightbloom must be affecting him, driving him to these dangerous lengths.

But we do not have to be like him. This forest need not fall to the bloom like Shaladrassil's roots did.

The Hara'ti brought enough Alndust to bolster our allies against corruption, and prevent the frenzy from spreading further. Together, you and I shall spread Aln'hara's gift across the forest. This frenzy shall advance no further.

title: Light Finds a Way
description:
I am an expert in the fauna of Eversong, and I can say with certainty that the vast majority of the wildlife here tend to be of fairly average size.

But these Light-frenzied saptors are decidedly, unequivocally not. One bite from those thorny jaws could claim the lives of half a dozen Farstriders, if they are caught unawares.

My rangers have their quivers full culling the chaff alongside the haranir. See to it that those towering tyrannosaptors don't catch them unawares.

title: Quelling the Frenzy
description:
The day is nearly won. The sin'dorei cull every new sprout that blooms, and the Hara'ti ward the surrounding wilds against the frenzy's influence. All that yet remains is to remove this lightbound serpent's misguided head.

Rootwarden Ruia has opposed our every action and shouted down our every word. But he is still a Rootwarden, and one with the power of a frenzying Lightbloom host at his beck and call.

He is strong. But he does not walk with Aln'hara.

It is time to end this.

title: Rise of the Haranir
description:
It is done. The frenzy is routed, the Rootwarden has fled, and the sin'dorei have reclaimed control of their forests.

I can scarcely believe it, but... we succeeded, {name}. By Aln'hara's grace, we found a path forward.

Now Halduron's people invite us to their den. I am not fond of your surface-cities, but...

You have aided me so many times, my friend. It is time that the Hara'ti reached out hands of our own.

title: Favor of the Court
description:
Ah, {name}, my favorite {class}! I am delighted to see you in attendance once more.

You have quite the natural talent for these sorts of affairs. The nobles are pleased with your presence and your favor has been swaying their coffers.

Let us not stop a good thing, shall we? Help me choose an ally of our representatives to invite to the Haven this week. I'm ever curious to see the drama you will stir up.

As my personal guest of honor, your favor will be seen as an extension of my favor.

title: Hitting the Hammer
description:
The Twilight Hammer is dirtying up the ruins of Zeb'tala. Clean up the area and bring back some of their personal tokens. One never knows what curiosities may appeal to those vying for relevancy.

Those mindless cultists may stomp around and act tough, but you will make quick work of them.

title: Hold the Watch
description:
There is still a lot of friction on the border with Zul'Aman, but we must hold the watch on all fronts.

Check in with Bloodguard Nelric at the Thalassian Pass. It is rumored to be one of the most uneventful posts. I am certain he would appreciate a reprieve, if only for a few moments.

Stay alert, {name}!

title: Naga Blades
description:
It seems the locals currently find great appeal in naga blades--the brinier the better. On our southwestern shore, you will find the Daggerspine naga.

They have these grungy, barnacle-encrusted scimitars that would perfectly fit the bill.

Go grab a few of those. They could have value in some of our upcoming discussions.

title: Sunfire to the Blade
description:
I guess there is one benefit to being part of the courtly intrigue: one does hear things. We have this concoction of sunfire infused with the holy energy of the Blood Knights, courtesy of the court alchemists.

Take this down to the Sanctum of the Moon and test it on the Twilight intruders. I look forward to hearing the results in detail.

title: That's Our Wood
description:
It seems the lure of the lightwood continues to be a problem.

We may as well take advantage of the trolls' hard work! Collect what they have harvested. We can make use of it.

What we do not use for weapons, we can hand over to the carvers. The nobles will clamor over lightwood decor.

title: Lightbloom Dimmed
description:
All of this small talk makes my ears twitch. I would be so much more useful doing field work. For now, you get to do the fun part.

The creatures within the Lightbloom are getting a bit dense. I need you to get out there and lower the volume.

I am sure you'll run into many options while you are out there. Take a whack at a few for me.

title: Less Lawless
description:
The Tideborne are always running just over the legal line. They are an annoyance at best, but will push boundaries when given too much rope.

The ones in Sunsail Anchorage made the mistake of ripping off some nobles not too long ago. Maybe go smack them around a bit--remind them to respect their betters.
