title: Taxing the Tideborne
description:
Have you ever noticed, that sometimes there is benefit in letting someone think they are getting away with something? The Tideborne will continue to smuggle in plain sight, feeling clever at the taxes they're avoiding paying to Silvermoon.

This does add up, though, over time. When you have a moment, please head down to the harbor and relieve them of some of their back taxes.

title: Chop It Down
description:
We received word there's a creature from Lightbloom Ath'Ran making trouble on the western coast. It has caused a bit of drama.

Head over and resolve the issue.

title: Lovely Plumage
description:
The beautiful members of the Silvermoon Court do so love their colorful embellishments.

For this, we can stock up while doing a good deed! Head over to Thuron's Livery and pick up some of the brightly colored feathers shed from the hawkstriders in training.

Vaelith gets clean up service and we get the lovely plumage.

title: Sungrub Silk
description:
Oh, {name}, have you ever visited the Solaarian Sunbath? If not, it's highly recommended.

The sungrubs that wander nearby have a precious silk from which amazing fabric can be made. Solwin Brightstitch is reputed to be masterful in this, with his sartorial magic.

The raw materials are often sought out to have him create unique and personalized goods from scratch. Bring us some of that lovely silk. It will provide a soft cushion when faced with harder discussions.

title: Windrunner Memorabilia
description:
The nobles do so love the unusual. Take a look around Windrunner Village. There are sure to be some interesting mementos still tucked away there.

I will find an advantageous home for the pieces you bring back.

title: Put a Little Snap in Their Step
description:
Those naga are, as ever, being a beachfront nuisance. Do they not understand that is a prime location?

We may not be able to reliably drive them off, but a little mischief would not be untoward.

I found this tune that drives the snapdragons mad. They will just attack whoever is nearby. Take this and give it a shot!

title: Potted Lashers
description:
It seems some of the nobles are botany-curious where the Lightbloom is concerned. They seek the exotic to adorn their windowsills and lurk in the dangerous section of their greenhouses.

That's where you come in, {name}!

Go harvest those small and pernicious plants. Their bulbs will be the best for planting.

title: Brightwing Conservation
description:
The conservators at Brightwing Estate have a mischievous young dragonhawk named Cerul that likes to run away whenever their backs are turned. He doesn't go far, but it has become a game for him to get them to chase him.

Take this delicious treat with you and use it to lure him back home.

title: Ghostland Peppers
description:
The delicate blue ghostland peppers may not look like much, but they have a mean burn. Many nobles won't admit it, but they are quite popular in the noble games of "Truth or Challenge."

Please procure a few for me. I like to watch them sweat.

title: Sunset to Sea
description:
Naga have set up camp on the Sunset Strand. The sunset slugs that called it home are distraught and seem unsure what to do. This little tune will remind them of their home.

Go inspire them to seek the freedom of the open sea.

title: A Little Errand
description:
When conversations get tense, it can be challenging to keep words flowing in a productive way. At times like these, I find a little social lubrication is in order.

Swing over to Goldenmist Village and pick up a cask of Vintner's Golden. It's proven to have a euphoric side effect that can be quite helpful.

title: Light Snacks
description:
Classics are classic for a reason, darling. One simply cannot host a worthwhile soiree without a fine selection of mana for one's guests to indulge in.

The Magisters could bring some, but mana is best when freshly siphoned. The mana wyrms in southern Eversong have a decadent brightness to them that I find particularly lovely.

Be a dear and gather some for us.

title: Where Has the Wine Gone?
description:
I entrusted one of my apprentices with the delivery of several cases of particularly expensive wine. He was to bring them here from my private storage, but he has not yet arrived.

I should hate to ask you to retrieve him, but I cannot stress enough how expensive those bottles are.

Please see if you can locate him and my delivery. He should have been traveling from the north to arrive here.

title: Diminutive Demand
description:
Keeping preciously small animals as pets is a popular pastime among the nobility. The more unconventional the animal, often obtained through magic, the more of a conversation piece it is.

I have enchanted this wand with the appropriate shrinking spell. Use it on some of the colorful toads out near the ponds and bring them back here. The nobles will find them delightful.

Naturally, the toads may not take kindly to the enchantment. But I trust you can handle that, should it occur.

title: Pyrepetal Purposes
description:
Lord Saltheril, that old lynx, has put me in a bit of a bind.

He has requested a grand fireworks display to accompany tonight's dinner service, but we are dreadfully low on pyrepetal dust. It is an herb native to Eversong, highly flammable and quite dazzling when set aflame by pyromancy.

Could you help us replenish our stores? We mostly harvest it from the woods near Scintilas Falls.

title: What Horrible Magic
description:
Have you seen the encampments the naga have been constructing along our coast? Dreadful business.

Not only do they menace our ships and disrupt our trade routes, but they might pose a real threat to Eversong's inland settlements if allowed to continue operations unchecked.

Their arcanists are particularly dangerous. Dispose of them and our guests will be in your debt.

title: Fit for a Magister
description:
Battle prowess and magical skill are important, of course, but one cannot deny the power of appearances. A well-dressed army instills far more faith than one bedecked in rags.

Fortunately, a fine set of robes can be both flattering and functional. The mana silk produced at the North Sanctum can be woven into robes that deepen and intensify our magical reserves.

Be a dear and gather some for us, would you? My wardrobe could use a seasonal refreshment.

title: Power Clean
description:
The festivities have been wonderfully entertaining, which means the nobles have been leaving a bit of a mess around the estate. Lord Saltheril's servants have been keeping up with the cleaning, but the Magisters could lend a hand.

Enchanting a few brooms to sweep the pavilion should be an easy but impressive feat.

I was informed that an arcanist was employing the same tactic over at Farstrider Hold. Go retrieve some of his brooms and I will have them re-enchanted here.

title: A Bit of Bloodthistle
description:
You know, I bring Saltheril a lot of "imported" wine, but one of the first things I started supplying him with was bloodthistle. It's an herb that only grows around Eversong. It's got a smooth bite to it, sort of like mana. Nice and classy. His parties really go through the stuff.

I took a look and I don't think he has enough to last this whole thing. We should get some more.

The best stuff grows over by the West Sanctum. Grab as much as you can carry. Trust me--it'll go fast.

title: Bring the Ruckus
description:
This place is pretty fun so far, despite all the dry political talk. I know Saltheril is trying to get the nobles to buy up war bonds, but we could really use some more energy in here. If the nobles are all asleep, no one's buying anything.

Tell you what. Go invite some of my friends. They hang out under the bridge right outside of Silvermoon's gates.

If anyone can bring some energy to the soiree, it's us.

title: One Smuggler to Another
description:
The whole point of this deal is showing the nobles what we bring to the table, both for Silvermoon and for them personally.

These other groups bring strength, power, and really nice armor. What we bring is resources: getting the sin'dorei what they need, when they need it, with no questions asked.

I heard some folks over at Sunsail Anchorage have been trying out the same business, but they're putting gold in the wrong pockets. Swipe their stock and bring it here.

title: Dangerous Showpieces
description:
You'd be surprised at the money you can make off of weapons. No, not the kind you're meant to use--the kind you stick up on the wall and have conversations about.

Usually those weapons are expensive, or dangerous, or otherwise hard to get your hands on. The more rare and exclusive, the better.

The weapons those Twilight cultists are carrying around should sell pretty well, especially to buyers with edgier tastes. If you happen to come across any, I'll gladly take them off your hands.

title: Artisanal Replicas
description:
Don't let the austere demeanor fool you. Nobles are just as susceptible to flashy party favors as the rest of us.

I had my people bring over a batch of Felo'melorn replicas. They look great on display, and even better--they're enchanted. Swing them around and they leave a trail of fire.

I haven't sold an enchanted batch before, so I'd like to do some testing before I hand them out. Saltheril wouldn't be too happy if I burned a guest to a crisp.

Wanna take one of the replicas for a test run?

title: Shady Dealings
description:
I feel like I just got here and I'm already fresh out of half the poisons I brought.

What? Don't look at me like that. Nobles are some of my top customers for poisons. Quieter than magic and cleaner than blades.

I need to restock. The frogs way down in the south produce a poisonous slime that I can bottle without much fuss. Bring some of it to me and I'll get you a cut of the earnings.

title: Throw the Dice
description:
We've been making friends with some of the guests by teaching them how to play dice. It's a nice way to draw people out of the crowd and get them in a quieter space where you can talk privately.

I wasn't expecting so much interest, to be honest. We're running out of dice sets.

I can carve some more, but I'll need materials. Usually teeth or bone are good picks. Actually, the fangs from those naga along the coast would be perfect. Now that's a conversation starter!

title: We Need a Refill
description:
I talked to some of the servants earlier and it sounds like the casks are running low. That's a recipe for disaster at an event like this.

Fortunately, I've got a lot of contacts across Eversong who can help us restock.

I need to stay here and play politics, but could you head over to Tranquillien and grab a few bottles? I'll give you a list.

title: Begged, Borrowed, or Stolen
description:
The oldest trick in the book is to create a problem on purpose, solve it yourself, and look like the hero.

One of the nobles just got word that some prized valuable was stolen from their estate while they were occupied here. It just so happens I might have put one of their servants up to it.

Go talk to a man named Seridis over at the livery. He'll hand over the valuable to you, then you'll come back here and pretend you valiantly subdued the thief and retrieved the item.

Everyone's happy.

title: Illuminate the Space
description:
I share our dear Saltheril's love for vintage fixtures, but the lighting enchantment he is using is far too common for such an auspicious occasion.

My Magisters can create a far more stunning enchantment. Simply gather some fibers from the Lightbloom for me and I can have them broken down into spell reagents.

The effect will be marvelous.

title: Minding Our Duty
description:
While the court does demand our consideration, we cannot forget duties. Our guests will enjoy themselves more freely if they are assured of the solidity of Eversong's defenses.

The Magisters are tasked with maintaining the runestone wards throughout Eversong. Please survey them for us and let us know if any wards need our attention.

title: Fortify the Runestones: Magisters
description:
We placed a number of runestones throughout Eversong Woods, each one charged with protective arcane magic intended to ward off the Lightbloom.

While highly effective, the runestones require an immense amount of power to function. They must be regularly fed with latent arcana lest they weaken.

As Magisters, it is our duty to maintain the runestones--especially when the eyes of this Haven are upon us. Recharge their arcane power and defend them from those who would threaten us.

title: Fortify the Runestones: Blood Knights
description:
The Magisters placed a number of runestones throughout Eversong Woods, each one charged with protective arcane magic intended to ward off the Lightbloom.

Naturally, the runestones require an immense amount of power to function. They can be fed with latent arcana, but that power draws enemies seeking to claim it.

As Blood Knights, it is our duty to defend the runestones--especially when the eyes of this Haven are upon us. Recharge their arcane power and defend them from those who would threaten us.

title: Fortify the Runestones: Farstriders
description:
The Magisters placed a handful of runestones across Eversong, each one charged with arcane magic that wards off the Lightbloom.

As you might guess, the runestones require a huge amount of power to function. The Magisters feed them with latent arcana, but that kind of power draws a lot of attention. Bad attention.

As Farstriders, it's our duty to defend Eversong from those kinds of threats--especially when the Haven is looking at us. Go recharge the runestones and fight off whatever tries to stop you.

title: Fortify the Runestones: Shades of the Row
description:
Have you seen the runestones out in Eversong? Huge rocks full of arcane magic that ward off the Lightbloom.

I'm not the magic type, but I know an opportunity when I see one. Those runestones need a lot of power to work. If we help keep them charged, there are a lot of people who would owe us favors--and favors are even better than gold.

Here's the plan: I help you get some latent arcana, you drop it into the runestones, beat up whatever tries to stop you, then we bask in the accolades. Deal?

title: One Adventurous Hatchling
description:
One of my hatchlings was frightened off when that giant construct marched past the ranch. He bolted right through the fence and into the woods before I could stop him!

He's small, fast, and probably hiding somewhere nearby. Please find him before something else does, and return him to me.

title: A Roost-ed Development
description:
A comfortable hawkstrider is a happy hawkstrider. We don't expect them to stand all day. They need someplace cozy to roost!

There's a plant called golden sunleaf that sprouts just beyond the ridge. It makes the softest bedding--the hawkstriders love it. Could you gather some for me?

title: A Hungry Flock
description:
Some think that raising hawkstriders is an easy task, but it most certainly is not. Hawkstrider appetites tend to be notoriously fussy!

More often than not they'll turn their beaks up at anything I offer. But this time of year? Frog legs. They love the slimy little things. They can't get enough of them!

There's a pond just down the hill just swarming with the leggy little morsels. Could you gather some for me, please?

title: The Path of Mourning
description:
Hash'ura, could I ask for your aid?

My mother passed recently, and I'm de one responsible for performin' her death rituals. It's a lotta work for one person to do.

Most would get help from their family, but I don't have any left. Can you help me?

title: Somber Siblings
description:
Iz'ken left us many years ago, {name}. Preparin' may be tense, but at least we won't have to do it alone.

First, we need to collect de spores of de weepin' mushrooms. You can get dem from the mushrooms, or de boars.

I'll be usin' dem to create incense for de ritual.

Be careful, {name}--de boars might be unhappy when we take their food.

title: Strong Ties
description:
Next we need to collect sinew from giants. De sinew makes sure de bonfire is not gonna fall apart, and de act of risking ya life against a powerful foe honors de fallen.

I am a tested warrior, so do not worry for me. But please make sure Iz'ken does not come to harm from de giants.

title: Kindling Aplenty
description:
Next, we need to collect de logs for our bonfire. Kan'sha, de large tree at de end of de valley, was infected with poison from a stray arrow long ago.

She's quite tough. To survive, she sheds her branches regularly.

In a way, Kan'sha has become a regular part of our rituals. Her strong wood burns warm and hot--like Amani blood.

It is perfect for bonfires.

title: Reasonless Worship
description:
Ahead is an old shrine. Nobody knows anymore what we worshipped there or why, but it was a place my mother loved.

It would honor her if we put a stone from it on de pyre with her.

title: A Quiet Farewell
description:
Now we set up de bonfire to burn de body. Destroying the body means it cannot be used by de Vilebranch for evil and makes sure de spirit finds rest.

It still chills me how quiet she is now.

The tribe should be gatherin' down at de Funerary Coast.

title: Camp Stonewash
description:
You're not the first strange thing I've seen today.

<Scout Zel'kanra shakes her head.>

Saw a big, shelled creature along the path to Jan'Alor. Amaxi took a bunch of scouts and went after it.

They've been gone a while. Think you could check in on them?

title: I Have a Permit
description:
These Witherbark youths are telling us we can't stay here.

But I've been talking to one of their elders for months to be sure we'd have permission to set up camp and explore. I even have copies of all the letters!

Can you find Elder Ren'zen in Witherbark Village and show him his letter of approval?

title: Cuisine Connection
description:
Dis "seeker" has been askin' a lot of questions about de foods we eat.

It sounds like these tortollans like to eat poisonous foods. De Witherbark do as well. There are many foods we eat that could kill us if not treated properly.

Go collect zapgut eel stomachs and blisterskin kelp. I'd like to test dis turtle's tongue.

title: A Witherbark Story
description:
The Witherbark say they have a lot of stories to tell, but I've heard two and BOTH involved a sparring match!

Please, can you ask the Witherbark here for stories? My old bones can't take another reenactment!

title: Sightseeing Stegadon
description:
Wow, your armor is sooooo cool!

<Trishe stares, slack-jawed.>

Oh, have you seen my friend Steggy? She was here with me, but then there were all these pretty bugs and things, and she ran off after them, and I was too slow to follow her.

<Trishe pouts.>

I'm s'posed to watch Steggy. I'm response-able! And now you're response-able for finding her, okay?

title: Afterthought Artifacts
description:
This location is ideal for finding artifacts. Searching through the rubble at the bottom of that lake could reveal many treasures.

If you have a moment, you're welcome to take a look around yourself. Bring me anything you find and we'll see if it's valuable.

title: Dangerous Delicacies
description:
A Witherbark troll could survive eatin' each of these ingredients raw, but dey would need aid.

Together, de ingredients would be deadly. We're going to cook dem into one dish.

Don't worry. I'll show you how to take out enough poison so it tastes good AND has paralytic effects.

title: Unlikely Friends
description:
This old Witherbark pot you found is quite interesting.

I've made a series of notes on its construction and use, but now it's time we speak to Elder Ren'zen. The pot should be returned to him. And he can fill in the gaps in the pot's story!

Let's speak with him in the village.

title: Go Get Orweyna!
description:
Hey! Champ!

Look, I swear I'm glad to see ya. I'm just in a little bit of a diplomatic situation here.

Can you go get Orweyna and bring her back here? See if she'll talk this beast into letting me go?

I'm not an elekk plushie, for cryin' out loud.

title: The Home of the Haranir
description:
I am eager to show Monte my home, but I know it is very different from what he is used to.

Come with us as he meets my people.

title: Leave Your Mark
description:
One thing that is very important to the haranir is our past. Some may remove their ability to hear the Goddess, but we are all connected to who came before us.

You and Gazlowe are a part of our history now. You have earned the right to leave your mark here in the Den, for as long as the Den remains.

Go, touch the wall with your painted hand, and know that you touch the same stone as generations before you.

title: Missing Paladins
description:
Some of our brothers and sisters in the Light were responding to a request for help by the Magisters at the Sanctum of the Moon. Apparently the Twilight's Blade has been seen lurking in the area.

We expected to hear back from them awhile ago, but we've heard nothing.

Given the strength of the party that went, Salandria and I wanted some additional backup before we looked into the issue. Care to lend a hand?

title: Missing Paladins
description:
Some of our brothers and sisters in the Light--including my friend Dawnheart--were responding to a request for help by the Magisters at the Sanctum of the Moon. Apparently the Twilight's Blade has been seen lurking in the area.

We expected to hear back from them awhile ago, but we've heard nothing.

Given the strength of the party that went, Taelia and I wanted some additional backup before we looked into the issue. Care to lend a hand?

title: Twilight Missive
description:
We need to figure out what the Twilight's Blade is trying to do here. We also need to get rid of these cultists so the Magisters can get back to their work building runestones.

Take out as many cultists as you can. See if any of them are carrying information we can use.

title: Signs of the Struggle
description:
The Twilight's Blade are acting boldly in their takeover here. None of these Magisters have any information for us.

We'll hold this end of the bridge and protect any Magisters who escape. You get in there and look for signs of the missing paladins. Were they taken? Did they put up a fight, or were they ambushed? Are they still armed?

I have a bad feeling we are running out of time.

title: A Somber Sun
description:
<The corpse of this Blood Knight has been horribly mangled and tainted by Void magic. He put up a fight, but was unable to survive.

Salandria will want to know of this.>

title: Captured Information
description:
We need to find out where the paladins were taken. Easiest way to do that is ask... under a bit of duress.

Find an isolated target, beat them down, and then drag them to the hollow on the other side of the Sanctum. Taelia and I will get the civilians to safety and meet you there.

title: Interrogation
description: We need to know where Dawnheart and the others were taken. This one doesn't seem to want to talk... but we can fix that.

title: To the Ruins of Deatholme
description:
Thanks to our "friend" here, we know the missing paladins were taken to Deatholme. We have to find them before it's too late! 

Salandria and I will meet you there.

title: Executing the Blades
description:
Those monsters will pay for what they have done!

Take out any Twilight's Blade cultists you see. We'll scour them from Eversong for good!

title: Leave Ashes in Your Wake
description:
We need to make it clear to these cultists that they aren't welcome.

Tear down their banners, and destroy their books and supplies. Do whatever you can to wreck their camp!

title: Blessing of Freedom
description:
Our friends are being held in this area. I have no idea what state we'll find them in, but I pray they are at least alive.

Help me find and free our missing paladins in the Ruins of Deatholme.

title: Cutting off the Head
description: We'll crush their leader! For justice and the Light!

title: Tales of the Sky
description:
Wait a minute... You're no rutaani at all! Are you one of those "outsiders" that Saku and Orkon keep talking about?

Oh! I just had a great idea. Did you know that the Goddess used to have a pet? Rumor has it that when the Goddess disappeared, Aln'sharan was SO sad that he flew up into the highest roots of Harandar, never to be seen again. But every so often, he goes looking for food... And his scales fall as skyglass!

I wanna gather some. It's my dream to see Aln'sharan firsthand! Will you help me?

title: Ugh, Chores!
description:
Okay, so I may have a DIFFERENT thing that I'm supposed to be doing in the Cascades, too. It's my turn to gather saptor fronds for stew. It's such a pain. Not only are saptor fronds horribly bitter, but the saptors are barely even fun to hunt! They don't even make your blood flow that fast before they die.

But hey! You probably don't get to fight saptors as often as I do. Maybe while you're looking for skyglass, you could gather some up for me? You know... while you're in the area.

title: Carry On, Wayward Kuri
description:
Oh, that Kuri. She holds tightly to our ancient traditions and tales... perhaps too tightly.

There is a difference between holding reverence for our ancient ways and chasing them into dangerous caves. I fear she might bite off more than she can chew in her search for Aln'sharan.

Won't you go after her? She seems to trust you, now that she sees you as her co-conspirator in this quest. Please bring her home safely.

title: Skyglass Scavenging
description:
The adults find all sorts of stuff in the caves around here that we use back at the village. A lot of the critters that live in the caves like to gather skyglass, in particular. I guess they think it's shiny.

That's good for us, though! It means we can go and get a bunch of skyglass. Go in there and make sure the coast is clear, and then I'll come in and gather up as much as I can carry!

At this rate, we'll be able to summon Aln'sharan in no time.

title: The Legend of Aln'sharan
description:
This is it! We have all the skyglass we can carry.

The legends say that Aln'sharan cries crystal tears to mourn the missing Goddess. Maybe if we give the crystals back to him, he won't be so sad? Maybe then he'll appear for us!

Meet me back at the village. I think I know the perfect place to make our offering.

title: Be Grudge You
description:
You look... different! Ever be to Grudge Pit?

Grudge Pit is great place! Full of fighting and cheering and fungarians. Also decaying!

Boletus is always look for strong creatures to be kill in Grudge Pit. Or for making others be kill.

Are strong creature? Should go to Pit!

title: You Strong?
description:
You look strong.

Should test yourself in our arena.

See how you compare to strong fungarians.

title: A Few Fun Guys
description:
You are very strong, {name}.

Will teach fungarians to fight?

Much we could learn from you.

Many fungarians watch battle.

Ask if they want to be strong in Pit.

title: What Doesn't Kill Them
description:
Have team, now are ready to fight.

Take your team to empty area. Practice fight, and see what can do.

title: We Ready Now
description:
Thank you, {name}! We ready for Pit.

We trained to be strong. You hurt us. Now we more strong.

You tell Boletus we ready now.

title: The Most Important Thing
description:
We will be strong for the Pit!

But Boletus right. Another thing needed. Must have great name.

Best teams all have good name. Fungus Force. Spore Storm. Cap Clash. And champions, Glow Guard!

All good names.

We need amazing name. Best name. So will be best team.

You help choose?

title: To the Ring
description:
TeamName? Not bad. I like!

You and the TeamName head on down, and we say words.

You fight Spore Storm. If beat, fight Cap Clash.

If beat, fight Fungus Force.

Always-winners are Glow Guard. You won't beat.

No run out! Run out is big, big lose.

title: Tiny Heroes' Journeys
description:
You taught them. Why did they run out?

All fungarians were excited to see if a {race} could teach fungarians for be strong.

You try one more time, okay?

First do what need to get them ready.

title: Mushrooming Courage
description:
Brakko sorry that Brakko let {name} and TeamName down.

Brakko is much scared of sporegliders. Could sneak up at Brakko above cap, where cannot see. Could really hurt cap!

Very sorry, {name}. Brakko doesn't think Brakko can be tank for team.

Unless... {name} practice sporegliders with Brakko?

title: Mushrooming Resilience
description:
First Tuktuk runs, then Brakko runs. Cannot be the only one still fighting.

Ziny will fail by Ziny's self. Fight is too hard alone, will not be strong enough.

Do not want to fail. Cannot let PH_Teamname down!

Had choice, fail or run.

But run was also fail. Ziny very sorry to {name}!

Can practice, fight big and scary enemies together?

title: Mushrooming Confidence
description:
Tuktuk's poison is not good enough. Tuktuk had to run, before we lose.

Sorry if you lose! Tuktuk did not lose. Tuktuk quit. Quit is safe from lose.

Grudge Pit very, rrr... is so intense. Tuktuk cannot handle. Tuktuk will stay out where is safe.

Tuktuk will find place to relax. Away from Grudge Pit.

Tuktuk cannot lose if stay far away.

title: Not-Yet Defeated Champions
description:
{name}, the PHTeamname look stronger now! More ready. Hope they won't run again.

Next fight is against Glow Guard. Glow Guard always never lose.

Hope your training makes fight better, before TEAMNAME lose.

Will be fun for watch!

title: Charge of the Vanguard
description:
Turalyon reports the Vanguard of the Light is prepared to march on Xal'atath's stronghold.

Would that I could join you for the charge. I will keep my retainer of forces here to maintain the defense of the Sunwell and Silvermoon while you take the fight to our enemy. 

Muster in the Voidstorm, take the fight to the Voidspire, and bring an end to this great enemy. Good hunting.

title: The Voidspire
description:
At long last, we will ride to glory and dispense justice upon this vile creature who would levy the very Void itself against us. 

Today is the day Xal'atath's forces will face our judgement.

Now is the hour we strike back!

Come, let us make an end of this enemy.

title: The Broken Sky
description:
All those people. Our army. Our heroes. Our leaders.

Gone.

We are exposed here. Umbric's tether saved us, but we need to ensure we survive this. Report to Lor'themar and tell him what transpired in the Voidspire. I will get Umbric to safety and learn what I can here.

title: The Listener
description:
Now that you've learned about our legends, you've earned the notice of one of our elders. He wants to meet you.

His name is Aksem, and he knows things that the rest of us have never learned. History that remains hidden, even in the paintings.

Go and introduce yourself. He's expecting you.

title: In the Name of the Goddess
description:
The high priest Aksem has asked for you again. He said you are not the only one on a quest for knowledge about the deep history of Harandar.

Go to him. I believe he already has another guest who is also interested in learning this hidden history.

title: The Stolen Staff
description:
This time, I do not have a story for you. 

Instead, Aksem has asked that you meet with him to help recover the next of the relics. Go to speak with him, and he will tell you more of what you must do.

title: To the Blossoming Terrace
description:
When the haranir first arrived in Harandar, they brought with them a staff formed of the roots of a great tree. That tree has since gone away, but the roots remain.

It has been our duty to protect that staff and pass it on through the generations, but now it has been stolen.

Your task is to find it and return it to Zur'Ashar Kassameh.

Go into the mists below the Den and seek out the Shul'ka. They will guide you on your search.

title: That's Not Yours
description:
The rutaani in the Blossoming Terrace are crafty. They've trained their petalwings to search and grab anything shiny that rolls down from the Den.

And, I suspect, sometimes they grab things that haven't naturally fallen.

So we want to get those things back. That's where you come in.

Go into the terrace, and don't hesitate to use force to send a message. That's a language we all understand.

title: Nested Clues
description:
You're looking for a staff called the Root of the World. It's a powerful twist of woven roots, back from before the haranir traveled deep into the mountain.

It's irreplaceable, as Aksem knows. So if a rutaani petalwing stole it, that's embarrassing. The only way for everyone to save face is to get it back.

Search the nests of the bigger birds. They've been trained to look for more valuable items.

title: The Root of Things
description:
Looks like you didn't find any sign of the rootstaff among all the thiever's nests. That's a good thing, honestly.

But there's more to this test than just recovering stolen goods. Yes, it's a test. You're seeking secret knowledge. That means something here doesn't add up, and the real answer is waiting to be found.

So, come find me when you're ready to get that answer.

title: A Legend to Know
description:
I am entrusted with many secrets, and the benefit of that is not having to explain myself often.

You have recovered the Root of the World, as I asked. Now, take it to Zur'ashar Kassemeh, and learn more about it.

There will be more for you, later.

title: Once More Into the Rift
description:
Aksem has asked for you again. He says he's ready for you to undergo the final ritual for the knowledge he wants to teach you.

I admit, I'm envious, but I do not envy the trials you'll need to undergo in order to prepare for this knowledge.

Go speak to Aksem, and do as he says. Orweyna will be joining you.

title: Shrouded by Aln'hara
description:
As you know well by now, the creatures in the rift are infused with the essence of Aln'hara. What we need is that essence mixed with water.

Aksem wants us to collect water here and then infuse the essence of Aln'hara into it. I can only imagine what it might be used for.

title: Ritual Reinforcement
description:
You know the dangers of the Rift of Aln, but this is the best place to prepare for the kind of ritual Aksem wants to perform.

Go about the rift and place offerings to prepare yourself.

title: The Dreamrift: Malformed Mystery
description:
We have prepared ourselves as best we can for the next stage. Now, we must venture into the deepest part of the Rift of Aln.

Into the Dreamrift.

It's a place so dangerous that even the Shul'ka hesitate to go, but we must. Aksem needs us to collect the most powerful infusion of Aln there is.

Are you ready?

title: My Brother's Alive!
description:
En'liahn saved me from the Rift of Aln. He's my older brother. I know it was stupid to go there, but it wasn't my fault!

We all thought En'liahn was dead, but I saw him earlier at the wetland. When he saw me, he ran away.

<Chua looks sad.>

No one here believes me and now I can't go. Will you help me find him again?

title: The Healing Waters of Ahl'ua
description:
I'd hoped Chua hadn't seen me. Better he thinks I died. Instead, I'm self-exiling.

I was trapped in the Rift of Aln while saving him. Being in there for so long corrupted my connection to the goddess--if I don't sever it soon, it'll drive me mad, or worse.

The Shul'ka who saved me has agreed to mentor me through the process. He told me to come here and gather some things for a ritual to sever the connection.

I must collect the nearby healing waters. Will you help me fill this jug?

title: Only the Poisonous Parts
description:
My Shul'ka mentor instructed me to collect the toxic glands from the creatures here. He said they were crucial to the Ritual of Severing in order to break my connection with the goddess.

<En'liahn shakes his head in pain.>

I'm not looking forward to whatever this ritual is, but her disjointed song in my head is getting stronger and making it hard to focus.

title: Meeting My Mentor
description:
I'm in your debt, {name}. I didn't think I'd get any help. My mentor only provides guidance. He says that is the way of the Shul'ka.

Thanks to you, I have everything I need for the ritual. Will you continue on with me? I'm heading for the cliffside overlooking the Rift of Aln to the northeast.

title: The Path Will Reveal Itself
description:
The next step is to locate the ritual site. It's different for each potential Shul'ka.

Amarakk says that I must open myself to the power of the rift--that some part of myself tied to this place will guide the way.

All I can tell you is it hurts.

Are you still with me?

title: Doing Is Becoming
description:
If En'liahn is to succeed as a Shul'ka, he must learn to disrupt the manifestations in the rift. Sometimes that means closing small tears as they open. Most of the time, we simply slay the manifestations where we find them so they do not become so numerous as to threaten the rest of Harandar.

It is a never-ending responsibility each of us bears. It is why we are Shul'ka.

This, of course, assumes that he survives the ritual, or even survives to find the ritual site.

title: As Her Voice Goes Silent
description:
En'liahn is about to drink the mixture of the poison and healing waters to begin the ritual. He will be unconscious and fighting for his life.

The manifestations of the rift can sense this, and they are gathering.

{name}, you must place the wards and use their power to defend him while he severs his final connection to the goddess before her song drives him to madness.

title: The Final Rite
description:
Foolish. En'liahn is still weak from the ritual and not prepared.

He has run off to confront Morta'ka the Sundered Echo. This is the final rite of all who would become Shul'ka, but it is usually only attempted after years of preparation.

En'liahn will not survive, and in dying will only make Morta'ka more powerful. Will you save him, {name}?

title: From This Point Forward
description:
I don't know how to feel about losing my connection with Aln'hara. There's no more pain... I'm not going mad, but there's a hole inside. Does that make sense?

Will you rejoin us back up on the cliff?

title: The Nethersent
description:
<The draenei first tries to speak to you in his native tongue, adapting after you fail to respond.>

Men of Void take us to here. Come from sky. Fighting, killing. Want to go back. Help brothers and sisters.

Show you.

title: Universal Language
description:
To go home, water of life. Need.

<Tul'amar attempts to discern if you understand what she's talking about with a look. After a pause, she opts to stab her own fingertip with the end of her staff and then displays a bead of her blood to you.>

This. Of here. Is of fight. Of fight is Void. Void is power. With power, go home.

<She shows her fangs with an exaggerated snarl before pantomiming a claw swipe.>

Water of life. Of beast. Much. Yes?

title: Drenched In It
description:
<Tul'amar looks away from the blood-soaked and mortified Juras, and toward the portal as it flickers to life. She reads it with her powers of the arcane.>

Good. Blood is vio... violent? Violent is power. Need more. Now, brothers and sisters.

<She gestures to the blood in your possession, and then to her people. Many of them return wary looks your way.>

You can. I say. 

Then, go out. See of me.

title: Their Violent Ends
description:
Go home. How?

<She offers a dramatic flourish with her fingertips, producing some arcane sparks. Then, she makes an X with her index fingers.>

Of me? No. Of they.

<Tul'amar gestures to a group of voidwalkers up ahead. She then draws her thumb across her neck.>

Kill. And.

<She reaches into a pouch on her hip and retrieves a cracked stone of domanaar make. It looks like a spell reagent of some kind.>

As this. Need.

title: Across Worlds
description:
Man of Void. Have key. Need. Key to home.

<Tul'amar gestures down the way to the northwest. She grimaces back at her injured kin.>

Go back, we. Wait. Hope. For you.

title: Late Bloomers
description:
It is good to see you again, {name}. Orweyna told me that you might be able to help me with something.

A group of rutaani were due to arrive here earlier today, but they're late. They're coming up from Blooming Terrace with my mentor, Ru'gar, and I am worried they've had trouble on the road.

Will you scout along the road from here down to the Terrace? I'll come up from there and we can meet on the way if we find nothing.

title: Rutaani Rescue
description:
Over the hill north of here is a fungarian village, Fungal Cleft. I believe the missing rutaani have been taken there.

The fate that awaits them is a brutal one if we do not get them back quickly. Usually, fungarians favor hosts that are already dead for their reproductive purposes. The ones here are strangely vicious, however. I have seen some mushroom-covered animals and rutaani who are still moving around! It is unnatural.

Go to Fungal Cleft and see if you can rescue the missing travelers.

title: Back in the Bag
description:
What a mess this is. The sack you found was supposed to be filled with seeds. They're part of an important cultivation project that Ru'gar was working on.

It appears the fungarians made off with the seeds when they captured the rutaani.

While you're looking for the rutaani, see if you can recover the seeds that were stolen.

title: Caves of the Cleft
description:
Good work freeing the captured rutaani. With the seeds you recovered, I can try to carry on Ru'gar's work.

I did some scouting and gathering while you were keeping the fungarians distracted, and could use your help with another task. Meet me at the cave northeast of here.

title: Gathering Glowshrooms
description:
Ru'gar used to tell me about how he'd sneak into the nearby cave to harvest glowshrooms. They seemed to be integral to the development of the tla'tak seeds he was cultivating. Will you collect some of the mushrooms within?

Oh, and Ru'gar mentioned that the mushrooms have strange properties when harvested. I'm not sure what he meant by that, but maybe stay on your toes.

title: Gomphusta
description:
I would ask one last thing of you. I've heard quite a few of the fungarians mention their leader, Gomphusta. It seems he's behind the uptick in their violence and the reason they're stealing the seeds.

While you're in the cave, find him and take him out. Without Gomphusta pulling the strings, the other fungarians will be thrown into chaos and won't be able to organize ambushes as well.

title: A Born Killer
description:
Ah, {name}. I have so enjoyed our time together. I hunger for more. 

I wish you knew more of me than my predilection for the taste of mortal suffering. One of my greatest joys is in crafting Void power into various objects and structures. It is my passion--my art.

Here, I would like to show you. First, we will need power. You already know how to get it, of course: kill, kill, kill.

title: Artifice of Aggression
description:
While we domanaar are best known for leading armies and savoring battles, there is more to us. 

Many, like myself, enjoy using our mastery over the Void to shape it into the buildings and objects you see around you. 

How do you think Xal'atath learned to create that great tower of hers? She's a quick study. 

Come now, and I will show you my work.

title: Seek to Destroy
description:
It's no easy thing, intertwining Light and Void together. The result is unstable, so we must continue to temper it. 

We shall quench this blade in the blood of our enemies. 

Some domanaar attempt to reclaim and re-power Nexus-Point Xenas. That won't be good for your people. I'll assume you want to stop it. 

What is it you mortals say? Two birds, one stone?

title: Harvester of Savagery
description:
The blade hungers! The blood of our enemies will help stabilize and hone our nascent weapon. 

You'll need to drench the blade inside some corpses. So, you'll need to make corpses. 

<Decimus's smile grows broader.> 

You're good at that, right?

title: The Unforgiven
description:
Since even before our little misadventure, Nexus-Point Xenas has always been the weakest link. The beasts here are stubborn and powerful, always pushing deeper and deeper in. 

Some domanaar seek to reclaim the Nexus-Point. They are known to me: weaklings, fighting for scraps. Let's ensure they fail.

title: The Fiend That Failed
description:
The blade squirms. It is ready for its next transformation.

There is only the leader, Vortensiun, left. 

He is known to me. Once, we formed a pact together to defeat a rival. When the time came for him to betray me, he did so clumsily. 

I let him live, so I could savor the taste of his regret. 

I wonder how well that pain has marinated by now? Let's find out. When the blade is tempered in his essence, it shall grow new power.

title: Warmth for the Soul
description:
Enjoying the view? 

The blade is stronger now, but it is not yet complete. A weapon worthy of you deserves nothing less than perfection. 

But let us relax for a moment. I understand you mortals enjoy a... casual exchange of conversation to get to know each other better.

I am familiar with the concept, but am curious to see it in action. Indulge me?

title: The Wicked End
description:
The time has come. The final step. 

<Decimus pauses, savoring something.>

Let us create the greatest weapon your kind has ever seen.

title: The Fallen Wake
description:
<The shade flickers. You can barely make out its whisper.>

You... left me... to die...

<Its arm reaches toward you, then dissipates. Somehow, the presence is a familiar one. You feel that you know this spirit. 

You see more flickers in the distance. What are these shades?>

title: Shepherd of Fear
description:
Our journey continues... one final battlefield. There are vicious beasts aplenty here. Their Void shall fuel the blade even more.

And the blade's newfound power shall draw their Void into itself by mere proximity!

Though, this place is different. You may find things you don't expect.

Keep an eye out.

title: Den of Nalorakk: A Taste of Vengeance
description:
Zul'jan has taken de troops without my orders--again!

And de number of casualties... for what? For wood?

I want to take his tusks and...

<Zul'jarra growls but seems to center herself.>

Kinduru will need help with de soldiers' death rites. Help him prepare de herb mixture and pour it onto de fires.

My brother and I need to... talk.

title: Den of Nalorakk: Waking de Bear
description:
We must set aside what Zul'jan has done and focus on de final hash'ey trial: facin' Nalorakk, de Loa of War.

De Revantusk were once beloved of Nalorakk. Legend has it dat his loa speaker was so devoted, she followed him into his temple and his den with de most faithful of de Revantusk's warriors.

But now de temple is sealed. We cannot speak to de loa speaker, but maybe Lilaju knows how to get Nalorakk's attention.

She keeps watch for de Twilight's Blade at Torntusk Overlook with her guards.

title: Den of Nalorakk: Unforgiven
description:
Nalorakk is de Loa of War. His trial must be a battle!

There would be no greater death for an Amani than to die in combat against de Loa of War. No greater thrill than fightin' him.

Does it not make your blood pump faster and harder in your veins, hash'ura?

<Zul'jarra lets out a feral howl and laughs.>

Come. Meet me at Nalorakk's Prowl and we will sit at his fire and face our last trial--together.

title: Lost in Atal'Abasi
description:
Tolbani and Jehnira have been missin' for some time. Last I heard, dey were scoutin' Atal'Abasi for supplies.

That area is covered in murlocs. Too many of 'em is dangerous even for an Amani.

Find dem and make sure dey alright!

title: Vengeance for Tolbani
description:
Me and Tolbani had loaded de skiff with medicine, steel, and food to bring upriver to de forward camp.

It was meant to be a quiet run: no trouble, and no eyes on us.

But in de shallows, de murlocs came swarmin' out of de reeds all foam and teeth. Dey hit us hard--too fast to fight back proper. We tipped and lost de cargo. I barely made it to shore.

Tolbani didn't. I dragged his dart-riddled body here.

De murlocs must pay for what dey have done.

title: Reclaim the Goods
description:
While I was limping back from de river, I spotted a few of de murlocs dragging our food bags through de mud. They weren't working together either--kept snapping at each other, trying to snatch de best pieces for themselves.

I saw one of them stash a whole satchel behind a fallen tree. Another buried something under a pile of rocks. Greedy little monsters think they can steal from us and get away with it.

title: The Menace of Atal'Abasi
description:
Our boat didn't ground on its own.

We were fightin' de murlocs, and then suddenly we were in de air.

Dats when I saw him--one dat wasn't like de rest. Bigger. Stronger. When he attacked, de other murlocs scattered.

He picked up our boat like it weighed nothin' and hurled it on de rocks. I've never seen a murloc do somethin' like that.

We're gonna keep losin' people and supplies if dat muscle-bound murloc is watchin' de river.

title: Loa's Flame
description:
I will have to take what's left of de supplies back on foot.

De boat... it's no good now. De hull's cracked, and de frame's split. Won't carry another soul across de water.

But it'll still serve one final purpose.

It can be his pyre. We will burn it, de weapons, de armor he wore--everything with him.

Vengeance is done. Now we let de fire take him to de loa and to his rest.

title: Reports Returned
description:
I will go to Silvermoon but... I will return here to advise Zul'jarra.

I feel I let my past and my prejudices get in the way of my judgement regarding the Amani. You could say that being a hash'ura is a befitting atonement but... I feel drawn to stay, perhaps to help Zul'jarra in guiding her people back to the loa.

I will go to Silvermoon with you to bear the report--and Lor'themar's reaction.

If we go now, we may return to... party.

<A small smile cracks Liadrin's usually stoic face.>

title: The Conquered Heroes
description:
A domanaar was harrying our back lines. It was... different than the other void beasts--like it was playing rather than fighting. My partner, Callum, led a squad to hunt it down. They never came back.

Callum's an Arathi through and through. He wouldn't fall here--I know it.

Commander Lightblood refused to send a search party. He said there's a difference between personal and important. Well, the Vanguard can have their war, but I'm going after Cal. I know the route he took, but I can't search alone.

title: Flickering Light
description:
I feel traces of Light here--dim, scattered across the plain, but unmistakable. It's Callum. I know his Light like I know my own.

That domanaar is a monster: using others like toys, smashing them together until they break. We'll find them, {name}, and then I will turn that void beast to ash.

<Anais grips her spear.>

One thing at a time. Find the Light, and we find Callum.

title: Strung Along
description:
When the Light called, it called both of us. Why? So Callum could die on some void-scarred rock?

I can't think here. All I can picture is Callum bleeding out. Come, we'll return to camp. With his belongings, I should be able to find him. 

Then... I don't know. If we can't break the shadowgrafts, we'll subdue him and drag him back to Silvermoon if we must. I'm not losing him.

title: Bury Me Not
description:
I knew we'd never grow old together, but I thought we'd have more time.

<Anais coughs, blood pooling beneath her. Flecks of the Void shimmer in the spreading crimson.>

Don't worry. I'm not afraid. I promised myself I wouldn't go home without him... wouldn't spend a night in an empty bed. I'm just keeping that promise.

Don't let her have him. Take his tinder box. You'll know what to do.

title: The Blooming Lattice
description:
You're that stranger I've been hearing about, aren't you? I'm Su'meera, apprentice painter to Ja'kul. You heard of him? No? Maybe his work hasn't made it to your lands. That makes sense, I guess.

Anyhow, I heard you like helping people out. Well, a few important things went missing when Ja'kul was attacked and killed. I want them back.

Will you meet me near Blooming Lattice?

title: Purloining Petals
description:
A couple of days ago, a group of rutaani attacked and killed my mentor, Ja'kul. When they did, their stupid birds stole a few things from him. Those petals-for-brains will make off with anything shiny that isn't thorned down.

Will you recover Ja'kul's things for me? I'm a painter, not a fighter. I wouldn't last very long in there on my own.

title: Petal Bristles
description:
Ja'kul was out hunting petalwings when the rutaani came upon him and killed him. We use their feathers for some of our paintbrushes. They are especially useful in helping the young sprouts learn how to be more precise and delicate. 

While you're up there, will you bring me back some of their plumes?

title: Nipping the Buds
description:
The rutaani of Blooming Lattice were behind the attack on Ja'kul. They're led by a Prime Bloomwarden who's even more vicious. I hate them and they're all terrible!

How about you go prune the overgrowth and make the rest of us safer?

title: Behind the Falls
description:
The scroll you brought back was left behind for me by my mentor, Ja'kul. I didn't even have a chance to read it before it was stolen away! 

Ja'kul left directions in the scroll to a cave that's behind a waterfall in Blooming Lattice. I'm not sure what we'll find there, but I would really appreciate if you came along while I check it out. Will you meet me there?

title: Memories in Stone
description:
Ja'kul was killed before he could finish my training, but I would recognize his work anywhere! He must have painted this vision stone for me!

The rutaani will catch on pretty soon that we're up here, and I won't be able to defend myself. Will you keep watch while I journey into the memory he left me?

title: Prey: Magister Sunbreaker (Normal)
description:
Once a noble Magister of Silvermoon, sworn to protect Quel'Thalas at any cost, he has broken away from the order and now seeks vengeance on his own terms.

I see something of myself in him.

Kill it for me.

title: Prey: Magistrix Emberlash (Normal)
description:
Alas, you can only catch so many innocent bystanders in your fireballs before you find yourself ostracized from polite society. Emberlash was always more interested in flame than friends...

She has burned one too many mutual acquaintances. 

If she will not control her power, we will gladly take it for our own.

title: Prey: Senior Tinker Ozwold (Normal)
description:
Rumor has it that Ozwold single-handedly raised insurance premiums in Gnomeregan before their little exodus. Be that as it may, he's consistently taken to perfecting his gadgetry regardless of the cost to others.

His ambition is admirable, but his methods are irredeemable. 

Go and see that his inventing days come to an end.

title: Prey: L-N-0R the Recycler (Normal)
description:
Do you know why they call her the Recycler, {name}? I don't. And given her history of "disappearing" shop assistants, I'm not really inclined to ask her.

That being said, watch your step. I understand that she likes to incorporate her victims into her gadgetry. We wouldn't want you becoming a pocket watch or some such thing.

title: Prey: Mordril Shadowfell (Normal)
description:
The Void is a perfidious thing, like water. The wise keep it at a respectful distance, never dancing too close to the edge. The foolish dive in, and in so doing, drown.

Mordril is a danger to himself and everyone around him, so lost is he in his own obsession.

See to it that he no longer poses a threat to this realm.

title: Prey: Deliah Gloomsong (Normal)
description:
In a previous life, Deliah Gloomsong was a prodigious student--truly a marvel of ambition, talent, and unrelenting curiosity. 

It was the latter that got the best of her.

Put an end to her murderous spree.

title: Prey: Phaseblade Talasha (Normal)
description:
Ethereals... they make my skin crawl. Esoteric. Unknowable. I don't trust anything I can't look in the eyes while it grovels before me.

My scouts report that a particularly dangerous ethereal is active in a nearby region. We would do well to nip this in the bud while we still can.

title: Prey: Nexus-Edge Hadim (Normal)
description:
Our activities are drawing unwanted attention. I have heard whispers that a powerful ethereal assassin is on my... on our trail. It would be very difficult indeed to defend our city as twin piles of bloody ribbons.

Go out there and show this "assassin" the true art of the kill.

title: Prey: Jo'zolo the Breaker (Normal)
description:
Jo'zolo the Breaker is a troll who abides by his name. Long has he been the bane of our scouts, but no more! 

Kill him. Feed his anguish to the defenses of our beloved city.

title: Prey: Zadu, Fist of Nalorakk (Normal)
description:
Our neighbors produce some of the most skilled hunters in all of Azeroth. It is a shame they are so difficult to reason with. 
 
There is one named Zadu. Her anguish would make for a most excellent trophy...

title: Prey: The Talon of Jan'alai (Normal)
description:
There is a powerful troll shaman who calls himself the Talon of Jan'alai. Long has he confounded our city's Farstriders, sowing confusion and death in his wake.

See to it that he joins the spirits he so ardently venerates.

title: Prey: The Wing of Akil'zon (Normal)
description:
Do you smell that? There is death on the air. This can only mean one thing. The Wing of Akil'zon is near. Go out and slay her.

Oh, and be careful. She's responsible for more than one lost Farstrider patrol.

title: Prey: Ranger Swiftglade (Normal)
description:
It is a difficult thing, to hunt one of our own. Ranger Swiftglade is beyond redemption. His bloodlust could not be sated against our foes, and he has since turned his martial prowess upon his own people.

End his sadistic killing spree and put his anguish to better use.

title: Prey: Lieutenant Blazewing (Normal)
description:
I do not know what to make of Lieutenant Blazewing. Her entire patrol disappeared during a reconnaissance mission into Zul'Aman. She was the sole survivor, but she returned with a fiery bloodlust and a complete disregard for the lives of others. 

Whether this is possession or sedition I am unsure, but she must be dealt with.

title: Prey: Petyoll the Razorleaf (Normal)
description:
Reports are coming in of shredded Farstriders adorned with petals and berries--entire patrols found strung up by razor-vines. There are more gruesome details, but they chafe even my... refined sensibilities. 

Hunt whatever manner of creature is embarking on this horrific killing spree, and see to it that its anguish is added to our crystals.

title: Prey: Lamyne of the Undercroft (Normal)
description:
Do you garden, {name}? I've been known to dabble. It seems the plants in our realm are becoming somewhat overbearing these days, sprouting legs and arms and running around murdering our brave soldiers. 

Let's give them a bit of a pruning, shall we?

title: Prey: High Vindicator Vureem (Normal)
description:
High Vindicator Vureem is a victim of his own unyielding faith in the Light and the superiority it gives him. His crusade continues unabated and unresolved. He exists in a constant state of fury at the imperfectness of the world and those within it.

In the absence of total purity, Vureem sees only violence and the shadows of his own dogma. 

What say we put his worldview to the test?

title: Prey: Crusader Luxia Maxwell (Normal)
description:
We are not sure if Luxia Maxwell is a deserter or has simply decided to embark on her own personal crusade. Regardless, reports are coming in of her attacking both friend and foe alike, yelling aphorisms of purity and duty.

She has become a liability to the defense of Silvermoon. 

Go and harvest her anguish.

title: Prey: Praetor Singularis (Normal)
description:
The domanaar...

<Astalor shudders.>

I do not profess to know much about them, other than their proclivity for subterfuge, guile, and the absolute destruction of anything that stands before them. Praetor Singularis is no exception. They have been confounding our forces at every turn.

Go and put an end to their games.

title: Prey: Consul Nebulor (Normal)
description:
I have heard that there is one under Xal'atath's command who takes particular delight in dominating their foes. Whispers of a domanaar that taunts its victims with whispers and riddles before annihilating their mortal coil with void magic.
  
Consul Nebulor is their name.

Go and show them the limits of their power.

title: Prey: Executor Kaenius (Normal)
description:
The domanaar have a reputation for guile and cunning, but there are brutes among them: those who seek to dominate their victims through sheer power and force of will.

Executor Kaenius is one such domanaar.

They stalk the battlefield, singling out our strongest soldiers and viciously cutting them down.

Put an end to their rampage.

title: Prey: Imperator Enigmalia (Normal)
description:
Imperator Enigmalia is personally responsible for the deaths of at least a dozen Farstriders. I say "at least" because this particular domanaar makes it somewhat difficult to identify the bodies, so utterly destroyed are they.

Go and avenge our fallen soldiers.

title: Prey: Knight-Errant Bloodshatter (Normal)
description:
Death knights are unstable killing machines at the best of times. Knight-Errant Bloodshatter has forsaken any oath or promise that would ostensibly bind one of his kind to the bottom rungs of civilized society, and has gone completely rogue.
 
He is starting to become a liability. 

Recoup his anguish so that we can make the best of such an unfortunate situation.

title: Prey: Vylenna the Defector (Normal)
description:
Vylenna the Defector lives up to her name. After a recent engagement on the field of battle, she became so preoccupied with profaning the dead that she completely abandoned the duties ascribed to her.

Then her dereliction turned violent.

A veritable font of anguish if you ask me...

title: Prey: Lost Theldrin (Normal)
description:
Those of us who succumbed to our base desire for mana were slowly weeded out of civil society, if they survived at all. Theldrin is one such example, only it is a testament to his cunning and will to live that he has made it as long as he has.

He accepts no aid, choosing instead to stalk careless students and idle Magisters. His sole obsession is the mana that others possess...

See to it that he never harms another elf again.

title: Prey: Neydra the Starving (Normal)
description:
Poor Neydra... I knew her, in a previous life. She was full of vitality. Full of ambition. Unfortunately, she let that ambition get the best of her. She grew too enamored with the raw power of the arcane. She became resentful of all other things. Of all other people...

And now she is a murderous creature who beheads rats and attacks innocents in a desperate attempt to garner enough arcane energy to slack her unquenchable thirst.

It's funny, the paths life takes us...

title: Prey: Thornspeaker Edgath (Normal)
description:
This one presents a prickly problem indeed. Thornspeaker Edgath has made a name for himself by using his druidic powers not to preserve Azeroth, but to twist its strength both against his enemies and innocent bystanders alike. He has no regard for anything that is not a beast or growing in the ground.

It is time we clip this thorn.

title: Prey: Thorn-Witch Liset (Normal)
description:
Thorn-Witch Liset is a curse upon civilization and a blight upon the land itself. She twists the very trees and beasts, communing with the dark, evil things that live on the very edges of the natural realm.

I despise the natural realm.

title: Prey: Grothoz, the Burning Shadow (Normal)
description:
The Twilight's Blade are an embarrassing band of mediocre cultists and uninspired conspirators. Grothoz is the brains of a particularly stupid cabal that has been active in a nearby region. 

As his stupidity prevents him from using his whole brain, let us unlock his potential for him...

title: Prey: Dengzag, the Darkened Blaze (Normal)
description:
Do you know why the Twilight's Blade call Dengzag the "Darkened Blaze?" It is because they are stupid, small-minded, and utterly bereft of imagination. What a silly name indeed. 

I, however, am not small-minded. I have quite the prodigious mind, and it is currently intent on putting an end to Dengzag and whatever his tiny brain is up to. 

Get to it.

title: Prey: Magister Sunbreaker (Hard)
description:
Once a noble Magister of Silvermoon, sworn to protect Quel'Thalas at any cost, he has broken away from the order and now seeks vengeance on his own terms.

I see something of myself in him.

Kill it for me.

description:

description:
Our neighbors produce some of the most skilled hunters in all of Azeroth. It is a shame they are so difficult to reason with.

There is one named Zadu. Her anguish would make for a most excellent trophy...

title: Prey: The Talon of Jan'alai (Hard)
description:
There is a powerful troll shaman who calls himself the Talon of Jan'alai. Long has he confounded our city's Farstriders, sowing confusion and death in his wake.

See to it that he joins the spirits he so ardently venerates.

title: Prey: The Talon of Jan'alai (Nightmare)
description:
There is a powerful troll shaman who calls himself the Talon of Jan'alai. Long has he confounded our city's Farstriders, sowing confusion and death in his wake.

See to it that he joins the spirits he so ardently venerates.

title: Prey: The Wing of Akil'zon (Nightmare)
description:
Do you smell that? There is death on the air. This can only mean one thing. The Wing of Akil'zon is near. Go out and slay her.

Oh, and be careful. She's responsible for more than one lost Farstrider patrol.

title: Prey: Ranger Swiftglade (Hard)
description:
It is a difficult thing, to hunt one of our own. Ranger Swiftglade is beyond redemption. His bloodlust could not be sated against our foes, and he has since turned his martial prowess upon his own people.

End his sadistic killing spree and put his anguish to better use.

title: Prey: Ranger Swiftglade (Nightmare)
description:
It is a difficult thing, to hunt one of our own. Ranger Swiftglade is beyond redemption. His bloodlust could not be sated against our foes, and he has since turned his martial prowess upon his own people.

End his sadistic killing spree and put his anguish to better use.

title: Prey: Lieutenant Blazewing (Hard)
description:
I do not know what to make of Lieutenant Blazewing. Her entire patrol disappeared during a reconnaissance mission into Zul'Aman. She was the sole survivor, but she returned with a fiery bloodlust and a complete disregard for the lives of others. 

Whether this is possession or sedition I am unsure, but she must be dealt with.

title: Prey: Lieutenant Blazewing (Nightmare)
description:
I do not know what to make of Lieutenant Blazewing. Her entire patrol disappeared during a reconnaissance mission into Zul'Aman. She was the sole survivor, but she returned with a fiery bloodlust and a complete disregard for the lives of others. 

Whether this is possession or sedition I am unsure, but she must be dealt with.

title: Prey: Petyoll the Razorleaf (Hard)
description:
Reports are coming in of shredded Farstriders adorned with petals and berries--entire patrols found strung up by razor-vines. There are more gruesome details, but they chafe even my... refined sensibilities. 

Hunt whatever manner of creature is embarking on this horrific killing spree, and see to it that its anguish is added to our crystals.

title: Prey: Petyoll the Razorleaf (Nightmare)
description:
Reports are coming in of shredded Farstriders adorned with petals and berries--entire patrols found strung up by razor-vines. There are more gruesome details, but they chafe even my... refined sensibilities. 

Hunt whatever manner of creature is embarking on this horrific killing spree, and see to it that its anguish is added to our crystals.

title: Prey: Crusader Luxia Maxwell (Hard)
description:
We are not sure if Luxia Maxwell is a deserter or has simply decided to embark on her own personal crusade. Regardless, reports are coming in of her attacking both friend and foe alike, yelling aphorisms of purity and duty.

She has become a liability to the defense of Silvermoon. 

Go and harvest her anguish.

title: Prey: Lamyne of the Undercroft (Hard)
description:
Do you garden, {name}? I've been known to dabble. It seems the plants in our realm are becoming somewhat overbearing these days, sprouting legs and arms and running around murdering our brave soldiers. 

Let's give them a bit of a pruning, shall we?

title: Prey: Lamyne of the Undercroft (Nightmare)
description:
Do you garden, {name}? I've been known to dabble. It seems the plants in our realm are becoming somewhat overbearing these days, sprouting legs and arms and running around murdering our brave soldiers. 

Let's give them a bit of a pruning, shall we?

title: Prey: High Vindicator Vureem (Hard)
description:
High Vindicator Vureem is a victim of his own unyielding faith in the Light and the superiority it gives him. His crusade continues unabated and unresolved. He exists in a constant state of fury at the imperfectness of the world and those within it.

In the absence of total purity, Vureem sees only violence and the shadows of his own dogma. 

What say we put his worldview to the test?

title: Prey: Praetor Singularis (Hard)
description:
The domanaar...

<Astalor shudders.>

I do not profess to know much about them, other than their proclivity for subterfuge, guile, and the absolute destruction of anything that stands before them. Praetor Singularis is no exception. They have been confounding our forces at every turn.

Go and put an end to their games.

title: Prey: Consul Nebulor (Hard)
description:
I have heard that there is one under Xal'atath's command who takes particular delight in dominating their foes. Whispers of a domanaar that taunts its victims with whispers and riddles before annihilating their mortal coil with void magic. 
 
Consul Nebulor is their name.

Go and show them the limits of their power.

title: Prey: Executor Kaenius (Hard)
description:
The domanaar have a reputation for guile and cunning, but there are brutes among them: those who seek to dominate their victims through sheer power and force of will.

Executor Kaenius is one such domanaar.

They stalk the battlefield, singling out our strongest soldiers and viciously cutting them down.

Put an end to their rampage.

title: Prey: Imperator Enigmalia (Hard)
description:
Imperator Enigmalia is personally responsible for the deaths of at least a dozen Farstriders. I say "at least" because this particular domanaar makes it somewhat difficult to identify the bodies, so utterly destroyed are they.

Go and avenge our fallen soldiers.

title: Prey: Knight-Errant Bloodshatter (Hard)
description:
Death knights are unstable killing machines at the best of times. Knight-Errant Bloodshatter has forsaken any oath or promise that would ostensibly bind one of his kind to the bottom rungs of civilized society, and has gone completely rogue.

He is starting to become a liability.

Recoup his anguish so that we can make the best of such an unfortunate situation.

title: Prey: Vylenna the Defector (Hard)
description:
Vylenna the Defector lives up to her name. After a recent engagement on the field of battle, she became so preoccupied with profaning the dead that she completely abandoned the duties ascribed to her.
 
Then her dereliction turned violent.

A veritable font of anguish if you ask me...

title: Prey: Lost Theldrin (Hard)
description:
Those of us who succumbed to our base desire for mana were slowly weeded out of civil society, if they survived at all. Theldrin is one such example, only it is a testament to his cunning and will to live that he has made it as long as he has.

He accepts no aid, choosing instead to stalk careless students and idle Magisters. His sole obsession is the mana that others possess...

See to it that he never harms another elf again.

title: Prey: Grothoz, the Burning Shadow (Hard)
description:
The Twilight's Blade are an embarrassing band of mediocre cultists and uninspired conspirators. Grothoz is the brains of a particularly stupid cabal that has been active in a nearby region. 

As his stupidity prevents him from using his whole brain, let us unlock his potential for him...

title: Prey: Neydra the Starving (Hard)
description:
Poor Neydra... I knew her, in a previous life. She was full of vitality. Full of ambition. Unfortunately, she let that ambition get the best of her. She grew too enamored with the raw power of the arcane. She became resentful of all other things. Of all other people...

And now she is a murderous creature who beheads rats and attacks innocents in a desperate attempt to garner enough arcane energy to slack her unquenchable thirst.

It's funny, the paths life takes us...

title: Prey: Thornspeaker Edgath (Hard)
description:
This one presents a prickly problem indeed. Thornspeaker Edgath has made a name for himself by using his druidic powers not to preserve Azeroth, but to twist its strength both against his enemies and innocent bystanders alike. He has no regard for anything that is not a beast or growing in the ground.

It is time we clip this thorn.

title: Prey: Thorn-Witch Liset (Hard)
description:
Thorn-Witch Liset is a curse upon civilization and a blight upon the land itself. She twists the very trees and beasts, communing with the dark, evil things that live on the very edges of the natural realm.

I despise the natural realm.

title: Prey: Dengzag, the Darkened Blaze (Hard)
description:
Do you know why the Twilight's Blade call Dengzag the "Darkened Blaze?" It is because they are stupid, small-minded, and utterly bereft of imagination. What a silly name indeed. 

I, however, am not small-minded. I have quite the prodigious mind, and it is currently intent on putting an end to Dengzag and whatever his tiny brain is up to. 

Get to it.

title: Prey: High Vindicator Vureem (Nightmare)
description:
High Vindicator Vureem is a victim of his own unyielding faith in the Light and the superiority it gives him. His crusade continues unabated and unresolved. He exists in a constant state of fury at the imperfectness of the world and those within it.

What say we put his worldview to the test?

title: Career Counseling
description:
How good are you at teaching, {name}?

One of my pupils, Kyltus, has been having particular trouble with deciding what path along the road of life he wants to pursue. 

Perhaps a talk with you will aid his tumultuous heart?

title: A Path Not Yet Chosen
description:
I'm trying to figure out what career path to follow, but I keep second-guessing myself. The Farstriders, the Blood Knights, the Magisters... they all seem so incredible that I can't decide.

I was hoping if I spoke to each and earned a recommendation, I might finally understand where I'm meant to be. Would you help me?

title: A Test of the Hunt
description:
So, you want a Farstrider recommendation? We don't hand those out like festival ribbons.

If you want to earn it, then you'll need to do what every other recruit does. Bring me lynx pelts, clean and proper.

No shortcuts, no excuses. Let's see if you've got the spine for it.

title: A Test of Blood
description:
Ah, you seek a Blood Knight's recommendation, do you? In that case, you'll need to prove your worth... against me. 

Only those with true strength and honor may join our ranks! Prepare yourself. This won't be a simple challenge, but if you can best me, I'll gladly offer my recommendation!

title: A Test of the Arcane
description:
Ah, you seek a recommendation from a Magister, do you? Very well... but first, you must prove your understanding of the arcane.

Go to the nearby crystals we have for training, extract their energy, and channel it into this staff. Show me that you can harness the arcane, and I will see you worthy of my recommendation.

Should you fail, don't bother returning. My time is too valuable for such trivial matters.

title: How to Train Your Protege
description:
I did everything they asked--the trials, the lessons--and they all said I did well... but I still don't know where I truly belong.

Each path felt right in its own way, and now my heart feels pulled in three directions.

Please... could you help me decide?

title: A Fish!
description:
There is a deliciously delectable dish that I am dying to share with my guests. We start with just the right fish, then chop it up, drop it in a blend of citrus and our special savory seasonings, and let it bask in the glorious flavors.

In the shake of a tail feather, it becomes a magical delight for the senses!

We are the talk of the town, you know. Could you please procure for us the perfect fish?

title: Pesky Pests
description:
Nearby creatures are suffering an infection of Light, and are being drawn to the Sunbath. Our guests find it disturbing. That is a problem, when one is trying to enjoy a tranquil escape from the world.

I confess, having one run into your leg while you are swimming can be unnerving.

Please, dissuade the invading sungrubs from joining us, and take to the sky to chase off those persistent butterflies.

title: Secret Ingredients
description:
Our gregarious guests have partaken so much from our stores that we are running dangerously low of our special Solaarian sintalia.

The rich taste of belanise, with its intoxicating scent, contrasts perfectly with the sharp tartness of the local berries. Carefully prepared then soaked in our special blend of spirits, then served--it's to die for!

Just between us, if the berries are not handled just right, the infusion can be quite deadly. Otherwise... delicious, invigorating, purifying, glorious!

title: Lost in Light
description:
It seems some of our more adventurous guests have not returned from their hike. Val said he saw a rutaani skulking about earlier, but I was certain he was just trying to add some drama to our day.

The Lightbloom infiltrated the Sanctum to the north and has been spilling residual issues our way.

Our guests are here to escape the troubles of the world... This all could become a bit too real for them.

If you'll take care of that situation, I'll make sure Val keeps the guests distracted.

title: Far from the Hinterlands
description:
