As a Torntusk of de Revantusk tribe, I am cautious of Witherbark because of our history with dem in de Hinterlands. My family moved here because of dem.

The Witherbark here in Zul'Aman seem to respect us, but de Witherbark I remember in de Hinterlands killed and backstabbed their allies for power.

They worshipped de spider loa, Shadra.

When de Hinterlands's Witherbark temple Shadra'Alor fell, de Witherbark must have fled somewhere. Look around for evidence of Hinterlands Witherbark and of Shadra.

title: The Eye of the Loa
description:
I lost friends to venoms made from Shadra's spiders in de Hinterlands. I gotta know if Shadra's lurkin' around here, twistin' de minds of these Witherbark. No Revantusk will be safe in Zul'Aman with Shadra-worshippin' Witherbark around!

Maybe I seem paranoid, but I have my reasons. And you have Halazzi's ear. You could ask him to reveal to you any hidden spiders, yes? I know those long ears can hear dem skitterin'!

There is a shrine behind me. Stand in front of it and see if he'll grant you a boon.

title: Probable Paralytic
description:
Halazzi says dat de Vilebranch have made a new venom dat para... parliz--makes its victims not move--so they can drag them off to be sacrificed. He says it's frog venom.

I said that doesn't make sense. Frogs are poison, right? Halazzi said frogs are normally poisonous, not venomous. But these have sharp horn bits on them, so maybe they have venom! I didn't know frogs could have venom, did you?

Let's find out if these do--then de Witherbark can make an anti-venom!

title: A Most Vile Venom
description:
De Vilebranch are cookin' up more bad things. You should go to de source and make dem stop!

Halazzi has smelled their venom-mixin' place someplace to de southeast, and he thinks it's more den just frog venom.

...Oh, Halazzi also said to bring back some kind of huge fang. Frogs don't have fangs, though. Well, find it anyway!

title: Validating the Venom
description:
This means there are both frog and spider venoms in de mixtures. Halazzi says he senses somebody beseeched Shadra with dat fang. Dey must be a really big spider if their fang is dat big!

Halazzi doesn't like that de Vilebranch had it. I can tell. He's unhappy. His ears get all flat like de wings of a kite.

So we gotta help out. Take de samples and de fang to de Witherbark Chief. We Witherbark have alchemists dat can brew anti-venoms for both spiders and frogs.

title: Seeking Shadra
description:
We will untangle dis mess about Witherbark and Vilebranch soon enough. De loa Shadra and her schemes must be our first priority.

I watched the spiders run in terror from you, and somethin' struck me. De spiders are not just running in panic--they're going somewhere... somewhere dey feel safe, and even I can see dem now. 

Dey are not hiding anymore. Dey are on de run! Our hunt continues.

Let's follow dem.

title: Stolen Sight
description:
I can explain, I swear it!

I concealed my knowledge of Shadra before because so many people assume she is an evil force. Our lady is merciless, but only to her enemies. 

When de Witherbark left de Hinterlands, we had to leave our temple of Shadra'Alor. I brought two of her relics with me: de eye and de fang.

De Vilebranch stole de fang. Dey are near where I hid the Eye of Shadra, collectin' venom from her children! Please kill de Vilebranch trespassing in the crypt. We can't let dem find de eye.

title: Dreaming of Spiders
description:
There are three relics of Shadra. I only had two. I had de fang in de village and de eye hidden here. Dey musta stolen de fang somehow, then came for de eye!

We never had de Shell of Shadra--it was lost when we left Shadra'Alor. It's possible de Vilebranch have de shell.

De fang and de eye should let us talk to Shadra briefly, even if we lack de last ritual relic to summon her.

Come with me. I will show you how.

title: Maisara Caverns: Deep in Maisara
description:
Shadra has asked dat we recover de third piece of Mixer Jamanga's ritual, de Shell of Shadra. With it, maybe we can bring her back.

She recognized both de Witherbark and Revantusk as her allies. I felt her approval upon all of us. I believe this is de right choice.

Halazzi set us on dis path, and we each got our roles to see it through. Gather your allies and go into Maisara Caverns! I would expect one of their leaders to hold de shell. Kill dem and take it back.

title: Return of the Venom Queen
description:
It is time to bring Shadra to her proper place as a loa of de Witherbark and Revantusk. She will be small at first, but one day could be great like Halazzi and Nalorakk!

Even a small loa will be a good ally against de Vilebranch venoms. She is de Loa of Spies, and will block their thievery of our people and relics, just as Halazzi hides them in the mists.

Hold de Shell of Shadra in de center of de room, and we will call de loa to us!

title: Shared Loa
description:
De Witherbark Chief knows many of us came from de Hinterlands with our worship of Shadra'Alor, but believed Shadra was long dead.

We should let him know how Halazzi helped us to find and restore Shadra, and of Shadra's presence in de Witherbark crypt. He also must learn of de rapport with de Revantusk.

I will stay with Shadra for now. Will you carry de message?

title: Tomes of Terror
description:
Knowledge is power, and de ogres been reading.

Bring me dey tomes so dey never grow dis powah again.

title: Ashes of the Void
description:
De void elementals have one use.

De dust dey drop is useful for shadow magic rituals.

Collect some for our tribe.

title: Blades of the Fallen
description:
After de battle we left a lot of our weapons behind.

Please go collect dem so de Twilight's Blade doesn't get dem.

title: Dead by Dusk
description: De Twilight's Blade cultists have de audacity to try and take back our temple. Kill de peons and bring me their femurs.

title: Ultra Deadly Ultradon
description:
I saw some of de void elementals doing a sketchy ritual in de center of the battlefield. It looked like dey were summoning some void creatures.

Make sure whatever dey summon is dealt with.

title: Supplicants to the Goddess
description:
We have long tended to the roots with the help of the creatures who live and grow here. Think of it as a symbiotic relationship from which we, the creatures, and the roots all benefit. That said, my partner Riverscale isn't contributing very much lately!

Please help me find a way to motivate him again.

First, you must cleanse off the essence of the outside. Cleanse yourself in the waters of Har'kuai.

title: Fungal Lashers B Gone
description:
We are blessed to live in Harandar, but this is also our responsibility. The roots of the World Trees wind through these lands. We tend them, and they thrive. That is why it is so important for Riverscale to be back on duty.

We need to keep these grounds clear of these fungal lashers before they leach off the roots. As a plus, Riverscale really loves lasher fronds. Can you find and bring some back for him?

title: Weeding Out the Unwanted
description:
The roots of the World Trees are infused with both nutrients and the magic of Harandar. As the large vessels that they are, they will draw to them unwanted fauna and flora. Some of them are harmless, but we want to keep the roots clear.

Haranir can often be allergic to the green weeds that flourish beside the roots of the World Trees. If you rip them out by the root, you would be saving us a lot of time--and hives!

title: Back on Duty?
description:
Finally! I knew the little guy could do it.

No, that is a bald-faced lie. If this grovecrawler could somehow find a way back into his cocoon, he would already be there.

<Cyenna sighs.>

His second favorite treat is the potatoad. If that doesn't work, his third favorite treat is the petalwing. That should get him back onto his mite-hunting duties.

...I have the backbone of a wet frond.

title: Slithering Closer
description:
Do you bring tidings from Numia? She was supposed to be here and help me assess these fair flowers.

She must be occupied. The naga have encroached on my town and hers... they have kept to the beach so far, but that could change quickly.

Will you check on my friend?

title: Not What I Ordered
description:
<A bite wound wrecks Hathvelion's forearm.>

Snapdragons... cruel creatures made to kill and feed. Their poison moves as you move. The naga bred the perfect pet.

I'm here on a tip from Lyssara Duskmourne. Sunsail Anchorage's shipping routes have been sabotaged by the naga, and those routes converge... here.

I intercepted a few shipments. Could you help me search them? My team is still out in the field, but they should report back soon.

title: Daggers in My Spine
description:
The naga have a history of abusing arcane magic. I bet my boots this is no different--arcane is their lifeblood and source of power. They are clever to operate now while Eversong is besieged by other threats.

Since I became a Farstrider, I have safeguarded Eversong's western coast. My strike team set up watch on Goldenmist's perimeter already. Fortunately, the naga do not seem interested in the town yet, but they are far too close for comfort.

Our course of action is simple: clear them out!

title: Familiar Faces in Peril
description:
Fair winds guide your steps. My wish has come true--Eversong flourishes. But like before, evil threatens all that is good.

Two townsfolk are unaccounted for since the naga arrived: Enith--Goldenmist's most curious apothecary, and Varnis--he's a student visiting from the city. It is my charge to find them.

My fellow ranger, Vedoran, must still be in the field. I wonder if he's found something important. Will you keep an eye out for him?

title: One Elf's Trash, Another Elf's Treasure
description:
The arcane arts are not my specialty. I'm more versed in tangible magic--herbs, mixtures, distillation, topical applications, extraction, suture, painkilling...

Regardless--that wand. To my uneducated eyes, it looks usable. Why don't you test it on the arcana container? Maybe it could help us against the naga. If it's truly broken, no harm done.

title: Arcane Amassing
description:
Well, you heard Numia. She'll have my bow split in two if I don't rest.

Ranger Vedoran reported activity at the lighthouse. A swarm of mana wyrms are gathered there, and of course, more naga.

If that's where the head of the snake lives, chop it off! My rangers Vedoran and Krann'an will accompany you. And take the wand with you. It proved its worth. Powered up, it should pack a punch against whatever you face.

title: A Game of Silence and Shadow
description:
You're different! That makes you kinda cool... and kinda really suspicious. We wanna see what you're made of!

So here's the game: we hide, and you try to find us. If you do, well... guess we'll have to train more for next time. But if you don't? Then maybe our parents will finally let us go on our first solo hunt!

This isn't just a silly game, okay? It's a trial called Silence and Shadow. It's serious. Very, very serious.

Are you ready?

title: De-nest-stration
description:
If mom and dad find out, we are going to be stuck on fish gutting duty for the rest of our lives! 

The elders keep telling us that fear is only useful if it sharpens the mind. Right now, we need clarity, not panic.

We have to get rid of those nests. Maybe if they are gone, we can eventually find the one they took Shao'mal to. And this way, we can finally drive off those stupid birds!

We're going to find him. Will you help us?

title: Feathered Fury
description:
This would have never happened if those stupid petalwings weren't here!

Scare them off, chase them, whatever it takes--we just can't let them stay here. If they stay, they'll just keep hurting people. We can't let that happen.

title: Haranir Never Say Die!
description:
That's the one! The big one! The one that took him!

We didn't stop it before. But this time, we will. We are haranir. And haranir never back down. Haranir never give up.

We're bringing him back. And we're ending this. Are you with me?

title: Stronger Than Before
description:
That must be the monster that killed Hieron. It's tearing open a backdoor for the Void's forces to pour into Silvermoon.

This damn city. I'm not doing this for them.

Signal Umbric. Let's do this. Together.

title: To Be Changed
description:
There is one unresolved matter...

<Astre reaches into their pocket and reveals the worn letter you had unpackaged alongside Hieron's journal. They run their fingers over it with a pensive expression.>

Hieron wanted her to have it. She should know what happened to him.

This is on me. Silvermoon again... Guess I'll see you when I'm back, {name}.

title: Familiar Energies
description: I know that Hieron died here, but I feel echoes of someone else--of a great clash, where Void met Void. Hieron must have crossed blades with someone... something else. Something that's still out there.

title: Running Amok
description:
I've heard from the other scouts about that metal--the shadowgraft. The domanaar use it to enforce their control over others.

I have to imagine that there's someone holding the reins still. Maybe it's Xal'atath, or maybe someone else. Gather pieces of their shadowgraft and maybe we can make sense of who.

title: Retaking Control
description:
This world is violent and dangerous, but it has an order to things. After you and the others took down the domanaar that ran this citadel and its arena, the food chain must have fallen to disarray.

Harrowers swarm the skies and darken the horizon. I worry they'll seek new hunting grounds closer to our settlements if they aren't dealt with.

Point them out for me and I'll use my grappling hook to pull them to the ground. Hieron's survival gear proves its use when we need it, as always.

title: The Town Inside Me
description:
I can't say that drinking the tea is a pleasant experience. But it helps. I want the others to experience that too.

That Hieron left us with a gift like this is so... him. He was always doing little things like that for others. For me.

Share the tea with the others, then join me at Obscurion Citadel. I know better than to be alone in a place like that.

title: Unchecked Emotions
description:
Ren'dorei learn early to check our emotions. That by controlling ourselves, we can control the Void. Otherwise, our unsuppressed feelings can lead us to ruin.

But I felt just now the powers of facing my feelings head-on. Maybe I could have done it on my own... But the tea, and your aid, made it easier.

We should share Hieron's recipe with the others. I'll retrieve the kettle you brewed.

title: Confronting It
description:
I've put the kettle on. I trust you to brew it, if you wouldn't mind. It shouldn't take more than a moment before the tea is ready to drink.

Let me try it first, just in case it's poisonous. I'm the one that asked to try it.

title: Like a Weed
description:
<Astre looks from Hieron's journal to the worn letter. Their expression flickers with anger.>

Blood elves. They were our people until we embraced the Void. In their ignorance, they exiled us. Yet they are happy to ask us to fight their battles for them.

They say we threaten the Sunwell. The truth is, they are afraid of us.

Let's protect something of actual value. Kill any beasts that threaten those flowers for Hieron's tea. Silvermoon can save itself.

title: Home Sweet Grave
description:
<Astre spends a long time in silence, thumbing through Hieron's journal.>

Killed on another world, defending a city that didn't want him. There was so much we planned to do. Now...

<Astre trails off, their eyes focusing on the journal's pages.>

Oh. The tea recipe that Hieron was telling me about. He was excited for its properties--called it a purgative for the soul. He never had a chance to brew it.

I know it seems stupid, {name}. I just want to taste Hieron's tea. The leaves should be nearby.

title: What We Leave Behind
description:
Lothraxion deserved to pay for his arrogance with his life. Hieron didn't. Without him, I'd be dead. I know that Maella and Darkglen feel the same way.

Hieron's journal, the one you recovered for us, ended up with me. I knew Hieron, but Astre knew him better. The two of them had found a closeness I admired. Astre deserves to hold onto it.

Last I saw Astre, Hieron was tending to their wounds back at Locus Point.

You were there when Hieron passed. They should know of his heroism and sacrifice.

title: Ambition's Reward
description:
You have filled the reliquary with power I can draw on. Now, we reap the reward of our preparations.

Kalgorg waits just ahead, draining the beast that once made this cave its lair. Weaken Kalgorg, and I will bind it as I first did years ago.

title: Unlimited
description:
Now, we descend. The Void energy below is unlike any I've felt before, as if gravity itself yields to the Void. Once you enter the cave, its influence will be upon you.

Kalgorg has already slain and claimed power from the creatures within. Their violent deaths have left behind some kind of cursed terrain, but we must proceed and claim the power within. Fill the reliquary to the brim. 

I know not what to expect from my voidwalker. We will need all the power we can manage.

title: For Violence's Sake
description:
Like many warlocks, I bind my demons with terms of a contract. I should have realized the Void and violence of this world could affect the rules I took for granted. Kalgorg used that power to break its bindings and end our arrangement. 

When we confront Kalgorg, the scent of blood and violence must be upon us. Slay the beasts you find to demonstrate to Kalgorg we are willing to do the same to it. Control over the Void is found and lost within the blink of an eye.

title: Smothered in the Crib
description:
I summoned my first imp in a dirty, haze-filled lounge beneath Murder Row after a dozen failed attempts. Each time, I reached further for what made so-called greater powers submit to mortals. I finally managed it after wetting my hands with the hot blood of a young rabbit. Bloodthistle never satisfied me again.

I have matured my technique since then. Seek the whelps of these beasts. Siphon their energy into my reliquary of darkness. From their sacrifice, we will draw the power to defeat Kalgorg.

title: Overwhelming Darkness
description:
A dreadful presence calls from the den just to the southeast of here. Many of the dazed ren'dorei you see in camp were overwhelmed by its raw power on a scouting mission.

Let us go together and see if whatever dwells within can be put to use. We cannot afford to spurn power in the fight against Xal'atath.

Before that: we have little need of Kalgorg. The Voidstorm has made my voidwalker unruly and disobedient. Assist me in sacrificing it. We will need all of the Void energy we can muster.

title: Fresh from the Garden
description:
Age has caught up with me this morning and I have pulled something. 

<Yu'relen clutches his back.>

Still, our village meal must be prepared. Otherwise, it will mean the loss of much more than just an empty stomach.

If you would gather some ingredients for the meal, your aid would not go unappreciated.

title: Soil-Based Alternatives
description:
This meal is more than tradition--it is a promise.

The haranir, rutaani, and fungarians share no love, but in Har'mara they share this table. To honor that pact, each must be fed in their own way. That is what this meal means.

For the rutaani, they will need nutrient-rich soil as they draw sustenance through their roots. A few bags of it should do.

title: Root Dash Delivery
description:
It's simple fare, but made with care and meaning. Please, take it to the gathering place. I am sure the villagers will show up when the food comes.

Despite the simplicity, if it brings quiet among them, it has done its part.

title: Harandar's Kitchen
description:
Now comes the true work: preparing what's been gathered. 

The vegetables must be washed, the bones removed, and the fish roasted. A shared meal is only worthy if made with care. Only then may the meal carry the weight of peace.

title: Carcass Cuisine
description:
The fungarians are caretakers of decay, transforming death into life's renewal. 

They require a sporeglider carcass to sustain their vast community. There is one--larger than most--that has taken to warding off the root drifters we need to sustain our village.

Its end will feed many, and ease our burden. Balance, in more ways than one.

title: Saltheril's Haven
description:
{name}, I presume? Very good. Your reputation precedes you.

Lord Saltheril sends his most sincere greetings, along with a cordial invitation to join him at his estate south of Silvermoon.

A portion of the grounds have been carved out as a decadent haven for those seeking refined company and pleasant conversation. He believes you would bring a... unique perspective.

<He looks you over with a hint of polite disdain.>

Receiving an invitation is a rare honor. His lordship hopes you will accept.

title: Honored Guests
description:
I am pleased you could join us. Today marks the start of a very special occasion.

The sin'dorei are no strangers to the war table, but its aristocracy prefers to play more coy. In wartime, the crown sends representatives to curry favor with the nobles. If they are pleased, they might lend gold to the war effort.

It is very much like courting a lover, with all the jealous games you might imagine.

So let us play the game, shall we? Deliver these invitations while we finish preparations here.

title: High Esteem
description:
Invitations to my Haven are highly exclusive, especially at a time like this. Extending an offer, even a temporary one, is a great honor.

My favor carries quite a bit of weight amongst the aristocracy. If I invite an ally of one of our representatives, it suggests I think highly of them--and if I think highly of them, so too will our noble guests.

But is it not terribly fun if I let you choose an invitation instead?

Go peruse any enticements they may offer, then make your selection.

title: The Subtle Game
description:
Appearances are everything. If the court sees that I favor a certain group, they will follow suit.

Take these tokens of my favor and hand them out to our honored guests as you see fit. I suspect they may invite you to partake in their plans and share in the profits, if you choose to align yourself with their cause.

title: If You Want It Done Right
description:
With all the problems facing Eversong, the Farstriders have their hands full!

I'm a capable arcanist. I've always admired the Farstriders--why not lend my talents to their cause? They do so much work by hand, when a bit of magic would allow them so much more time for adventuring!

Alas, my enchanted broom idea has not quite gone as I'd hoped. They're so ornery, they end up attacking the recruits instead of cleaning!

Perhaps this is a problem for elbow grease after all.

title: To the North Tower
description:
Ranger Valsarin is a chef at Farstrider Hold. He's decent in a kitchen, but his passion is field cooking.

There was a time when you could wander any path in Eversong and find the ingredients for a good meal. A Farstrider's dream! With enough hard work, we can help the forest recover, and it starts with rehabilitating the soil.

Meet Valsarin at the North Tower and he can tell you more.

title: Strider Stampede
description:
It's hard to believe this used to be the Dead Scar.

Up here, close to the city, the land has almost completely recovered. Which is good, because we've got a lot of mouths to feed.

Hawkstriders are the perfect helpers for preparing the soil. Their tough claws can easily spread seeds and squash bugs!

Meet me down in the Tranquil Repose and I'll saddle one up for you.

title: See a Mana 'bout a Wyrm
description:
Mana wyrms may not be as glamorous as lynxes or hawkstriders, but they're crucial to keeping magic flowing through the forest.

They congregate on the ley lines to molt, but sometimes they get a little agitated. And we have to help them out! Aggressively!

I've set up some ley channels to get things started. Find wyrms that look like they could use a little help, and beat them up until they shed their arcane carapace. Don't worry--all that mana returns to the forest eventually!

title: The Dark Part of the Woods
description:
Much of the forest has recovered, but to the south... Everything that Eversong has faced--the Scourge, the fel, and now the Twilight's Blade--all that magic leeches into the soil. It builds up in the creatures until they're completely overwhelmed...

My instruments can detect corruption in these animals. We can cull the ones that are too far gone and save the ones that aren't. It isn't fun... but it's for the good of Eversong!

title: A Real Assignment
description:
Captain Dawnrunner was supposed to check on the Enclave and then meet us here. She's as capable a ranger as any! It's a bad sign if she's been detained.

Farstrider Enclave is usually where they do wilderness training. With all the trouble however, everyone's been dispatched elsewhere. She could be up there alone, in serious danger!

There's no time to lose. I'm going to save my Captain and prove myself as a Farstrider!

title: Tidy Up
description:
Captain Dawnrunner is fine, thank the sun. The only danger was... we didn't keep up on our chores.

It's not heroic, it's not romantic, but we can't go on with Farstrider Enclave in this state. The rangers here are overwhelmed. We can all pitch in to help them get it cleaned up again.

I can't believe it all comes back to sweeping. That's what being a Farstrider is really about.

title: Recovery Mission
description:
We've been deploying Farstriders across the forest to fight the Amani, the Lightbloom, the Void... but for all our heroics, there's no one left to tend to the wildlife! Or maintain the building! Or keep track of supplies!

And now our beloved Enclave looks like a hawkstrider pen. I've had enough!

I may not be able to help, but I trust you all can gather anything that was taken and get this place looking respectable again.

title: A Ranger's Spirit
description:
Taemin has proved himself as capable as any Farstrider today. But I'm afraid he might not see it that way.

He came to us with such lofty ideas about the rangers--all those swashbuckling adventure stories, all the romantic old legends. But at the end of the day, being a ranger is mostly... chores. Difficult, boring, important chores.

Why don't you go talk to him, and make sure he's not too disappointed?

title: Range of Knowledge
description:
I've always dreamed of being a Farstrider--the adventure! The romance! The beauty of nature! When they put out a call for help, I knew I had to join.

Captain Dawnrunner assigned me to the library initially, but with everything going on, we all have to be flexible.

I don't mind, but I had hoped to collect all the overdue books before being sent on a real assignment. If you find any, would you bring them to the library for me?

And feel free to give them a read! There are some interesting volumes!

title: To the South Tower
description:
Despite being new to the Farstriders, Taemin has the hardest assignment. Eversong Woods has mostly recovered, but the further south you go, the more dangerous things get.

He's a capable mage, though. And we need his nose for magic.

We'll meet you down there once things are wrapped up here. Captain Dawnrunner was going to provide backup. She should be there by now.

title: To the Central Tower
description:
Ranger Asterae is one of our veterinarians. She usually cares for our lynxes, but the mana wyrm molt is too crucial to the health of the forest to go unmonitored.

The wyrms regulate the flow of magic from the ley lines to the rest of the woods. When the ley lines were disrupted, so was their molting season. It's not so dire as it once was, but they still need help from time to time.

Meet Asterae at the Central Tower. She can tell you more.

title: A Ranger's Dream
description:
Apprentices! Who can keep them tied down?

Taemin was a particularly talented arcanist, but his head was always in the clouds. Or in the woods, really.

Instead of studying, he buried his nose in storybooks! The legends of Eversong! The Rangers of old! Heroes of the Sin'dorei! He couldn't get enough, the dreaming fool!

And now... no, he's not dead. Did I imply that?

He's just run off to join the Farstriders. Check in on him, won't you? See if he's come to his senses?

title: The Spiritpaw
description:
I just chased away some furbolg dat were actin' real strange.

Dey were arguin' and then one walked toward Lilaju with his hands in de air.

I charged at dem an' scared dem off. Dey are usually hostile and who knows what dey were gonna do?

But Lilaju wants to see what dey wanted.

Our scouts saw dem follow de river east to de north side of de lake. Track dem down. See what dey want.

If it's a trick, then kill dem.

title: Not Quite Nalorakk
description:
Were you the one we saw enter Nalorakk's temple with Zul'jarra?

I thought so! Zul'jarra spoke to him, didn't she?

<Furgh growls in an unsettling way from deep in his throat.>

That doesn't make sense. The new Voice of Nalorakk says Nalorakk won't speak to anyone but him.

He gave some of the tribe trinkets to feel closer to Nalorakk. But the tribe now fights over them. The ursas think it's unfair they don't get any. But the chosen cling to them.

We must take them before more blood is spilled.

title: Too Much Twilight
description:
This big bear called himself the Voice of Nalorakk and has "chosen" some of our tribe as his favorites. He doesn't know, but I saw him put dark, glowing lodestones in the totems around the village to give his chosen power.

Dark power radiates from them. The power smells wrong. Not like Nalorakk.

Our guardian bears get agitated and upset near them. They can smell it too!

We must cleanse the camp. Get rid of those totems!

title: It's Just Not Right
description:
If Chief Groffa is leading the tribe into the dark, he needs to be stopped. The Voice of Nalorakk dazzled Groffa with trinkets. He keeps them locked away so no one else can have them.

If that is true, he's corrupted too. Even more than the others!

Stop him and get the gifts out of the village.

title: Precious Trinkets
description:
Finally we get to see what treasures Groffa was hoarding.

<Furgh's eyes go black as he looks at the bag. The drool on his jowls turns to foam and his claws dig into the sides of the bag as his grip on it tightens.>

Maybe the loa speaker's gifts aren't so bad. We should not be so hasty. I'll take a look. You watch and keep me safe.

title: Perils of Trust
description:
That was scary. We nearly lost Furgh!

<Fleeg's fur twitches in fear and worry as he looks Furgh over.>

Furgh said he heard the Voice of Nalorakk talking to him after touching the treasures.

But it can't be Nalorakk's voice. Nalorakk doesn't need trinkets!

Rip out the throat of this evil that dares to say it speaks for Nalorakk!

title: Delves: Measure Once, Cut Twice
description:
We've done well to see those deserters to safety, but don't fool yourself into thinking we've freed them all.

I've heard whispers of a place they use to break those whose loyalty is in question. The location isn't common knowledge, but I know where to start looking.

If we're quick, we might find others before the cult decides they're too great a risk to keep alive.

Meet me near the site. Keep your blades sharp and your steps light.

title: The Regent's Request
description:
Lor'themar has requested our presence. He wasn't exactly forthcoming with the details, but the location he chose speaks volumes.

Silvermoon Harbor. Quiet and out of sight. Not exactly the kind of place one picks for diplomacy or ceremony. No, this feels like something else entirely. Whatever he's planning, it won't be written down in any official record. 

Come. Let's find out what the Regent Lord has to say.

title: Lines Cut, Tongues Silenced
description:
The Twilight's Blade slipped into Tranquillien so easily because they'd stolen knowledge and secrets. About our defenses, our magic, our people. Once inside, they uncovered even more information. 

Now they pass that intelligence between themselves using enchanted orbs. Quiet whispers drifting through the dark. Every message tightens the noose around Quel'Thalas.

Find the orbs. Break them. Every shattered crystal is a whisper silenced.

title: The Thieves' Trail
description:
From what I can see, it looks like the Twilight's Blade stole several arcane artifacts used by our Magisters to enhance long-distance communication. If they learn to wield them, they'll cast their reach far beyond what we can contain. They might even be able to hear our communications... and we can't have that.

Recover the artifacts. If anyone gets in your way... you know what to do.

title: Keys Are Optional
description:
These people joined the Twilight's Blade looking for purpose, power, or place to belong. For a reprieve from the fear and a promise of certainty.

Now they see it for what it is--a prison dressed up as faith. And they want out.

We go in together. Get them out before the cult realizes what's happening.

title: No Loose Ends
description:
You handled yourself well.

These documents are too important to pass through couriers or ravens. I'd like you to deliver them personally to Lor'themar. He'll want to see the truth for himself and know who brought it back.

Keep them close, and don't let anyone else near them.

title: A Favor for the Lion
description:
A letter arrived for me. No seal, no name. Just folded parchment and handwriting I'd recognize anywhere.

It didn't say much. He never wastes words. 

I've known his family a long time. When they reach out, I answer.

title: One by One
description:
Every last cultist is a threat to the people we're trying to save. To Silvermoon itself.

Blasted fanatics with blood on their hands and no intention of letting anyone walk out alive.

We're already in the camp. Let's make it count. I want them to feel this.

title: Intercepted
description:
The Twilight's Blade hit Tranquillien hard. Slipped in and took all the supplies and information they could ever need, right beneath everyone's nose.

Time to return the favor.

I've marked a few tents. Command posts, messengers' satchels, a supply ledger tucked under a wine crate. That's where they keep their orders, maps, and routes.

Grab everything you can.

title: Dead Men Keep No Secrets
description:
From what I've seen, this group isn't acting without direction. There's someone leading them, a man hiding in one of their structures.

If I had to guess, he's the one holding whatever they considered too important to trust to crystal or courier. Documents, plans, names.

Find him, silence him, and bring back whatever he was protecting.

title: Dirty Deeps
description:
De Vilebranch are sinkin' deeper into their cursed minds. Dey spit in de face of de loa, and believe dey have mastered death.

I could see a certain... someone not likin' dat way of thinkin'.

Dey have taken our kin and now dey take our shaman. Meet my scout there and help dem break their plans.

Cut de head off de beast and de body will fall.

title: Send Dem Home
description:
My friend, dis is a mighty gift. Shadra is givin' us a blessin' for de tasks ahead.

These Vilebranch really are livin' up to their name. Dey drain de spirit from de Witherbark and create undead to serve dem.

We must free de Witherbark from these necrotic bonds.

My friend's still in there... I hope. Me and Bin'zin cut our tusks together growin' up. He grew into a strong shaman. Our village needs him, and he is like a brother to me.

title: Respect de Totem
description:
When dey took Bin'zin, de priestess destroyed his totems and gave de shattered remains to her minions--like some grisly memento.

Take de pieces back, one by one if ya have to. Dat disrespect will not stand.

title: De Vile Diminished
description:
These vile orbs are harvestin' de souls of our people and sendin' dark energy to de Knife Priestess. She's gettin' mighty powerful.

Destroy de orbs so her ritual of death is ruined. With de gift of Shadra, ya will not fail.

title: One Will Not Rise
description:
Now's our best shot! We end de sacrifices here! Stoppin' de dark energy has weakened her hold.

Then, you can be de one to bring back her head.

Uh, and {name}--no tellin' de chief about me callin' him Big VunVun. He'll be turnin' me into a frog if he hears dat again.

title: Sacrifice Denied
description:
Our shaman is home and Jin'zin's full of joy to see him again. She was always his little wildcat apprentice.

Dey will have sad moments--Tan'zin is a mighty loss.

Today, ya brought him home. Go enjoy dat for a bit, {name}, before ya be on ya way.

title: The Former Rootwarden
description:
{name}, I was the Rootwarden of Teldrassil. For years, I watched the roots here below the surface, but I was not aware of what was threatening the tree outside of Harandar.

For just as long, I have regretted that failure, but we cannot change the past.

I wonder, however, if roots so great may still have life within them. Might there be something which can still be done?

I do not know, but I do hope. Will you join me at the Shrine of Ages, to tend the roots?

title: Buffer Zone
description:
At this shrine, I have worked to gather roots that seem to still have the possibility of life within them. They are each greatly damaged, some more than others. My hope is some might recover, with enough time and enough care. In that way, I honor the great tree's memory.

For that to be possible, though, we have to keep them safe from the hazards of Harandar.

Please be sure that nothing nearby threatens them.

title: Natural Remedy
description:
If we ever hope to see new growth, Teldrassil's roots needs a great deal of special attention.

The source of this stream is one of the great roots. The water carries ash, which I believe still contains the tree's essence.

The stalkers in the stream carry a mixture of that ash and slime from their tendrils--a very potent mixture.

Bring me as much of it as you can carry, and I will combine it with reagents I've gathered. Then we'll be ready to apply it to the roots.

title: Flare Up
description:
{name}, I see smoke rising above Har'mara. The roots are on fire--not here, but beneath the remnants of the tree!

I have seen this happen many times, but we must always respond with haste.

Take to the skies and douse them, immediately!

title: Tending Hope
description:
Thank you for tending to those fires, {name}. While you were gone, I've used that sludge to make a salve. Help me spread it over Teldrassil's roots.

It may take years to see a difference, if we ever do. But though I failed, I made oaths to her, and I can still choose to keep them. I will never stop doing what I can to... help her perhaps grow again.

If I do not succeed, the next Rootwarden, or the one who comes after that, may be the one to finish this work. Even so, I shall not stop trying.

title: A Lover Not a Fighter
description:
De elders are plannin' a festival to give thanks to de loa. I wanna ask Namaji to accompany me but....

It is tradition that a suitor give a gift that proves their prowess as a warrior. But I am a poor fighter and de others laugh at me because of it.

<Kagara hangs his head dejectedly.>

Perhaps you can help me? I know Namaji has always wanted a threshadon tooth necklace. De gnarldin make dem, but only de strongest have one.

Could you find one for me and deliver it to her on my behalf?

title: A Fighter Not a Lover
description:
You goin' to de loa festival? Young trolls like me are expected to ask someone dey like to go wit' dem. And to give a gift to show their worthiness.

I wanna go wit' Namaji. I'm a great fighter, but I'm sure there are many other warriors dat wanna go wit' her!

They'll gift her trophies and spoils. But I'll be different.

I think she'd like a headdress, but I have no idea how to make one. Could you make a feathered headdress for me and give it to her?

You can find feathers at Akil'zon's Reach.

title: Love Triangle
description:
It seems we have a problem: Kagara and Zak'kash both wanna go wit' me to de festival! Which one do I choose?

I like both of dem well enough. Please {name}, you need to help me! What do you think?

title: The Loa of Murlocs
description:
Have you heard of de commotion comin' from Atal'Abasi? De murlocs there are gettin' riled up about somethin'.

It seems like de murlocs have summoned a loa of their own. And dis loa is rallyin' de murlocs to war.

I saw it myself! Just today, lookin' down from dis cliff. De biggest murloc you've ever seen!

I'm not de only one who've seen it either! But de elders say we have bigger problems. Bah!

Dey better not come to me when we get overrun by murlocs!

title: Murloc Madness
description:
These murlocs have come to me, the Loa of Murlocs, to seek my protection against an evil invader.

The Deepfin have risen from the abyssal depths of the ocean. They have invaded this land and defiled its grounds!

I charge you, champion, to defend this kingdom and drive the Deepfin back into the sea!

<King Mrgl-Mrgl lowers his voice to a whisper.>

Get rid of the invaders and maybe the natives won't need me to protect them, and I can leave!

title: Fish Are Food, Not Friends
description:
I have heard your mrgls, my people. Your stomachs grgl in hunger!

The invaders have cut you off from the food you so desperately need to survive.

I will send my champion to gather fish so you may fill your bellies once more!

<King Mrgl-Mrgl lowers his voice to a whisper.>

Or you can just give me fish if you have some?

title: Following Suit
description:
My fellow murlocs! One among you was chosen to be my... my chosen!

She is destined for greatness and is my assistant... erm, priestess! Yes, priestess of the loa!

Your priestess has gone missing, possibly taken by the treacherous Deepfin.

To showcase my great power, I will send my champion to find and return her to you.

<King Mrgl-Mrgl lowers his voice to a whisper.>

My assistant is missing, and I'm stuck here! Find her and tell her I'm on top of the temple!

title: There Can Be Only One
description:
I've observed the Deepfin kidnapping native murlocs and raising troll corpses as undead thralls. But they don't push further inland--they take them all back to the sea.

I think they're being led by a powerful murloc who remains safely offshore, hiding from view as it accepts its minions' tribute.

We can draw it out. If you put on this murloc suit and offer tribute, it might fool the leader into showing itself.

If we eliminate the leader, we might be able to save the native murlocs!

title: Uncommon Threads
description:
Our patron--Gaari, I suppose--should count himself lucky. These mana wyrms produce the finest silk in Eversong. I know Magisters who would kill to have a robe trimmed in this, and yet here we are, using it for a mending job. Ah well.

Once the wyrms have eaten their fill of arcane energy, you can siphon the silk from them onto these bobbins.

If they are reluctant to release their silk, you may have to, ah... beat it out of them. But I've been assured it's entirely ethical.

title: Clothes Make the Man
description:
It is not merely the material that makes an arcanoweave cloak so fine, but the weaving process itself.

To mend one, particularly one that has built up so much history and latent arcane energy... I am about to embark on a very dangerous bit of tailoring indeed. Finally, a real testament to my skills.

I am excited. I hope nothing explodes. Meet me inside when you're prepared.

title: Material Gains
description:
How on Azeroth one of these encroaching paladins managed to come by such an exquisite example of Thalassian tailoring is beyond me. But it would be negligent of me to turn down this opportunity.

The materials we need are far more bespoke than I keep in my shop, however. We'll need to source our manasilk directly. Fortunately, it isn't far--just outside the city gates.

And at least there we'll be safe from these dabblers.

title: Calling in the Cavalry
description:
The wards on the runestones weaken by the hour. Without fresh arcane-charged crystals, the Lightbloom will gain ground.

I know this is not your burden to bear as you have much more to handle, but if you could see these supplies delivered, it would mean more than I can say.

Will you lend your strength to this task, for the sake of all who call this place home?

title: And Then They Came
description:
Brace yourself--the time is upon us!

Hold the line and keep them from reaching the runestone at all costs. I'll focus on restoring its power, but I need you to protect me and everyone here.

Are you ready?

title: Dawnstar Defense
description:
The nearby runestone has gone dark, and the Lightbloom is on its way. I can't hold this ground alone. Help me set the weapons, line up the potions, and get our supplies in order.

We'll meet that growth with discipline and precision and send it back in pieces.

title: Truth of the Past
description:
Thanks for freeing me from those ethereal cages in the Ethereum Overcroft. The Void is powerful here in ways we're only beginning to understand, and that danger brings new opportunities.

If you remember, my quest to combine the power of the Void with the strength of the Illidari has not changed.

Backing Adarus was a mistake, but a necessary one to progress. And now I've found a willing teacher in Allari. I ask for your help again, {name}. Help me find the strength to protect my people.

title: A More Potent Foe
description:
We have what we need.

Prepare yourself to see Illidan's confrontation with the naaru so many years ago.

title: The Mark of Sacrifice
description:
The tattoos that mark our flesh are more than just ink. They're a promise inscribed across our flesh.

The promise that we will hold back the dark and the infernal with our very bodies. That our lifeblood is a currency used to purchase Azeroth's future.

Surprised? We are not simply rage and defiance manifest, although we are also that.

Help Leona collect the vitae of the void creatures at Nexus-Point Antius. We'll use it to inscribe her tattoos, affirming the vow she makes to protect her people.

title: One Cruel Implement
description:
Voidstorm is a world of apex creatures. One in particular, the ultradon, stands above others in its strength and ferocity.

But make no mistake, {name}. We demon hunters are the true apex predators wherever we go, because we are willing to go to any length in order to dominate. I see no better creature to harvest an implement in which to inscribe Leona's tattoos.

Head to Nexus-Point Antius with Leona. Defeat as many ultradons as it takes to find the perfect implement.

title: Dark Infusion
description:
The branding ritual we're about to perform is adapted from what demon hunters go through on the Fel Hammer. But given Leona's unique nature and the experimental substitution of Void energy for fel, there's considerable risk here. Even more so than usual.

Leona will be vulnerable during this process, as will I. We've found a secluded area to perform it, but we'll need your protection once we start.

title: Event Horizon
description:
It may not surprise you to know that the stabilization of portal entry points is something I'm quite familiar with.

If Leona lives, and I believe she does, she'll need beacons to help triangulate her exit point. Take these beacons, set them up where I've marked on your map, and return here.

If Leona is alive, she should be able to use these beacons to identify and stabilize her exit.

If she isn't--

<Umbric pushes the thought away.>

Let's focus on the task at hand for now.

title: Devourer
description:
I'm not sure where I was when I stepped through that portal, {name}. Somewhere in between, Void energy surrounding and suffusing me.

Without Umbric, I wouldn't have made it back. I feel different now, but I don't think this is over until we confront that domanaar.

Will you meet us at the Voidspire?

title: Scholarly Pursuits
description:
{name}... a favor.

My research partner Matrean and I are studying the local wildlife and their relationship to the Void.

I've concluded my findings, but Matrean--reckless and obsessive as ever--insists there is more to discover.

Can you help convince him to return here?

title: A Bigger Beast
description:
Return to Umbric? But we're on the brink of deeper understandings!

Hypothesis: there exists an apex predator amongst these creatures that might hold immense power! And this predator's relationship with the Void could further our own understanding of the infinite darkness.

Eradicate some of the smaller creatures and see if such a beast answers the call of violence!

title: Sampling the Local Fare
description:
This place is a food chain of ferocity and far too many teeth.

Our studies grow more dangerous by the minute, but if Matrean insists on staying, we may as well assist him.

We're studying a link between the nature of these creatures and the Void's accumulation here. 

Dispatch some of the beasts below and bring samples of their tissue back to me. We can study them to learn more of their connection with the Void.

title: Yolks on You
description:
Our research has proven fruitful. I can feel the promise of power beckoning. Do you hear it, {name}? The apex predator calls to us. The beast must be nearby.

We must gather tribute. Yes... we must offer it a gift of vitality! We must meet it bathed in the essence of the young...

Some of the smaller creatures are nesting in the tunnels below. Break a few eggs and bathe in their juices. Then, perhaps, the beast will be drawn to the smell!

title: Void is in the Air
description:
We void elves all face the same risk... that should our resilience falter, madness awaits.

<Kelia frowns in Matrean's direction.>

The caves below are steeped in Void's vile touch. Void Surges erupt from the earth, creating a miasma of filth that fills the nesting grounds.

<She hands you a magical device.>

Take this. It will capture the surges of dark energy bursting from the soil. We will study them safely back at the Ingress.

title: Violent Conclusions
description:
Our research here is complete.

Conclusion: the Void is attracted to this realm due to its violent nature! The Voidstorm is a melting pot of torment and savagery. The Void was drawn to this aura of endless suffering.

This place is a dire threat to the ren'dorei. However, our mission here is important. Let us take our findings back to the others.

Can you help me snap Matrean out of it?

title: Honorin' de Sacrifice
description:
It's de hash'ura again. You willin' to help me, or does Zul'jarra got you on a short leash?

No? Then I got a job for you. One of my guards, Idago, was killed in de massacre at Revantusk Sedge. I wish to honor him with funeral rites. We Revantusk burn de bodies of our dead. But we don't have Idago's body.

So, I'm goin' back to de sedge to retrieve somethin' of his. We'll burn dat instead.

My honor guard Pel'na will not go and says it is too dangerous, but you'll keep me safe, won'tcha?

title: What Remains of Idago
description:
Idago was my most trusted guard. He was a mentor to me for as long as I remember. I never thought dat he would be taken from me so soon--and then de Twilight's Blade attacked us without warnin'.

Idago is de one that saved me from de village. He left his spear and his banner behind. I also left a note he gave me in case he...

Bring me those items. With dem, we can honor his strength and his loyalty--and send his spirit on.

title: Disruptin' de Blade
description:
Look at what these cultists did to Revantusk Sedge!

Dis was once a place of peace... dis was our home. An' dey are desecrating it.

I know we're here to retrieve Idago's things, but I won't be bothered if you take up arms against de Twilight's Blade.

Put dem in de ground. Show dem de ferocity of de bear.

title: Spears Against de Shadow
description:
Idago's still alive, {name}!

I saw him dragged up into de crypt, bound in magic runes. 

De cultists say dey are takin' him to some Deathchanter Mish'mosh. Dis cultist must be behind the death and destruction brought to our sedge.

For the honor of the people we lost, root this snake out of our home!

title: Simply Magical
description:
Who... Who are you? Who sent you? Lilaju?

De cultists massacred our village. I stayed behind to make sure everyone got out.

But de cultists captured me and now dey say dey got somethin' extra special for Idago. I'd rather not find out what.

Dis magic holdin' me here is amateur work. De spells to release de bindings are sure to be in de magical tomes de cultists brought here.

Find de tomes and rip some pages out. Then we can piece together de right words to release me.

title: The Wisest Leaders Follow
description:
Idago reminded me dat my first obligation is to my tribe. I missed him dearly, but it was not right for me to put my own life--or yours--at risk to find him.

Pel'na tried to tell me this. I would not listen.

I must be a model for my people. I will allow de bear loa to guide my direction. Sometimes we must sacrifice de things we hold dear.

Nalorakk grant me strength and resilience.

title: Harvest of Darkness
description:
You... make yourself useful.

As a master of terrible beasts, it is my job to mold these hatchlings into dreaded beasts. I pit them against each other and they grow ever more violent.

But the elves here insist that I will yield better results with just a bit of... tender loving care. Let us put this to the test.

This runt is Fidoficus. You will show him tenderness and affection, and then we will see how ferocious he becomes.

Collect some delicious dietary treats for him and return to me.

title: Belly of the Beast
description:
I normally raise my beasts on a diet of raw, bloody carcasses and souls ripped asunder by the Void.

But we shall try your mortal ways.

I hear you... cook? And that makes the treat better?

Do this. Then, take him on a walk around the Howling Ridge. Even I know exercise is good for digestion.

Only the most ferocious of creatures is worthy of serving me. I hope neither you nor Fido disappoint me.

title: Mighty and Superior
description:
The beast is fed and exercised. Imagine, mortal... one day his maw will grow to fit you inside it a thousand times over. 

Now, we will test his potential for true violence. 

There is a beast that has laid claim to a den south of the Howling Ridge. 

Fidoficus trusts you now. Go with him and bear witness to his might.

title: The Illusion of Motion
description:
You! Have YOU seen them?

I've been tasked to investigate some of our scouts that have gone missing. My reconnaissance had come up empty until recently.

Now I've been catching glimpses of the missing scouts, but... they appear formed of pure Void energy.

Each time I chase the figures, they lead me further and further before disappearing!

The others think I'm crazy. You haven't seen anything like that, have you?

title: Drain You
description:
Do you... see...? They follow... They plead... for help...

<The voidwalker strains and shudders as it tries to communicate.>

Too weak... too starving...

<It feebly hands you a shard of dark matter.>

This... will feed me... then together... we find the missing elves...

title: Voices of Omens
description:
The voices of your elven allies rattle inside my head.

I know of a way we can find them. There is a way to locate missing things... using old domanaar scanning devices.

But they require immense energy to function. Together, we can use these devices to focus the images of your lost friends. Their cries for help will become clearer, and then you can find them.

Come. Lend me your strength.

title: Dominion of Deceit
description:
We were right to be cautious. The threats we've found here in the Voidstorm are corrupting and irredeemable.

This voidwalker's meticulous deceptions are unusual... I can't help but wonder why it would go through all the trouble. 

<She looks up at you from weary, withered eyes.>

But I'm too weak to go on. It really did a number on me. You have the upper hand now! It thinks you're walking into its trap.

Walk in with weapons drawn and spells ready!

title: Distant Memories
description:
I swore an oath to honor my family... difficult when my sister was converted to the Shadowguard. She may not be here, but tracking the Shadowguard has led me to this place. 

I'm looking for a necklace that belonged to my sister Veraan. It's odd... I've noticed many K'areshi heirlooms discarded around here. It seems the Shadowguard wish to destroy any reminders of our home world.

If you bring some of these trinkets and baubles to me, we can see if there are any I recognize.

title: Oaths and Heirlooms
description:
You, stranger... do you know of the Shadowguard that left K'aresh in ruins?

My sister was lost in the conflict, her heart forcibly turned to the Void, but I swore oaths to my family... to cherish them no matter their transgressions.

My sister, Veraan, is surely lost, but if I were to recover her necklace, at least then my oaths would be upheld in spirit.

I am a simple tinkerer. I've tuned this device to track the Shadowguard, but I cannot face them alone.

Would you help me?

title: Fits of Lucidity
description:
While I've been lurking, I've been listening.

These Shadowguard appear to have a minor rebellion on their hands. Some of the ethereals are acting erratically. They're being rounded up and questioned.

What's going on here?

Dispatch some of these Shadowguard and intercept their intel. I'll decode anything you bring back to me.

title: Truth From Power
description:
Thanks to your intel, we have learned that the rebels are being interrogated by Questioner Xen'daan.

It is said that after being questioned, the rebel ethereals are being taken somewhere to be "dealt with."

The head questioner is sure to know more. If we can find where they've taken the rebels, there may still be hope for them.

title: She Started the Fire
description:
Veraan is sure to be amongst the rebels taken to Shadowguard Point.

Her fiery spirit fills me with great pride!

I came here expecting to say a farewell of sorts. I did not come here expecting to find my sister.

Perhaps it's not too late, {name}!

title: Eating Their Own
description:
The Shadowguard seem to have no qualms caging and torturing their own kind.

The last thing I want is more violence amongst the people of K'aresh, but better them than us.

Disrupt their operation by subduing some of the Shadowguard.

title: Techno-Magnetic Pulse
description:
The betrayers are being held in stasis chambers.

I've seen these cages before. Their design is efficient but fragile. They will fail with the slightest energy variation.

Find crackling energy vessels strewn about and heave them at the stasis chambers. With true aim, you will disable them and free the betrayers.

title: Bursting at the Seams
description:
Veraan... alive...

<Kifaan shakes his head in disbelief.>

I've spent countless hours running this moment through my head, but now that it's here, I find myself grasping for words...

The Shadowguard attempted to raise Dimensius to power... they've sworn fealty to Xal'atath as she wages war on worlds. Am I supposed to just... pretend none of that happened? Is THAT what my oaths to family require?

And what of Veraan? Does she regret her involvement?

I should check on her.

title: Repress the Oppressors
description:
We were brought here to face the wrath of Soulbinder D'zaar. She commands the Shadowguard here. She leverages the power of the Voidstorm to convert souls to energy that she consumes.

She and her loyalists are traitors to our kind!

<Veraan grimaces in pain as the Void courses through her.>

They must be stopped... their devices dismantled... their will ended...

{name}... destroy them all!

title: Shedding the Yoke
description:
Rebels like us are brought to Soulbinder D'zaar. She suffers no betrayers.

Our fate was to be sacrificed--our souls converted to energy. D'zaar works the conduit with arcane mastery to absorb these souls!

We don't have much time. I can feel the Void pressing from within. If we are to leave this life violently, D'zaar and her conduit are coming with us!

title: Facing the Sun
description:
This sanctum was studying the Lightbloom well before the incident at Fairbreeze. We had no reason to be concerned over their research up until that point.

However, the last message we attempted to send never received a reply. And this doesn't look good now that we're here.

We need to clear out some of the wildlife first, though. We cannot risk any contamination.

title: Scattered in Sunbeams
description:
We've given the garden tenders some space to continue their work unimpeded. Hopefully they can keep this place pristine until Silvermoon has the chance to fix it up.

But now we need to figure out where the missing scholars are. They were studying mana influence on plants in the gardens toward the back. Perhaps we'll find a sign of them there.

title: Gardener Mishap
description:
The sanctum door seems to be locked. Probably from the inside.

I believe an arcane construct is used for heavy lifting and general upkeep alongside the treants. The core of it should override the lock on the door and work as a master key. I doubt it will just let us take it, so be careful.

title: The Light Provides
description:
We need to get into the Sanctum. Now.

Burn away the roots on that door! And be prepared--nothing good can be behind it.

title: Graveblossom Gardening
description:
My colleague Amalthea has taken ill. She requires a specialized poultice of sorts in order to ease the symptoms. I would forage for the plants myself, but the local fauna have grown so testy as of late!

While you are out and about, could you gather some graveblossoms for me? I'd happily make it worth your while.

title: A Venomous Vocation
description:
My poultice requires venom samples from the local giant bat populations. Please retrieve some of those, as well. Six common vials' worth should do.

Don't give me that look. They're bats. There are mad Light-plant-things to the east and a storm of voidwalkers swirling over our heads. Don't you dare give me guff about the dangers of big, fluffy bats. Now shoo. My colleague is depending on your prompt and efficient aid.

title: Suspicious Sundries
description:
Forgive me for my failure to provide specifics, traveler. The mournbat venom and the graveblossoms were only the first of the reagents I require for my associate's medication.

Luckily, the final few things I require can all be easily obtained in Goldenmist Village to the north. Be a dear and fetch these last few things for me, won't you? With these, my toil will be nearly complete.

title: House Call
description:
That woman, that "apothecary," has snared you in one of her schemes.

I'd be careful around her, {class}. Rumors travel like wildfire in Eversong. And while I would never disparage a fellow artisan of Eversong without cause, I have heard enough rumors to know that your apothecary deals in more than simple herbs.

Take your wine and your reagents, I suppose. But before you pass them off to your patroness, I'd recommend doing some digging--lest you find yourself wrapped up in something you come to regret.

title: Flowers for Amalthea
description:
<The final pages of the tome detail a meeting place. Whatever Talandra Dawnsprite intends to do, she means to do it amidst the ghosts of Windrunner Spire.

The time for Talandra's ritual grows nigh.>

title: The Brewing Storm
description:
Our foothold in Stormarion is tenuous at best. We're barely holding the enemy off on one front. We certainly don't have the forces to assault the rest of the citadel.

That's where you and I come in. Meet me in Stormarion Citadel when you're ready to get to work.

title: Tactical Acquisition
description:
All our forces are focused on this courtyard, but I think we've got another problem. I've scouted between assaults: there's activity in the lower keep. Not to worry you, but there are signs of another singularity.

Up for a little reconnaissance? We'll go through the trench. They've got an impressive operation for gathering Void energy. Might as well hit that on the way. After all, our defenses here can't power themselves.

If I'm wrong, we can tell Maella we were proactively shoring up supply lines.

title: Resource Denial
description:
I've got a hunch I need to follow in the lower keep, and that means going through the trench. I hate to miss targets of opportunity, and the Host's energy collecting operation is right there.

How do you feel about wrecking their production capacity? The longer it takes them to recover, the easier it'll be to maintain the offensive up here.

It's not complicated. If it looks useful to the enemy, smash it. After that, we'll move onto the main objective in the keep itself.

title: Null Implements
description:
Yes, there may be another unstable singularity. No, I didn't tell anyone. We don't have enough forces to divert our attention--that's why I asked you to accompany me. Besides, we've only got the one Singularity Anchor.

Don't worry though, I've got a plan. These domanaar are experts on shaping the Void, so they must have some means of controlling singularities. Get in there and grab any tools that look important.

We'll find a way to stabilize this singularity ourselves.

title: Hammer Meet Anvil
description:
I don't approve of Lysikas's disregard for duty, but I do trust his instincts. If he says there's something here we need to see, then he's probably right.

Even if he's wrong, we shouldn't waste this opportunity. A domanaar weaponsmith and researcher does his work from here. With most of the Stormarion forces attacking our Singularity Anchor, he should be an easy target. Take him out.

title: Core Collapse
description:
This singularity could reach critical mass at any moment. We've no time to evacuate. It will devour our forces in the upper citadel, maybe even the Ingress.

Will you handle the tools? I've no idea how they work, but you've used more artifacts and seen more strange magic than most. Remember, the Void responds to will. Use the tools and command the singularity to stabilize. I trust you'll figure out the rest.

The Host will try to stop us. Lay down some defenses, then I'll cover you while you work.

title: Message to the Molt
description:
Ah, champion! There are so many unique specimens to identify and catalogue here that I can't keep up.

Speaking of which, Lady Darkglen took time out of her busy schedule and coordinated an expedition to a particularly dangerous region north of here. I trust in her power, but I've not heard from her or her team in a while and am growing concerned. With any luck, they are simply caught up in their own research.

That being said, would you please find Lady Darkglen in the Molt and check in on her?

title: Pestilent Petals
description:
A strange pathogenic presence has infected my fellow ren'dorei.

When we arrived, I was awestruck by the colorful vibrancy of this decay-filled valley and its many blood-stained flowers. I had hoped to gather some for my collection... to study of course, but this pathogen was living in the same pestilent pools these blood petals thrive in. Are they naturally resilient, or do they have purifying qualities we can harness for a cure?

Dear {name}, please, cautiously gather blood petals for my research.

title: Virulent Vermin
description:
The pathogen appears to spread unevenly among the Molt's wildlife.

When my fellow ren'dorei were infected, they grew confused and unresponsive. The local fauna appear more resistant. I suspect some of the Molt's denizens have a stronger resistance than others. Perhaps by examining these beasts we can extract elements for a curative elixir.

Could you gather livers from the creatures nearby?

title: Expunging Explorers
description:
Let us hope we are able to concoct a cure from the reagents you acquired.

I have already prepared the base potion, but now we need to carefully mix in the blood petals and livers you procured. With some calculated determination and a bit of luck, we should be able to craft an antidote and administer it to the afflicted ren'dorei scouts nearby.

{name}, assist Blocciniux by mixing in the gathered ingredients.

title: Bloodborne Pathogen
description:
The bloodborne pathogen has mutated!

The ooze-like carriers of this pathogen usually die quickly, but this new variant is stable even without a host. If it escapes from here, it could infect more ren'dorei and spread across Voidstorm, or worse yet, Azeroth. Our entire operation is at risk.

We must stop this mutated variant before it spreads!

title: Calculated Culling
description:
This pathogen is spreading rapidly among the denizens of the Molt.

A swarm of netherwasp hatched shortly after our arrival. The infection spread throughout the region as they fought over territory. If their numbers continue to grow, they could carry the pathogen to the rest of Voidstorm.

{name}, can I ask you to reduce the number of infected creatures in the Molt?

title: It's Not Just a Rock!
description:
{name}, I have something strange, here.

While scouting in the Storm Fields, I found this rock a beast was chewing. Look at it! Could these be runes? Writing?

But so ancient? Here? What could it mean?

Strangely, I think I've been hearing a voice in my head since I found this thing...

We must investigate further. Maybe we can find more of this rubble in the bellies of those beasts?

title: Buried in the Dark
description:
We're at the brink of a monumental discovery! Hurry, there's no time to even call for backup. We must find this strange spirit!

With this residue, I can make a spell to adjust the vortex. It'll lead us to the source of this engraved rubble.

Based on the specter's guidance, I believe we'll find an entire cave.

Be ready, {name}.

title: Sifting Through Void
description:
There's no rubble on the surface. The beasts are burrowing, so they must be finding it in a cavern underground.

I've been thinking of how to get there.

You've noticed the vortexes that dot the Storm Fields? They twist space, moving things from place to place.

Step through one and I can reveal the anomalous residue that surrounds them.

Collect the residue and I can make a spell to redirect the portal to where the source of the rubble is!

title: In Over My Head
description:
Akintunde created a weapon to end all intelligent life on this world, eons and eons ago?

I hardly believe it.

But truth or lie, we can't leave this spirit be. He's clearly dangerous.

If he truly knows how to create such a weapon, we can't let him contact anyone from Azeroth. We both know a few warmongers who would do anything for that kind of power...

I think I can realign the vortex again to take us directly to Akintunde. Ready yourself for a fight, {name}.

title: Light Disturbance
description:
We have had victories against the Lightbloom, but Alndust is rare. Until we can gather more, the Lightbloom continues its spread. Our forces are stretched too thin to watch their every movement.

A scout spotted a strange corpse by the road to the east, along with a report of a general disturbance of the light-cursed creatures. I can't look into both at once. Could you find that corpse while I investigate deeper into the Encroaching Bloom?

title: Light Stroll
description: <These potatoad creatures are seldom seen near the Lightbloom. It is covered in spores and looks like it was trying to escape. You should search for more clues.>

title: Light Carnage
description:
The Lightbloom has infected many of these potatoads and spurred them into a frenzy. They spread the spores and reproduce at a dangerous rate.

If we don't act, we will be overrun. Will you cull this heard while I search for the source of the corruption?

title: Potatoad Tots
description:
It is too late for the adults, but the young may still be saved. If you can capture them, we can remove the spores before they take root.

Gather up the spore covered potadpoles and bring them to me. I will take them to Har'alnor myself to purge this Lightbloom.

Do not let their first breaths be their last. Please, {name}: save the babies.

title: O.K. Bloomer
description:
I have discovered the lair of a cunning creature I call Adzikel. With luck, this name will soon be forgotten. 

Adzikel sneaks into the potatoad nests to steal their young, and plants the Lightbloom spores to accelerate their growth into the frenzied toads, which spread more spores.

For the little ones to have a chance, I must return to Har'alnor, but you have earned the right to slay this beast. Go with honor, and finish the hunt!

title: Growing Up is Hard
description:
Oh, hi! You're new. I'm Ani. Dis is Jynx.

Have you been to de waterfall yet? All de kids dare each other to jump off, but I'm too scared.

Do you want to see it?

title: I Think I Can
description:
Yeah, it's pretty far down.

But if I don't jump, dey'll make fun of me. Maybe if you cheer me on, I can do it?

title: Not According to Plan
description:
<Ani's trinket bag sits in the soft mud of the river bank, but her trinkets are nowhere to be found.

If you're lucky, you may be able to follow her trail.>

title: Fearless
description:
Those lizards were waitin' at de bottom and dragged me off!

Did you find my toys and stuff? I tried droppin' dem so someone could find me. I can't believe dat worked!

I think you scared dem off. It should be safe enough for Jynx to help me out of here.

title: The Silence at Fungara Village
description:
Fungara Village has been silent for some time now.

We've largely been occupied with the Lightbloom and the threat it presents, so we haven't had time to check in on the fungarians to the east. Usually, they are rowdy yet manageable... but it's rare that they go completely silent.

I am going to investigate. If you wish to prove yourself an ally, you would do well to join me.

title: Little Monsters
description:
When I sent a request for aid from Har'alnor, I did not expect them to send me an outsider. No matter.

The denizens of Fungara Village have gone quiet. Normally, I would be thrilled: they're horribly screechy. But the way they are shambling about... it is like they have forgotten that they yet live.

<Ghikal shudders.>

I tried to get close, but they attacked me on sight.

I will need samples of their flesh. Go and bring them to me, so I can see what has happened here.

title: Spawn of the Dead
description:
It is obvious that something terrible has happened in the village. I can see the corpses of their largest specimens strewn across the southern reaches from here, but something is off about them too. Fungarians don't usually just leave the corpses of their kin lying about.

And though I am watching from afar, I swear that some of them are... twitching.

Investigate. Find out what you can, and report back to me.

title: You Are Legend
description:
Whatever these things are, they're not fungarians. Not anymore. 

Har'alnor has enough to worry about with the Lightbloom as it is. It cannot afford to be trapped between its forces and these... things.

Do what must be done. Destroy as many of them as you can.

title: Decayed Land
description:
As far as I can tell, these fungarians are all already dead. Something is animating their corpses from within, like puppets attached to unseen vines.

This fungal matter that's infesting them must be coming from somewhere... and judging from the size of the ones further north, we'll find plenty of it there. Find it, get rid of it, but for Aln'hara's sake don't breathe it in.

title: Reticent Evil
description:
I do not understand. We've searched high and low, north and south. Everywhere we go, we find animated corpses and small fruiting fungus, but where is the central body? Where is--

Oh, goddess. Of course.

The water, {class}. Check the water.

title: Dusk Among Pigments
description:
As Zur'ashar, our oath is to safeguard the harmony within the visions and the histories we preserve.

Yet Orn'shan's crude strokes are reckless. Their creative impulses lack refinement, disturbing the balance we are sworn to protect. In a misguided attempt at creativity, they've created uncontrollable chaos and are distorting the very truths they sought to record.

Perhaps they will heed the words of an outsider before their reputation collapses entirely.

title: The Stroke of Storms
description:
I... I don't think I can mend this. The Zur'ashar were right. I am no true paintmaker. I cannot even capture history properly within my own memories... how could I ever hope to honor it in moments that matter?

I thought I could create something beautiful... something worthy. I was foolish to try.

Can you... can you help me clean this mess? I... I have no hope left to do it myself.

title: Colors Born Anew
description:
Outsider... thank you. I'm still shaken... but I can feel a small spark returning. I'd nearly abandoned my work entirely, yet your kindness... it has steadied me.

I think I finally see what I'm meant to create: something meaningful that honors the Zur'ashar tradition through my voice, but to begin, I need the raw essence of color itself.

Will you help me collect the ingredients I need? I'd be looking for anything I can use to turn into paint.

title: Hues of Tomorrow
description:
I... never expected to feel this again. Your help has given me hope. These ingredients feel right, and maybe even enough.

I want you to be the first to paint. Do not fret over perfection. Paint what you feel, and I'll capture its essence into the memory I preserve.

I look forward to see what you create.

title: A Hunter's Plight
description:
I am desperate enough to ask an outsider for help.

To join the Ka'dani, the elite hunters of Har'athir, you must complete a ritual under the guidance of an experienced Ka'dani. However, there are none left to guide me, so I cannot take the trial.

But I have heard words of your actions. Maybe Akazi, the Ka'dani Elder, would let you stand as my guide. You may not know the ritual, but you know the hunt.

Please, let us speak to Elder Akazi. We must try.

title: A Hunter's Duty
description:
At the start of the trial, the mentor and trainee gather materials to forge a hunting weapon.

The weapon is more than an extension of the Ka'dani-- it's part of their being. There is no other weapon like it, as each is unique.

You will help Ketan hunt and gather the materials and decorations, as well as a gift for the weaponsmith.

title: A Hunter's Weapon
description:
