Forging the Ka'dani weapon is a crucial moment in the trial. We will have to follow Al'yaat's instructions.

Once Al'yaat forges the weapon, you will bring it to Elder Akazi.

There will be a short ceremony where he will hand me the weapon. The ceremony concludes with the elder announcing our final task.

I shouldn't let the excitement take the better of me, but one thing at a time. We must be patient and calculated as if we were on a hunt.

title: A Hunter's Prey
description:
The last trial stands before you. Together, you have forged a weapon that will fell any beast, but the weapon is nothing without the hunter.

Since you are not a Ka'dani, I present you with a difficult hunt. Ketan and you will hunt down and slay a prey known to all Har'athir hunters: the great Radooni. The beast ventured too close to Har'athir and should be a suitable hunt.

Return to me once your hunt is complete and Radooni's sap is on the spear.

title: Drift Them Away
description:
With the hydras hunted out of the area, the drifter population in the cascades to the west is on the rise. Egg sacks are making the water murky and undrinkable, and the drifters are becoming very territorial.

We must ensure that the reintroduction of the hydras will succeed. We need to cull the number of drifters and clear some of their egg sacks.

You look like someone capable of doing this task. Will you help me?

title: Feeding the Buds
description:
The orchid hydra's favorite food is the violet chloroceros--especially the buds on the bull's back.

With the hydras gone for so long, the violet chloroceros grazed too much of the fauna. Soon, there will only be mud in that once lush and beautiful part of Harandar.

I want to introduce my hydras to their primary source of food, and I would appreciate it if you could bring me four of those buds.

title: Re-Hydra-ted
description:
With the previous tasks done, it is time to reintroduce the budlings to the wilds.

I have marked suitable locations to place the budlings. The little hydras will be an easy pick, so expect predators to attack you.

I am sad to see them go. However, I know it is for the ecosystem's and their own good.

title: Gnarldin Trophies
description:
I was sent here to prove dat I am ready to be an Amani warrior. All dat want de honor must prove demselves.

I chose de gnarldin since dey are close now, and I heard Zul'jarra softened dem up a little.

Looks like I wasn't de only one who thought dat and was wrong.

But I still need to collect as many trophies from de gnarldin as possible.

If I have de bigshot hash'ura helpin' me, there's no way I can lose, no?

You get de trophies. I will stay behind to watch our backs.

title: Scavenged Victory
description:
Many warriors have chosen to face de gnarldin since dey came to Zul'Aman, but many have failed.

Dey thought now dat de gnarldin are in our territory and weakened, dey would be easier...

De gnarldin hunters have collected tablets from de Amani hunters dat fell here.

But with you at my side, we can get "vengeance" for those dat lost their lives here--no killin' or life riskin' required.

We don't have to kill de gnarldin. We just have to get close enough to gather de tablets. What do you say?

title: Bitter Fury
description:
De leader of de gnarldin here is known as Tusk Taker. Take a wild guess why she's named dat.

There is a trophy dis one has in a cave. Take it, and she will run out rarin' for a fight.

Defeat her for me, and no one can challenge my right to be an Amani warrior.

title: Amani Honor
description:
Dat is all de trophies we need.

I'll take dem to de meetin' point nearby. All we gotta do is wait there for Huntmaster Jinki to show up.

Come!

title: Butchery Basics
description:
We can't keep up with how much these soldiers eat. Lend us just a bit of your time to feed dis army, hm?

We need boar. Not just a steak or two, we need de entire boar. Nothin' goes to waste, yah?

title: Got No Rhythm
description:
Dat boy Kovu stormed out of here without even tryin' a single step. He thinks he's got two left feet, but he isn't even willin' to try.

You look like an impressive fighter. He'll respect you for dat.

Maybe he just needs de right dance partner to show him de steps. Help him out, hm?

title: Homework Support
description:
<The little girl keeps her voice low, making sure her father doesn't overhear.>

My friends are goin' out to play down where de shade lilies grow, but I'm supposed to do a buncha borin' apothecary stuff.

Papa wants me to collect some plants to make salves and balms we use for healin'. I asked if it could wait and he said no.

What if... you helped me out? Just dis once, pleeease?

title: Altar History
description:
De altars need to be infused with de power of de loas every so often.

I'm not as spry as I used to be and de altars are all spread out.

Help an old shaman out, eh? I'll give you my totem to channel de loas' power into de altars.

And maybe you can learn a thing or two from de other acolytes and myself in de process.

title: The Final Exam
description:
My trainin' is almost complete!

My commander says I need to prove myself against a strong fighter before she can recommend me for combat.

But I've been strugglin' to find someone willin' to spar with me after... uh, after what happened de last time...

You look capable. And intimidatin'. Mind helpin' me out here?

And no goin' easy on me, yes? I can take it.

title: Revantusk at Risk
description:
Fishers of de forest tribes constantly talk to each other about our hauls, and none are more legendary than de river-walking Revantusk and their crabs! But...

I've heard they've fallen on hard times. First, de Twilight's Blade ransacked their city. Now, their cages aren't bringing in enough meat for de refugees.

De Revantusk have a new head river-walker, Kahanea, who could use an extra set of hands in taking care of dis crab problem.

You up to de task, outsider?

title: Crab Clues
description:
De rumors are true. No matter what we try, we aren't netting as many crabs as before. And de ones we do catch taste wrong, even making some of my people sick!

I succeeded my mother's position as head river-walker after losing her to de Twilight's Blade. Dealing with dis famine, my new duties, and my grief... It's so much for me to bear.

Dis is de area where we first noticed an issue with our crabs. Can you investigate for me while I make more traps?

title: Clobbering Crawlers
description:
While I continue to scout de area for causes of dis famine, please harvest crab meat for our refugees. Their hunger only gets worse as our investigation goes on.

Since de traps aren't working, you have to hunt those crabs yourself and get food straight from de source!

Make sure you're getting healthy meat, though. I'm worried about some of those crabs being infected with de Void.

title: Caging Crawlers
description:
Our traps being mucked up with de Void would explain why we've been catching fewer crabs! And why some of de crabs have made our people sick.

Please clean our traps upriver! Based on Nak'tek's report, de infected crabs sound dangerous... so be careful approaching those traps.

You can find de baskets nestled among de sticks or by bases of waterfalls--dey provide a natural disguise! Crabs are easily tricked dat way.

title: A Crab of Unusual Size
description:
I followed de Void pollution trail upriver and spotted a beast before it ran off to its cave: an enormous, void-addled crab. It was shedding those shards dat were polluting de river!

As long as dis beast lives, it'll keep spreading pollution and infecting crabs with de Void. It must be stopped!

I'd stay and fight, but I must warn everyone at de Overlook.

Please, {name}. Slay dat crab and remove de source of its corruption before it can hurt more of my people!

title: Blind the Bandits
description:
Dis elf showed up here seekin' revenge, thinkin' we were responsible for de death of his sister.

But instead of killin' him, I'm gonna set de boy straight.

De Amani got no business with some farm!

He don't understand de Twilight's Blade is attackin' on both sides of de Zul'Aman border!

I don't blame de child. He don't know dat de Twilight's Blade can hide demselves with their void powers.

Take some lightwood splinters. Throw 'em on de shrouded Twilight's Blade. Show him who de real enemy is!

title: Salvaged Sabotage
description:
Rakani overreacted when de boy came crashin' into camp, but we all have been on edge.

De Twilight's Blade had already snuck into our camp and sabotaged our work.

It has already been challengin' to keep up with de demand for weapons. And our people need de weapons to defend themselves!

If we can get de broken weapons back, we can maybe make up for what we lost.

While you deal with dem, see if you can get any of our weapons back--whatever condition dey are in.

title: The Artisan's Apprentice
description:
Looks like de boy has realized there is more in dis world than his family farm an' de big bad trolls of Zul'Aman.

But I'm not done with him yet. He gonna learn hard work too. De boy needs to make up for de disruption he caused and de work we lost.

It'll be a learnin' opportunity--for de both of you.

You are goin' to make somethin' for us.

Pay attention. One wrong move can destroy de lightwood. With de Twilight's Blade sabotagin' our equipment, our resources are too limited already.

title: Another One Bites the Sawdust
description:
Dat boy is a fool.

But he's a courageous fool.

It's time to test dis weapon you crafted.

He'll be goin' after their commander. He's killed many of my crafters, but revenge is not worth his life or dat of anyone else.

Go after him before he gets himself killed.

I did not go through all dis trouble just for him to die now, eh?

title: Personal History
description:
With Mor'duun slain and de Twilight's Blade defeated, my people want to return to Atal'Aman.

It should be a moment of great celebration but... somethin' unexpected is barrin' our way.

Seek out Torundo and Elder Doru. Dey are supportin' de resettlement. They'll know how best to use your talents.

Prepare yourself, hash'ura, and do not hesitate to defend Atal'Aman no matter who you meet there.

title: A Quiet Walk Interrupted
description:
It is de hash'ura! De favored of our wily loa! Maybe you can help...

My father, Oban, is ill and I have been givin' him all my attention.

When I turned my back an hour ago, my daughter, Kanza, had run off!

I searched de village, but I can't go further and leave my father behind.

Please, find where she went? She can be on her own, but she's a sensitive girl, and I don't want her goin' far.

Dat fool boy Haz'kel wouldn't come down, but he may have seen her leave. Ask him first.

title: Childlike Devotion
description:
She's goin' to Filo's shrine. She's too old for dat. She's almost thirteen--an adult.

Filo is de Loa of Childhood and protects children. Parents pray to Filo for healthy children.

It is even said if a child dies, Filo waits with a child in de afterlife until their parents claim dem.

Dat is superstition, but dat's why she's goin'.

We lost her twin, Janaka. She must think she's with Filo.

The way to Filo's shrine is dangerous, and dat loa won't speak to her. Do what you can to bring her home!

title: Shrine Preparations
description:
<Kanza makes no sound but briefly meets your gaze. She points off toward the hot spring pools.

Maybe she wants you to do something there?>

title: Temple and a Teapot
description:
<Kanza is dancing with excitement.

She holds her small hand with one finger up.

One last thing to do.

You must almost be ready for the ritual to summon Filo--if that is indeed what she is asking for your help to do.

She fishes in her tunic for something and presents an object to you: the broken handle of a teapot, presumably from the one she dropped on the way here.

Perhaps she needs a substitute?>

title: Healing Homeward
description: I will go home.

title: The Story of Wey'nan's Ward
description:
You have walked this path before, {name}, yet the Vale of Secrets stirs anew.

The Visionstone awaits, its sacred paintings familiar to your eyes, the handprint at its heart calling for you again.
Press your hand upon it once more and let your spirit be known. The trance will take you as before, carrying your soul through visions whose truths still shift with every telling.

Return to me with what new wisdom the echoes grant you.

title: The Story of the Cauldron of Echoes
description:
Once more, the Cauldron of Echoes stirs.

When I first walked the path of the Zur'Ashar, my master spoke of its place at our beginning. Even they could not grasp its full meaning. Now, it beckons again.

Step into its quiet and draw forth what the ages have hidden. Bring me the truth as it speaks to you this time.

title: The Story of Aln'hara's Bloom
description:
The bloom still waits, its stillness carrying the same quiet call you once followed.

Yet the bloom's story deepens with each telling. Walk its path again, let the stillness speak anew, and see what more the goddess chooses to share.

title: The Story of the Echoless Flame
description:
The Echoless Flame rests as you left it, yet the air around it stirs once more.

You have walked Obayo's path before, felt the echo of that first Shul'ka's purpose. But the past is a shifting thing, and the Flame's silence may yet hide verses you have not heard.

Return to its vigil. Follow Obayo's steps again, and let the vision show you what remains untold.

title: The Story of Russula's Outreach
description:
The spear still waits where you last left it, pale threads of mushroom slowly weaving new patterns into its grain.

The culling grounds draw you again. Walk their softened paths, follow what spores still rise, and let the visions show what time has chosen to reveal this season.

title: The Story of the Root of the World
description:
The Root of the World stands as it did before--ancient, unbowed--yet the soil breathes differently today. Beneath your feet, the pulse of Harandar quickens, as though eager to share another verse of its oldest story.

You have seen through Ruilla the Huntress's eyes once before. But the Rootways are never walked the same way twice. Go again, and let the land decide which truths it will show you now.

title: The Story of Sky's Hope
description:
Sky's Hope rests in its cradle as before, yet the heavens above seem sharper tonight.

You have traced Awe'ohna's path once, but the tale shifts with every telling. Walk the path again, and perhaps the sky will tilt just so, revealing a truth you missed before.

title: Maisara Caverns: Maisara Hungers
description:
I will not leave my people to die to de Vilebranch's dark rituals. We must go after dem.

The heart of Maisara Caverns is where de Vilebranch keep their secrets. I expect dis to be a bloody trip. We will have to face de ugliness of the Vilebranch greed, and what it is costin' all of us.

We must discover what purpose they have for my stolen kin. If any still be alive. I will be dere to aid you... but bring a fierce party of warriors. We will need it.
